content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: France Family Reunification & Family of Foreign/EU Nationals Visas
COUNTRY: France / Schengen Area / French Overseas Territories
SOURCE: https://france-visas.gouv.fr/en/web/france-visas/ | https://www.ofii.fr
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== VISA CATEGORIES OVERVIEW ===

#### FAMILY REUNIFICATION (FOREIGN NATIONAL SPONSOR)
ELIGIBILITY - SPONSOR MUST:
□ Be legally residing in France for at least 18 months with valid residence permit
□ Have stable, regular resources meeting minimum thresholds
□ Provide adequately-sized accommodation meeting French housing standards
□ Not pose threat to public order/security
□ Submit application to Direction Territoriale de l'OFII in place of residence

ELIGIBLE FAMILY MEMBERS:
• Spouse or civil union partner (married/union must pre-date sponsor's asylum application if applicable)
• Minor children (under 18) of sponsor or spouse
• Adult children (18-19) if dependency established before sponsor's asylum application
• Children entrusted by foreign court decision

FINANCIAL REQUIREMENTS:
• Reference: French minimum wage (SMIC) net ~€1,400/month for sponsor
• Additional amounts: +~€400/month for spouse, +~€300/month per child
• Proof: Employment contracts, payslips (3-6 months), tax notices, bank statements
• Resources must be "stable and regular" (not one-time payments)

ACCOMMODATION REQUIREMENTS:
• Minimum size: ~9 m² per person (single: 9 m², couple: 16 m², +9 m² per additional person)
• Must meet local housing standards (salubrité, safety, sanitation)
• Proof: Lease agreement, property deed, accommodation certificate + floor plan

APPLICATION PROCESS:
1. Sponsor submits family reunification application to OFII Direction Territoriale
2. OFII assesses resources, accommodation, eligibility
3. If approved: File forwarded to Consulate in applicant's country
4. Consulate contacts applicant for visa application appointment
5. If granted: Long-stay visa valid 3 months issued
6. Upon arrival in France: Apply for residence permit at local Prefecture

PROCESSING TIME: 6-18 months from application to visa issuance

==== REFUGEE/SUBSIDIARY PROTECTION FAMILY REUNIFICATION ====
ELIGIBILITY - SPONSOR MUST:
□ Have refugee status, subsidiary protection, or stateless person status in France
□ Be over 18 years old (or minor with parents applying)

ELIGIBLE FAMILY MEMBERS:
• Spouse/partner (civil union) aged 18+, if marriage/union predates asylum application
• Children under 19 if dependency established before asylum application
• Children under 18 of sponsor or spouse (direct line of descent)
• Children entrusted by foreign court decision

SPECIAL RULES FOR MINOR REFUGEES:
• Unmarried minor refugees: Parents may apply for family reunification
• Parents may include their own unmarried minor dependent children

PROCESSING: May be prioritized vs. standard family reunification

==== SPOUSE OF FRENCH NATIONAL ====
STAY ≤90 DAYS (SHORT-STAY VISA):
• Purpose: Visit spouse in France temporarily
• Documents: Marriage certificate, spouse's French nationality proof, return ticket
• Visa: Short-stay Schengen visa (Type C)

STAY >90 DAYS OR SETTLEMENT (LONG-STAY VISA):
• Documents:
  - Marriage certificate (translated/legalized if foreign)
  - Spouse's French nationality proof (passport, ID card, naturalization decree)
  - Proof of community of life: joint accounts, correspondence, photos, communication records
  - Proof of intention to live together in France: accommodation, job search, enrollment plans
• Visa: Long-stay visa equivalent to residence permit (VLS-TS)
• After arrival: Validate visa online within 3 months, then apply for "vie privée et familiale" residence permit at Prefecture

==== CHILD OF FRENCH NATIONAL ====
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Foreign child under 21 years old, OR
□ Dependent adult child (studying, job-seeking, or disabled), OR
□ Child with legally established relationship to French parent, OR
□ Child adopted (simple or full) by French national

DOCUMENTS REQUIRED:
• Birth certificate showing relationship to French parent (translated/legalized)
• French parent's nationality proof
• Child's age proof (passport, birth certificate)
• If minor with joint custody: Consent from other parent for settlement in France
• If over 21: Proof of dependency (financial support, enrollment, medical dependency)

VISA & RESIDENCE:
• Long-stay visa valid 3 months issued
• After arrival: Apply for residence permit at Prefecture of residence department

==== DEPENDENT OLDER RELATIVE OF FRENCH NATIONAL ====
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Parent or grandparent of French national OR French national's spouse
□ Must prove dependency in country of origin OR sufficient personal resources

STAY ≤90 DAYS (SHORT-STAY):
• Documents: Relationship proof, French nationality proof, sufficient means, accommodation, travel insurance
• Visa: Short-stay Schengen visa

STAY >90 DAYS WITH SUFFICIENT PERSONAL RESOURCES:
• Follow tourist/private stay visa procedure
• Must prove resources meet ~€615/month standard

SETTLEMENT WITH INSUFFICIENT PERSONAL RESOURCES:
• Must prove:
  - Relationship to French descendant
  - Dependency in country of origin (financial transfers, care needs)
  - Accommodation conditions in France
  - Medical insurance covering first 3 months
• Visa: Long-stay visa valid 3 months
• After arrival: Apply for "ascendant de Français" residence permit at Prefecture

==== FOREIGN PARENT OF MINOR FRENCH NATIONAL ====
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Parent of minor French citizen
□ Has contributed to child's maintenance/upbringing since birth OR for at least 2 years (per Article 371-2 Civil Code)
□ Does not live in state of polygamy

DOCUMENTS REQUIRED:
• Child's French nationality proof, age proof, residence in France proof
• Evidence of actual contribution: financial transfers, school involvement, medical care, correspondence
• Proof of non-polygamy status (if applicable)

VISA & RESIDENCE:
• Long-stay visa valid 3 months issued
• After arrival: Apply for "parent d'enfant français" residence permit at Prefecture

==== FAMILY OF EU/EEA/SWISS NATIONAL ====
ELIGIBLE FAMILY MEMBERS:
• Spouse or civil union partner
• Direct descendant under 21 or dependent at any age
• Direct dependent older relative (parent/grandparent)
• Direct dependent relative of spouse

IF EU NATIONAL IS A STUDENT:
• Only spouse and direct descendants eligible (not older relatives)

DOCUMENTS TO PROVE EU FREE MOVEMENT RIGHTS:
• EU national's passport/ID card proving nationality
• Proof of residence in France: employment contract, enrollment letter, self-employment registration, sufficient resources
• Proof of family relationship: marriage/birth certificate (translated if needed)
• For dependent relatives: Evidence of financial dependency in country of origin

VISA PROCESS:
• Check visa wizard for short-stay requirement based on nationality
• If visa required: Short-stay visa issued, then apply for residence permit after arrival
• If no visa required: Enter France, apply for residence permit within 3 months
• Residence permit type: "Carte de séjour - membre de famille de citoyen UE/EEE/Suisse"

ADVANTAGES UNDER EU LAW:
• Faster processing (typically 1-3 months)
• Lower financial thresholds ("sufficient resources" vs. fixed amounts)
• Immediate work rights upon arrival
• Simplified renewal process

=== COMMON DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS ===
CIVIL STATUS DOCUMENTS:
• Marriage certificates, birth certificates, adoption decrees must be:
  - Issued within last 3 months (for some documents)
  - Translated by certified translator if not in French
  - Legalized with Apostille (Hague Convention countries) OR consular legalization

FINANCIAL PROOF:
• Bank statements (last 3-6 months)
• Employment contracts, payslips, tax notices
• Sponsorship letters with sponsor's financial documents + proof of relationship

ACCOMMODATION PROOF:
• Lease agreement, property deed, or accommodation certificate
• Floor plan showing compliance with size standards (~9 m² per person)
• Utility bills in sponsor's name

HEALTH INSURANCE:
• Short-stay: Travel insurance €30,000 minimum coverage
• Long-stay: Registration with French social security or private insurance covering entire stay

=== FEES (2024) ===
• Family reunification long-stay visa: €99
• Spouse/child of French national long-stay visa: €99
• EU family member residence permit: ~€25-100 (varies by Prefecture)
• OFII integration contract: Free (language courses provided if needed)
• Payment: By credit card or cash as specified by VAC/Consulate

=== PROCESSING TIMES ===
• Family reunification (OFII): 6-18 months
• Refugee family reunification: May be prioritized (shorter timeline)
• Spouse/child of French national: 2-6 months
• EU family member: 1-3 months
• Peak seasons: Potential delays; apply early

=== VLS-TS VALIDATION & RESIDENCE PERMIT ===
LONG-STAY VISA HOLDERS MUST:
1. Validate visa online within 3 months of arrival: https://administration-etrangers-en-france.interieur.gouv.fr
2. Pay validation fee (~€200-250 standard; reduced for some categories)
3. After validation: Visa serves as residence permit for its validity period
4. Before expiry: Apply for multi-year residence permit at local Prefecture

REQUIRED FOR RESIDENCE PERMIT APPLICATION:
• Validated visa or current residence permit
• Proof of address in France (lease, utility bill)
• Proof of continued eligibility (ongoing relationship, resources, accommodation)
• OFII integration contract completion (if applicable)
• Recent passport photos

=== INTEGRATION REQUIREMENTS ===
OFII CONTRAT D'ACCUEIL ET D'INTÉGRATION (CAI):
• Most family visa holders must sign CAI after arrival
• Includes: French language assessment, civic training, values of French Republic
• Free language courses offered if level below A1
• Completion may be required for residence permit renewal
• Exemptions: Minors, elderly, medical incapacity

=== OVERSEAS TERRITORIES ===
SPECIAL RULES APPLY FOR:
• Guadeloupe, French Guiana, Réunion, Martinique, Mayotte (Overseas Departments)
• New Caledonia, French Polynesia, Saint-Barthélemy, Saint-Martin, St. Pierre and Miquelon, Wallis and Futuna (Overseas Collectivities)

APPLICATION NOTE:
• Specify intended territory on visa application
• Processing handled by Consulate responsible for place of residence
• Some territories have additional health/security requirements
• EU free movement rules may not fully apply to all territories

=== ENTRY TO FRANCE/SCHENGEN ===
• Final admissibility determined by border control officer at first Schengen entry point
• Must present: Passport with visa, family relationship proof, accommodation proof, return ticket (if short-stay)
• Officer may ask about purpose, accommodation, financial support
• Must comply with visa conditions; violations affect future applications

=== POST-VISA COMPLIANCE ===
• Must validate VLS-TS within 3 months of arrival (if long-stay visa)
• Must maintain family relationship and living arrangements
• Must not engage in polygamous relationships (only one spouse can benefit)
• Must apply for residence permit renewal before current permit expires
• Must notify authorities of address changes, relationship changes, or departure

=== REFUSAL & APPEAL ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Insufficient proof of financial resources or accommodation size
✗ Relationship not proven genuine or entered for immigration purposes
✗ Sponsor does not meet 18-month residency requirement (family reunification)
✗ Document authentication issues (missing translation, apostille)
✗ Polygamy situation (only one spouse eligible)
✗ Security/health concerns

APPEAL PROCESS:
• Written refusal notice provided with legal basis
• File appeal (recours) within 2 months to Commission de Recours contre les Décisions de Refus de Visa
• Submit additional evidence addressing specific refusal grounds
• No fee for appeal; decision typically 1-2 months
• Alternative: Reapply with stronger, targeted documentation

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: France Young Professional, Working Holiday & Long-Stay Visas
COUNTRY: France / Schengen Area / French Overseas Territories
SOURCE: https://france-visas.gouv.fr/en/web/france-visas/
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== VISA CATEGORIES OVERVIEW ===

#### YOUNG PROFESSIONAL EXCHANGE VISA
PURPOSE: Professional, linguistic, or cultural development through work placement in France
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Applicant's country has bilateral agreement with France (check "Special features" for your country)
□ Already employed or recently graduated in home country
□ Age limit per agreement: typically 18-30 or 18-35 years
□ Secure job offer/training placement with French company
□ Employer submits work permit application to OFII Direction Territoriale

APPLICATION PROCESS:
1. Employer files work permit application with OFII in France
2. Applicant prepares file:
   • Valid passport (valid 3+ months beyond intended stay)
   • Biometric photos (35x45 mm)
   • Employment contract or training agreement
   • Proof of qualifications (diplomas, CV)
   • Proof of financial means (~€2,500 minimum)
   • Health insurance covering entire stay
3. Submit complete file to OFII or its delegation in home country
4. OFII forwards to Consulate for visa examination
5. If granted: Long-stay VLS-TS visa with "jeune professionnel" mention
6. Validate visa online within 3 months of arrival in France

VISA DETAILS:
• Type: Long-stay visa equivalent to residence permit (VLS-TS)
• Validity: Matches employment/training duration (max 12 months typically)
• Work rights: Authorized to work only for sponsoring employer
• Travel: Can travel within Schengen Area under 90/180-day rule
• Renewal: Must apply for residence permit at Prefecture if extending stay

#### WORKING HOLIDAY VISA (WHV)
PURPOSE: Cultural exchange + tourism; temporary work allowed to supplement funds
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Age: 18-30 years (18-35 for Canada, New Zealand, Australia agreements)
□ Nationality: From country with bilateral WHV agreement with France
□ Not accompanied by dependents (spouse/children cannot accompany)
□ Primary purpose: Cultural exchange, NOT employment

ELIGIBLE COUNTRIES & ANNUAL QUOTAS:
• Canada: 6,500 visas/year
• Japan: 1,500 visas/year
• South Korea: 500 visas/year
• Argentina: 500 visas/year
• Tunisia, Chile, Colombia, others: Check France-Visas for current list

FINANCIAL REQUIREMENTS:
• Proof of sufficient funds: ~€2,500-3,000 (varies by agreement)
• Return ticket or proof of funds to purchase one
• Health insurance covering entire stay in France/Schengen Area (minimum €30,000 coverage)

APPLICATION TIMING:
• Applications typically open January-February for summer intake
• Quotas fill quickly; apply early in application window
• Processing time: 2-6 weeks depending on Consulate workload

VISA DETAILS:
• Validity: 12 months, non-renewable
• Work rights: Temporary employment allowed; no permanent contracts
• Study rights: Short courses permitted; full-time study requires separate student visa
• Travel: Can travel within Schengen Area under 90/180-day rule
• Validation: Must validate VLS-TS online within 3 months of arrival

==== LONG-STAY VISA (GENERAL) ====
WHEN REQUIRED:
• Any stay in France exceeding 90 days within 180-day period
• Applies regardless of nationality (even visa-exempt nationals need long-stay visa for >90 days)

VALIDITY & RENEWAL:
• Duration: 3 months to 1 year (as specified on visa sticker)
• To extend beyond validity: Apply for residence permit at local Prefecture in France
• During validity: Equivalent to Schengen visa for travel outside France (max 90 days in 180-day period across Schengen)

VLS-TS VALIDATION (MANDATORY FOR ALL LONG-STAY VISAS):
• Must validate online within 3 months of arrival in France
• Portal: https://administration-etrangers-en-france.interieur.gouv.fr
• Fee: ~€200-250 (paid during validation)
• Required documents: Passport, visa, proof of French address, health insurance
• Failure to validate = illegal stay + potential deportation + entry ban

=== FRENCH OVERSEAS TERRITORIES ===
TERRITORIES WITH SPECIAL VISA RULES:
• French Polynesia, New Caledonia, Wallis-et-Futuna, French Southern and Antarctic Lands (TAAF)
• Most nationalities require separate long-stay visa for stays >90 days in these territories
• Metropolitan France long-stay visa does NOT automatically grant access to overseas territories

EXEMPTIONS (VISA-FREE ACCESS):
• EU/EEA/Swiss citizens: No visa required for ANY French territory
• Monaco/Andorra citizens: No visa required for ANY French territory
• EEA/Swiss citizens: Visa-free for most territories EXCEPT French Polynesia, New Caledonia, Wallis-et-Futuna, TAAF

APPLICATION NOTE FOR OVERSEAS TERRITORIES:
• Apply at French Consulate responsible for your place of residence
• Specify intended territory of stay on application form
• Processing times may differ from metropolitan France applications
• Some territories have additional health/security requirements

=== COMMON DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS (ALL LONG-STAY VISAS) ===
PASSPORT:
• Valid at least 3 months BEYOND intended departure from France/Schengen
• Issued (not extended) within past 10 years
• Contains at least 2 blank visa pages
• Signed by holder

PHOTO:
• One recent biometric photo (35x45 mm, light background, neutral expression)

PROOF OF RESIDENCE:
• Valid residence permit if applying from country other than nationality
• Proof of address in Consulate's jurisdiction (utility bill, lease, ID)

HEALTH INSURANCE:
• Coverage: Minimum €30,000 for medical emergencies, repatriation, hospitalization
• Validity: Entire duration of stay in France/Schengen Area
• From recognized insurer; French policies accepted automatically

FINANCIAL PROOF:
• Bank statements (last 3 months) showing sufficient funds
• Guideline: ~€615/month minimum for long-stay visitor visa
• Higher amounts required for Working Holiday/Young Professional (~€2,500-3,000 total)

=== FEES (2024) ===
• Young Professional/Working Holiday long-stay visa: €99
• VLS-TS validation fee: ~€200-250 (paid after arrival in France)
• Payment: By credit card or cash as specified by VAC/Consulate
• Fees non-refundable even if visa refused

=== PROCESSING TIMES ===
• Standard: 15-30 calendar days from complete application
• Working Holiday/Young Professional: May take longer due to OFII coordination
• Peak seasons (summer): Potential delays; apply early
• Overseas Territories applications: May have separate processing timelines

=== ENTRY TO FRANCE/SCHENGEN ===
• Final admissibility determined by border control officer at first Schengen entry point
• Must present: Passport with visa, return ticket (if applicable), proof of funds/insurance/accommodation if requested
• Officer may ask about purpose, itinerary, accommodation, funds
• Must comply with visa conditions; violations affect future applications

=== POST-VISA COMPLIANCE ===
YOUNG PROFESSIONAL/WORKING HOLIDAY:
• Must work only for authorized employer (Young Professional) or temporary jobs only (WHV)
• Must maintain health insurance throughout stay
• Must validate VLS-TS within 3 months of arrival
• Must not overstay visa validity; apply for residence permit at Prefecture if extending

LONG-STAY GENERAL:
• Must validate VLS-TS within 3 months of arrival
• Must register with French authorities if staying >1 year
• Must maintain health insurance and sufficient resources throughout stay
• Must not engage in unauthorized professional activity

=== REFUSAL & APPEAL ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Insufficient proof of financial means or health insurance
✗ Age or nationality not eligible for Young Professional/Working Holiday
✗ Quota already filled for Working Holiday visa
✗ Incomplete OFII coordination for Young Professional visa
✗ Passport validity issues
✗ Previous Schengen/French visa violations
✗ Security/health concerns

APPEAL PROCESS:
• Written refusal notice provided with legal basis
• File appeal (recours) within 2 months of refusal to Commission de Recours contre les Décisions de Refus de Visa
• Submit additional evidence addressing specific refusal grounds
• No fee for appeal; decision typically 1-2 months
• Alternative: Reapply with stronger, targeted documentation

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: France Short-Stay & Long-Stay Visitor Visas (Tourism/Private Stay/Medical)
COUNTRY: France / Schengen Area
SOURCE: https://france-visas.gouv.fr/en/web/france-visas/
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• Tourism/private stay: No professional activity permitted (except Working Holiday eligible nationals)
• Short-stay (≤90 days in 180-day period): Uniform Schengen Visa (Type C) or French national visa
• Long-stay (>90 days): Long-stay visitor visa (VLS-TS) serving as residence permit
• Medical care visa: Short-stay visa for treatment; does not cover costs via French health system
• Airport transit: Check if Airport Transit Visa (ATV) required based on nationality

=== ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA ===

#### SHORT-STAY VISA (≤90 DAYS)
APPLICANT MUST:
□ Have nationality requiring visa for France/Schengen (check France-Visas wizard)
□ Intend stay ≤90 days within any 180-day period across Schengen Area
□ Not engage in professional activity during stay
□ Prove sufficient financial resources for duration of stay + return travel
□ Hold valid travel insurance (€30,000 minimum coverage)
□ Have confirmed accommodation for entire stay
□ Possess passport valid 3+ months beyond intended departure, issued <10 years ago
□ Provide proof of intent to return to country of residence

FINANCIAL MEANS (GUIDELINE):
• €120/day if no pre-paid accommodation
• €65/day if hotel/pre-paid accommodation confirmed
• €32.50/day for minors traveling with family
• Acceptable proof: Bank statements (3 months), cash, traveler's checks, international credit cards

ACCOMMODATION PROOF:
• Confirmed hotel reservations (name, address, dates, confirmation number)
• Invitation letter from host in France + copy of host's ID/residence permit
• Rental agreement or property ownership documents

TRAVEL INSURANCE:
• Minimum €30,000 coverage for medical emergencies, repatriation, hospitalization
• Valid for entire Schengen Area + entire duration of stay
• From recognized insurer; French policies accepted automatically

#### LONG-STAY VISITOR VISA (>90 DAYS)
APPLICANT MUST:
□ Intend private stay >90 days without professional activity in France
□ Prove stable, regular, sufficient resources WITHOUT working in France
• Guideline: At least French minimum wage (SMIC) level ~€1,400/month net
• Sources: Pension, rental income, investments, savings, sponsor support
□ Provide proof of accommodation in France (lease, property deed, host certificate)
□ Hold comprehensive health insurance valid in France for entire stay
□ Sign formal declaration not to engage in professional activity
□ Complete online validation of VLS-TS within 3 months of arrival in France

VLS-TS VALIDATION PROCESS:
• Portal: https://administration-etrangers-en-france.interieur.gouv.fr
• Fee: ~€200-250 (paid during validation)
• Required documents: Passport, visa, proof of address in France, insurance
• Validation grants residence rights; failure to validate = illegal stay

#### MEDICAL CARE VISA (SHORT-STAY)
ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:
□ Medical certificate from home country doctor explaining necessity of treatment in France
□ Official appointment/acceptance letter from French hospital/clinic
□ Detailed cost estimate for proposed treatment
□ Proof of payment arrangement or financial guarantee
□ Travel insurance covering medical repatriation

IMPORTANT: French public health system does NOT cover visa holders; all medical costs are patient responsibility.

=== APPLICATION PROCESS ===
1. CHECK REQUIREMENT: Use France-Visas wizard https://france-visas.gouv.fr
2. COMPLETE ONLINE APPLICATION: Via France-Visas portal
3. BOOK APPOINTMENT: At Visa Application Centre (VAC) or French Consulate
4. ATTEND APPOINTMENT:
   • Submit documents in required order
   • Provide biometrics (fingerprints + photo) if aged 12+
   • Pay visa fee
5. AWAIT DECISION: Typically 15 calendar days (up to 45 for complex cases)
6. COLLECT PASSPORT: With visa sticker if granted

=== FEES (2024) ===
• Short-stay Schengen visa: €80 adults, €40 children 6-12, free under 6
• Long-stay visitor visa: €99 (plus ~€200-250 validation fee after arrival)
• Payment: By credit card or cash as specified by VAC/Consulate
• Fees non-refundable even if visa refused

=== PROCESSING TIMES ===
• Standard: 15 calendar days from complete application
• Complex cases / additional scrutiny: Up to 45 calendar days
• Peak seasons (summer, holidays): Potential delays
• Medical visas: May be expedited with urgent medical documentation

=== PASSPORT & DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS ===
PASSPORT:
• Valid at least 3 months BEYOND intended departure from Schengen Area
• Issued (not extended) within past 10 years
• Contains at least 2 blank visa pages
• Signed by holder

PHOTO:
• One recent biometric photo (35x45 mm, light background, neutral expression)

PROOF OF RESIDENCE:
• Valid residence permit if applying from country other than nationality
• Proof of address in Consulate's jurisdiction (utility bill, lease, ID)

RETURN TRAVEL PROOF:
• Confirmed roundtrip flight/travel reservations with passenger name
• Do NOT purchase non-refundable tickets before visa grant

=== SCHENGEN AREA RULES ===
90/180-DAY RULE:
• Maximum stay: 90 days within ANY rolling 180-day period across ALL Schengen countries
• Days counted from entry stamp to exit stamp (include partial days)
• Use Schengen calculator: https://ec.europa.eu/assets/home/visa-calculator/

MAIN DESTINATION PRINCIPLE:
• Apply at French Consulate if France is main destination (most days)
• If equal days in multiple Schengen countries: Apply at Consulate of FIRST entry point

=== ENTRY TO FRANCE/SCHENGEN ===
• Final admissibility determined by border control officer at first Schengen entry point
• Must present: Passport with visa, return ticket, proof of funds/insurance/accommodation if requested
• Officer may ask about purpose, itinerary, accommodation, funds
• Must comply with visa conditions; violations affect future applications

=== POST-VISA COMPLIANCE ===
SHORT-STAY:
• Must not exceed 90 days in any 180-day period across Schengen Area
• Must not work or study long-term without appropriate national visa
• Must maintain travel insurance throughout stay

LONG-STAY (VLS-TS):
• Must validate visa online within 3 months of arrival
• Must register with French authorities if staying >1 year
• Must maintain health insurance and sufficient resources throughout stay
• Must not engage in professional activity unless visa explicitly permits

=== REFUSAL & APPEAL ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Insufficient proof of financial means
✗ Unclear or implausible travel purpose/itinerary
✗ Inadequate proof of intent to return to country of residence
✗ Invalid/incomplete travel insurance or accommodation proof
✗ Passport validity issues
✗ Previous Schengen visa violations
✗ Security/health concerns

APPEAL PROCESS:
• Written refusal notice provided with legal basis
• File appeal (recours) within 2 months of refusal to Commission de Recours contre les Décisions de Refus de Visa
• Submit additional evidence addressing specific refusal grounds
• No fee for appeal; decision typically 1-2 months
• Alternative: Reapply with stronger, targeted documentation

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: France Young Professional, Working Holiday & Long-Stay Visas
COUNTRY: France / Schengen Area / French Overseas Territories
SOURCE: https://france-visas.gouv.fr/en/web/france-visas/
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== VISA CATEGORIES OVERVIEW ===

#### YOUNG PROFESSIONAL EXCHANGE VISA
PURPOSE: Professional, linguistic, or cultural development through work placement in France
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Applicant's country has bilateral agreement with France (check "Special features" for your country)
□ Already employed or recently graduated in home country
□ Age limit per agreement: typically 18-30 or 18-35 years
□ Secure job offer/training placement with French company
□ Employer submits work permit application to OFII Direction Territoriale

APPLICATION PROCESS:
1. Employer files work permit application with OFII in France
2. Applicant prepares file:
   • Valid passport (valid 3+ months beyond intended stay)
   • Biometric photos (35x45 mm)
   • Employment contract or training agreement
   • Proof of qualifications (diplomas, CV)
   • Proof of financial means (~€2,500 minimum)
   • Health insurance covering entire stay
3. Submit complete file to OFII or its delegation in home country
4. OFII forwards to Consulate for visa examination
5. If granted: Long-stay VLS-TS visa with "jeune professionnel" mention
6. Validate visa online within 3 months of arrival in France

VISA DETAILS:
• Type: Long-stay visa equivalent to residence permit (VLS-TS)
• Validity: Matches employment/training duration (max 12 months typically)
• Work rights: Authorized to work only for sponsoring employer
• Travel: Can travel within Schengen Area under 90/180-day rule
• Renewal: Must apply for residence permit at Prefecture if extending stay

#### WORKING HOLIDAY VISA (WHV)
PURPOSE: Cultural exchange + tourism; temporary work allowed to supplement funds
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Age: 18-30 years (18-35 for Canada, New Zealand, Australia agreements)
□ Nationality: From country with bilateral WHV agreement with France
□ Not accompanied by dependents (spouse/children cannot accompany)
□ Primary purpose: Cultural exchange, NOT employment

ELIGIBLE COUNTRIES & ANNUAL QUOTAS:
• Canada: 6,500 visas/year
• Japan: 1,500 visas/year
• South Korea: 500 visas/year
• Argentina: 500 visas/year
• Tunisia, Chile, Colombia, others: Check France-Visas for current list

FINANCIAL REQUIREMENTS:
• Proof of sufficient funds: ~€2,500-3,000 (varies by agreement)
• Return ticket or proof of funds to purchase one
• Health insurance covering entire stay in France/Schengen Area (minimum €30,000 coverage)

APPLICATION TIMING:
• Applications typically open January-February for summer intake
• Quotas fill quickly; apply early in application window
• Processing time: 2-6 weeks depending on Consulate workload

VISA DETAILS:
• Validity: 12 months, non-renewable
• Work rights: Temporary employment allowed; no permanent contracts
• Study rights: Short courses permitted; full-time study requires separate student visa
• Travel: Can travel within Schengen Area under 90/180-day rule
• Validation: Must validate VLS-TS online within 3 months of arrival

==== LONG-STAY VISA (GENERAL) ====
WHEN REQUIRED:
• Any stay in France exceeding 90 days within 180-day period
• Applies regardless of nationality (even visa-exempt nationals need long-stay visa for >90 days)

VALIDITY & RENEWAL:
• Duration: 3 months to 1 year (as specified on visa sticker)
• To extend beyond validity: Apply for residence permit at local Prefecture in France
• During validity: Equivalent to Schengen visa for travel outside France (max 90 days in 180-day period across Schengen)

VLS-TS VALIDATION (MANDATORY FOR ALL LONG-STAY VISAS):
• Must validate online within 3 months of arrival in France
• Portal: https://administration-etrangers-en-france.interieur.gouv.fr
• Fee: ~€200-250 (paid during validation)
• Required documents: Passport, visa, proof of French address, health insurance
• Failure to validate = illegal stay + potential deportation + entry ban

=== FRENCH OVERSEAS TERRITORIES ===
TERRITORIES WITH SPECIAL VISA RULES:
• French Polynesia, New Caledonia, Wallis-et-Futuna, French Southern and Antarctic Lands (TAAF)
• Most nationalities require separate long-stay visa for stays >90 days in these territories
• Metropolitan France long-stay visa does NOT automatically grant access to overseas territories

EXEMPTIONS (VISA-FREE ACCESS):
• EU/EEA/Swiss citizens: No visa required for ANY French territory
• Monaco/Andorra citizens: No visa required for ANY French territory
• EEA/Swiss citizens: Visa-free for most territories EXCEPT French Polynesia, New Caledonia, Wallis-et-Futuna, TAAF

APPLICATION NOTE FOR OVERSEAS TERRITORIES:
• Apply at French Consulate responsible for your place of residence
• Specify intended territory of stay on application form
• Processing times may differ from metropolitan France applications
• Some territories have additional health/security requirements

=== COMMON DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS (ALL LONG-STAY VISAS) ===
PASSPORT:
• Valid at least 3 months BEYOND intended departure from France/Schengen
• Issued (not extended) within past 10 years
• Contains at least 2 blank visa pages
• Signed by holder

PHOTO:
• One recent biometric photo (35x45 mm, light background, neutral expression)

PROOF OF RESIDENCE:
• Valid residence permit if applying from country other than nationality
• Proof of address in Consulate's jurisdiction (utility bill, lease, ID)

HEALTH INSURANCE:
• Coverage: Minimum €30,000 for medical emergencies, repatriation, hospitalization
• Validity: Entire duration of stay in France/Schengen Area
• From recognized insurer; French policies accepted automatically

FINANCIAL PROOF:
• Bank statements (last 3 months) showing sufficient funds
• Guideline: ~€615/month minimum for long-stay visitor visa
• Higher amounts required for Working Holiday/Young Professional (~€2,500-3,000 total)

=== FEES (2024) ===
• Young Professional/Working Holiday long-stay visa: €99
• VLS-TS validation fee: ~€200-250 (paid after arrival in France)
• Payment: By credit card or cash as specified by VAC/Consulate
• Fees non-refundable even if visa refused

=== PROCESSING TIMES ===
• Standard: 15-30 calendar days from complete application
• Working Holiday/Young Professional: May take longer due to OFII coordination
• Peak seasons (summer): Potential delays; apply early
• Overseas Territories applications: May have separate processing timelines

=== ENTRY TO FRANCE/SCHENGEN ===
• Final admissibility determined by border control officer at first Schengen entry point
• Must present: Passport with visa, return ticket (if applicable), proof of funds/insurance/accommodation if requested
• Officer may ask about purpose, itinerary, accommodation, funds
• Must comply with visa conditions; violations affect future applications

=== POST-VISA COMPLIANCE ===
YOUNG PROFESSIONAL/WORKING HOLIDAY:
• Must work only for authorized employer (Young Professional) or temporary jobs only (WHV)
• Must maintain health insurance throughout stay
• Must validate VLS-TS within 3 months of arrival
• Must not overstay visa validity; apply for residence permit at Prefecture if extending

LONG-STAY GENERAL:
• Must validate VLS-TS within 3 months of arrival
• Must register with French authorities if staying >1 year
• Must maintain health insurance and sufficient resources throughout stay
• Must not engage in unauthorized professional activity

=== REFUSAL & APPEAL ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Insufficient proof of financial means or health insurance
✗ Age or nationality not eligible for Young Professional/Working Holiday
✗ Quota already filled for Working Holiday visa
✗ Incomplete OFII coordination for Young Professional visa
✗ Passport validity issues
✗ Previous Schengen/French visa violations
✗ Security/health concerns

APPEAL PROCESS:
• Written refusal notice provided with legal basis
• File appeal (recours) within 2 months of refusal to Commission de Recours contre les Décisions de Refus de Visa
• Submit additional evidence addressing specific refusal grounds
• No fee for appeal; decision typically 1-2 months
• Alternative: Reapply with stronger, targeted documentation

content.fees: PAYMENT   Visa fee: 80 for adults, 40 for children 6-12, free for children under 6  Payable in USD only converted at current exchange rate  Payment method: As specified by German MissionVAC cash, card, money order  Fee non-refundable even if visa refused
content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: Schengen Visa (Short-Stay, Type C) - German Mission (USA Applicants)
COUNTRY: Germany / Schengen Area
SOURCE: https://www.germany.info/us-en/service/visa
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• Short-stay visa for tourism, business, visiting family/friends in Schengen Area
• Max stay: 90 days within any 180-day period (Schengen rule)
• Apply at German Mission if Germany is MAIN DESTINATION (most days) or FIRST ENTRY (if equal days)
• Submit application up to 6 months prior to planned trip
• Processing: Typically 15 calendar days (up to 45 for complex cases)

=== CORE DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS (ALL PURPOSES) ===
APPLICATION FORM:
□ Fully completed, signed application form (all pages + barcode appendix)
□ Signed declaration of accuracy

PHOTO:
□ One (1) recent biometric passport photo (35x45 mm, light background, neutral expression)

PASSPORT:
□ Valid for at least 3 months BEYOND intended departure from Schengen Area
□ Contains at least 2 blank visa pages
□ Issued (not extended) within past 10 years
□ Signed by holder

US RESIDENCY PROOF:
□ US Alien Registration Card (Green Card) OR
□ Valid US visa: Type A, E, F+I-20, G, H, I, J+J1, L, O, R

PROOF OF JURISDICTION:
□ Valid driver's license / State ID / utility bill / lease agreement showing address within German Mission's jurisdiction

TRAVEL RESERVATIONS:
□ Confirmed roundtrip flight/travel reservations with passenger name
□ Do NOT purchase non-refundable tickets before visa grant

TRAVEL HEALTH INSURANCE:
□ Coverage: Minimum €30,000 for medical emergencies, repatriation, hospital treatment, death
□ Validity: Entire Schengen Area + entire duration of stay/transit
□ Policy must show applicant's name and DOB
□ NOT accepted: Employment insurance cards, domestic US policies without Schengen coverage

PROOF OF FAMILY/SOCIAL TIES IN US (IF APPLICABLE):
□ Marriage certificate (if spouse resides in US)
□ Children's birth certificates (if minor children in US)
□ Proof of relation to other dependents + their US residency proof

=== FINANCIAL MEANS REQUIREMENT ===
PROOF OF ADEQUATE FUNDS:
• US personal checking account statements (last 3 months) showing applicant's name/address
• Guideline amount: ~€45-50 per day of stay (higher for luxury travel/longer stays)
• If financially supported by spouse/family member:
  - Signed sponsor letter
  - Sponsor's employment letter
  - Sponsor's 3 months bank statements
  - Proof of relationship + sponsor's passport copy

❌ NOT ACCEPTED AS PROOF:
• Foreign bank statements
• Fixed-term deposits or investment depots
• Travel Money Card statements
• Credit card limits or available credit
• Cash declarations

=== VISA PURPOSE-SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS ===

#### BUSINESS VISA (Consulting, Training, Meetings, Audit)
ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS:
□ Employer Letter (US Company - on letterhead, signed):
  - Duration of employment
  - Salary (monthly/annual)
  - Nature of business relations with German company
  - Travel purpose and duration
  - Guarantee to cover travel expenses (if applicable)
  - + Applicant's 3 months bank statements

□ Invitation Letter (German Business Partner - on letterhead, signed):
  - Nature of business relations with US company
  - Travel purpose and duration of trip(s)
  - Guarantee to cover expenses (if applicable)
  - + Inviter's 3 months bank statements (if covering costs)
  - For multiple trips: Explicitly state frequency/duration

□ If Student: Recent enrollment confirmation from school/university
□ If Self-Employed: Business license + last year's tax return

□ Hotel Reservations: Confirmed booking with hotel name, address, phone, confirmation number, guest name

#### TOURIST VISA
ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS:
□ Employer Letter (US Company - on letterhead, signed):
  - Duration of employment, salary, confirmation of approved leave, travel purpose

□ If Student: Recent enrollment confirmation
□ If Self-Employed: Business license + last year's tax return

□ Detailed Travel Itinerary:
  - Day-by-day plan of cities/attractions
  - Confirmed hotel reservations (name, address, phone, confirmation number, guest name)
  - Confirmed roundtrip flight reservations

□ Proof of Adequate Financial Means (as per Core Requirements above)

#### VISITOR VISA (Visiting Family/Friends in Germany)
ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS:
□ Employer Letter (same as Tourist Visa)

□ Signed Invitation Letter from Host in Germany:
  - Host's personal data, address, phone number
  - Relationship to applicant
  - Duration and purpose of stay
  - + Copy of host's German ID card (front/back) OR foreign passport + German residence permit

□ Proof of Relationship (if applicable):
  - Birth certificate, marriage certificate, family register

□ Proof of Adequate Financial Means:
  - Applicant's 3 months US bank statements, OR
  - Formal Obligation Letter ("Verpflichtungserklärung") from host issued by German authority, OR
  - Sponsor documentation (as per Core Requirements)

=== MINOR APPLICANTS (UNDER 18) ===
ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:
□ Application signed by BOTH parents (unless sole custody documented)
□ Proof of sole custody (if applicable): Birth certificate, adoption papers, court order
□ Passports of both parents (or custodial parent)
□ Birth certificate of minor applicant
□ If one parent cannot attend interview: Notarized travel consent letter + passport copies

=== BIOMETRICS & APPOINTMENT ===
• Fingerprints + digital photo required for applicants aged 12+
• Valid for 59 months; reuse if reapplying within validity
• Collected at Visa Application Centre (VAC) or German Mission appointment
• Appointment required; book via official scheduling system

=== FEES & PAYMENT ===
• Visa fee: €80 for adults, €40 for children 6-12, free for children under 6
• Payable in USD only (converted at current exchange rate)
• Payment method: As specified by German Mission/VAC (cash, card, money order)
• Fee non-refundable even if visa refused

=== PROCESSING & DECISION ===
• Standard processing: 15 calendar days from complete application
• Complex cases / additional scrutiny: Up to 45 calendar days
• Peak seasons (summer, holidays): Potential delays
• Decision communicated via email/SMS; passport returned by mail or pickup
• No statutory right to expedited processing

=== VISA GRANT DETAILS ===
• Validity period determined by consular officer (may be shorter than requested)
• Entry type: Single or multiple entries (based on travel history/need)
• Duration of stay: Max 90 days within any 180-day period (Schengen rule)
• Visa sticker includes: Validity dates, number of entries, duration of stay, annotations

=== SCHENGEN AREA RULES ===
90/180-DAY RULE:
• Maximum stay: 90 days within ANY rolling 180-day period across ALL Schengen countries
• Days counted from entry stamp to exit stamp (include partial days)
• Use Schengen calculator: https://ec.europa.eu/assets/home/visa-calculator/

MAIN DESTINATION PRINCIPLE:
• Apply at Consulate of country where you will spend the MOST days
• If equal days in multiple countries: Apply at Consulate of FIRST entry point
• Changing itinerary after visa grant may affect entry/admissibility at border

=== ENTRY TO SCHENGEN AREA ===
• Final admissibility determined by border control officer at first Schengen entry point
• Must present: Passport with visa, return ticket, proof of funds/insurance if requested
• Officer may ask about purpose, itinerary, accommodation, funds
• Must comply with visa conditions; violations affect future applications

=== REFUSAL & APPEAL ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Insufficient proof of financial means
✗ Unclear or implausible travel purpose/itinerary
✗ Inadequate proof of intent to return to US
✗ Invalid/incomplete travel insurance
✗ Passport validity issues
✗ Previous Schengen visa violations
✗ Security/health concerns

REMONSTRANCE (APPEAL) PROCESS:
• Written refusal notice provided with legal basis
• File written Remonstrance within 1 month of refusal
• Submit additional evidence addressing specific refusal grounds
• No fee for Remonstrance; decision typically 4-8 weeks
• Alternative: Reapply with stronger, targeted documentation

=== POST-VISA COMPLIANCE ===
• Must enter Schengen Area within visa validity dates
• Must not exceed granted duration of stay or 90/180-day limit
• Must not work or study long-term without appropriate national visa
• Must maintain travel insurance throughout stay
• Overstaying results in entry bans (1-5 years) for future Schengen applications

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: India Entry (X) Visa for Family Members/Dependents
COUNTRY: India
SOURCE: https://www.boi.gov.in | https://www.mea.gov.in | https://frro.gov.in
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• Entry (X) Visa for dependents of foreign nationals legally residing in India on long-term visas (Employment, Student, Research, Project, etc.)
• For: Spouse, unmarried children under 25, dependent parents (in exceptional circumstances)
• Validity: Matches primary visa holder's validity or up to 5 years, whichever is less; extendable in India
• NOT for independent travel/tourism; dependent status tied to primary visa holder

=== ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA ===
PRIMARY VISA HOLDER MUST:
□ Hold valid long-term Indian visa: Employment, Student, Research, Project, Conference, Medical Attendant, or other eligible category
□ Be registered with FRRO/FRO if stay >180 days
□ Demonstrate sufficient financial means to support dependents

DEPENDENT ELIGIBILITY:
□ Spouse: Legally married to primary visa holder (marriage certificate required)
□ Children: Unmarried, under 25 years of age, financially dependent on primary holder
□ Parents: Only in exceptional circumstances (e.g., primary holder is minor, or parent is medically dependent); requires special approval

NON-ELIGIBLE RELATIONSHIPS:
✗ Siblings, adult married children, unrelated companions cannot be sponsored on Entry (X) Visa
✗ Dependents cannot engage in independent employment without separate Employment Visa

=== DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS ===
MANDATORY DOCUMENTS:
□ Completed online application form (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in)
□ Original passport (valid 6+ months beyond intended stay, 2 blank pages)
□ Two recent passport-sized photographs (2x2 inches, white background)
□ Copy of primary visa holder's passport, visa, and FRRO Registration Certificate (if applicable)
□ Proof of relationship:
  - Spouse: Attested marriage certificate (translated to English if not in English)
  - Children: Attested birth certificate showing parentage
  - Parents: Birth certificate of primary holder + proof of dependency (medical reports, financial support evidence)
□ Letter from primary visa holder:
  - Confirming relationship and intent to sponsor dependent
  - Details of accommodation and financial support arrangements
  - Contact information in India
□ Proof of primary holder's legal status in India:
  - Employment: Employment contract, employer letter, salary proof
  - Student: Admission letter, institution letter, financial proof
  - Other: Relevant authorization documents

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (MAY BE REQUESTED):
□ Medical fitness certificate for dependent (especially for long-term stays)
□ Police clearance certificate from dependent's country of residence (for certain nationalities)
□ For minor dependents (<18): Parental consent letter if traveling with one parent
□ Proof of accommodation in India: Rental agreement, institution hostel letter, employer accommodation letter

=== APPLICATION PROCESS ===
STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Primary visa holder ensures their own visa/FRRO registration is valid and up-to-date
2. Dependent completes online application at https://indianvisaonline.gov.in
3. Print, sign, and gather all required documents
4. Submit application + documents at:
   • Indian Embassy/Consulate in dependent's country of residence, OR
   • Authorized VFS Global center (appointment required in many locations)
5. Pay visa fee as directed (varies by nationality and validity)
6. Processing time: 5-15 working days (may be longer if relationship verification required)
7. Upon approval: Collect passport with Entry (X) Visa sticker
8. Enter India; register with FRRO/FRO within 14 days if stay >180 days

PROCESSING TIMES:
• Standard cases: 5-10 working days at most Missions
• Cases requiring relationship verification or additional scrutiny: 10-20 working days
• Peak seasons: Apply 2-3 weeks in advance

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE) ===
ENTRY (X) VISA FEES (USD, VARIES BY NATIONALITY/VALIDITY):
• Up to 1 year: ~USD 80-120
• 1-3 years: ~USD 120-200
• 3-5 years: ~USD 200-300+
• Additional service fees if applying via VFS Global: ~USD 20-40

NOTE: Fees subject to change; check official portal for current rates by nationality.

=== VISA VALIDITY & EXTENSION ===
INITIAL VALIDITY:
• Typically granted for duration matching primary visa holder's validity OR up to 5 years, whichever is less
• Example: Primary holder has 3-year Employment Visa → Dependent Entry Visa valid for 3 years
• Multiple entry allowed; each stay typically up to 180 days

EXTENSION IN INDIA:
• Apply via FRRO/FRO portal (https://frro.gov.in) at least 2 months before expiry
• Required documents:
  - Valid passport and current Entry (X) Visa
  - Primary holder's updated visa/FRRO registration proof
  - Continued proof of relationship and financial support
  - FRRO registration certificate (if previously registered)
• Processing: 2-4 weeks; applicant may stay in India during processing with acknowledgment receipt
• Extension granted for remaining validity of primary holder's visa

DEPENDENCY TERMINATION:
• If primary visa holder's status ends (visa expiry, cancellation, departure):
  - Dependent's Entry (X) Visa becomes invalid simultaneously
  - Dependent has 30-day grace period to:
    • Secure independent visa status (e.g., Employment, Student), OR
    • Depart India
• Failure to regularize status may result in overstay penalties

=== FRRO/FRO REGISTRATION REQUIREMENT ===
MANDATORY REGISTRATION:
• Foreigners on Entry (X) Visa staying >180 days continuously MUST register with nearest FRRO/FRO within 14 days of arrival
• Registration is also required if total stay in India exceeds 180 days in a calendar year

REGISTRATION PROCESS:
1. Visit FRRO/FRO portal: https://frro.gov.in
2. Create account and fill registration form
3. Upload required documents:
   - Passport copy (biodata page + visa page)
   - Primary holder's visa/FRRO proof, relationship documents
   - Proof of Indian address (same as primary holder or separate accommodation)
   - Recent photograph
4. Pay registration fee: ~INR 100-500 (varies by duration)
5. Attend in-person appointment at FRRO/FRO office (if required)
6. Receive Registration Certificate (RC) – must carry at all times

CONSEQUENCES OF NON-REGISTRATION:
• Fine: INR 1,000-10,000 depending on delay duration
• Difficulty extending visa, obtaining NOC for travel, or accessing other immigration services
• Potential impact on future Indian visa applications

=== RIGHTS & RESTRICTIONS FOR DEPENDENTS ===
STUDY RIGHTS:
• Dependents on Entry (X) Visa CAN enroll in Indian educational institutions:
  - Schools: No additional visa required for minor children
  - Colleges/Universities: May continue on Entry (X) Visa for full-time study; no need to change to Student Visa if course duration < visa validity
  - If course duration exceeds Entry Visa validity: Must apply for Student Visa extension or change of status

WORK RIGHTS:
• Dependents on Entry (X) Visa CANNOT engage in employment without separate Employment Visa
• Spouse may apply for Employment Visa independently if eligible (meeting salary/skills criteria)
• Unpaid volunteer work, internships for academic credit may be permitted with FRRO/FRO approval

TRAVEL RIGHTS:
• Multiple entry allowed; dependent can travel in/out of India independently of primary holder
• Must carry passport with Entry (X) Visa and FRRO Registration Certificate (if registered) when traveling
• If primary holder departs India permanently: Dependent must regularize status or depart within grace period

=== SPECIAL CASES ===
MINOR DEPENDENTS (<18):
• Must travel with or be met by parent/guardian holding valid Indian visa
• If traveling with one parent: Notarized consent letter from other parent recommended
• School enrollment: Provide admission letter; no separate Student Visa required if Entry (X) Visa covers course duration

DEPENDENT PARENTS (EXCEPTIONAL CASES):
• Eligibility requires: Primary holder is minor, OR parent is medically/financially dependent with no other support in home country
• Additional documentation: Medical reports, proof of dependency, NOC from other family members in home country
• Approval granted case-by-case by Indian Mission/Ministry of Home Affairs

DIVORCE/SEPARATION DURING STAY:
• If marriage ends while dependent is in India on Entry (X) Visa:
  - Visa status may be reviewed by FRRO/FRO
  - Dependent may apply for change of status (e.g., to Student/Employment Visa) if eligible
  - If no independent status secured: Must depart India within 30 days of status change notification

=== MEDICAL & INSURANCE REQUIREMENTS ===
MEDICAL EXAMINATION:
• May be required for long-term Entry (X) Visa applications (>1 year)
• Tests: General physical exam, chest X-ray (TB screening), blood tests as per FRRO requirements
• Conduct at recognized medical facility; report valid for 3 months

HEALTH INSURANCE:
• Strongly recommended for all dependents
• Some primary visa categories (e.g., Employment) require proof of family health insurance
• Coverage should include: Hospitalization, outpatient care, emergency evacuation, repatriation
• Options: Indian insurance providers or international plans with India coverage

VACCINATION REQUIREMENTS:
• Yellow fever vaccination required if arriving from countries with risk of transmission
• Other vaccinations recommended based on region of residence in India

=== COMPLIANCE & REPORTING OBLIGATIONS ===
ADDRESS CHANGES:
• Dependents must report change of Indian address to FRRO/FRO within 14 days
• Update via FRRO portal or in-person visit with proof of new address

PRIMARY HOLDER STATUS CHANGES:
• If primary visa holder changes visa type, employer, institution, or location:
  - Dependent must update FRRO registration with new details
  - If primary holder's visa is cancelled/terminated: Dependent must regularize status within grace period

ANNUAL REPORTING (IF REQUIRED):
• Some FRRO offices require annual check-in for long-term dependents
• Submit updated documents: Passport validity, continued relationship proof, financial support evidence

=== EXIT & STATUS CHANGE OPTIONS ===
COMPLETION OF PRIMARY HOLDER'S STAY:
• When primary visa holder departs India permanently:
  - Dependent's Entry (X) Visa becomes invalid
  - 30-day grace period to secure independent status or depart

TRANSITION TO INDEPENDENT VISA:
• If dependent secures independent eligibility (employment, admission to study):
  - Apply for change of status via FRRO/FRO (if in India) or Indian Mission (if abroad)
  - Cannot commence employment/study until new visa is granted

DEPARTURE PROCEDURES:
• No formal exit visa required for Entry (X) Visa holders
• Ensure FRRO registration is up-to-date before departure
• Carry passport with visa and Registration Certificate when exiting

=== REFUSAL & REAPPLICATION ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Inability to prove genuine relationship (insufficient/inauthentic documentation)
✗ Primary holder's visa status invalid, nearing expiry, or under scrutiny
✗ Insufficient proof of financial support for dependent
✗ Previous Indian visa violations by primary holder or dependent
✗ Security concerns related to applicant or primary holder

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• Address specific refusal reason: Strengthen relationship documentation, update primary holder's status proof, enhance financial evidence
• For relationship verification issues: Provide additional evidence (photos, communication records, joint financial documents)
• Wait at least 30 days before reapplying unless circumstances changed significantly

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR ENTRY (X) VISA APPLICATIONS:
• Indian Embassy/Consulate: https://www.mea.gov.in/embassies.htm
• VFS Global (if applicable): https://www.vfsglobal.com/india/

FOR FRRO REGISTRATION/EXTENSIONS:
• FRRO Portal: https://frro.gov.in
• Email: frrodelhi@nic.in (Delhi FRRO; other regions have separate contacts)

FOR RELATIONSHIP VERIFICATION/COMPLEX CASES:
• Ministry of Home Affairs, Visa Division: https://www.mha.gov.in
• Email: visa@mha.gov.in (for employer/sponsor inquiries)

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: India Student Visa
COUNTRY: India
SOURCE: https://www.boi.gov.in | https://www.mea.gov.in | https://frro.gov.in
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• For foreigners admitted to recognized educational institutions in India for full-time academic programs
• Covers: University degrees, diploma courses, research programs, language studies, yoga/ayurveda courses at recognized institutions
• Validity: Duration of course + 1 year (or as specified by institution), extendable in India
• Mandatory registration with FRRO/FRO within 14 days of arrival if stay >180 days

=== ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA ===
APPLICANT MUST:
□ Have admission offer from recognized Indian educational institution (UGC/AICTE/ICMR/other recognized body)
□ Be enrolled in full-time academic program (part-time/distance learning not eligible for Student Visa)
□ Hold passport valid for at least 6 months beyond intended course duration
□ Demonstrate sufficient financial resources to cover tuition + living expenses
□ Not be of Pakistani origin or hold Pakistani passport (special clearance required)

RECOGNIZED INSTITUTIONS:
• Universities recognized by University Grants Commission (UGC): https://www.ugc.ac.in
• Technical institutions recognized by AICTE: https://www.aicte-india.org
• Medical institutions recognized by NMC: https://www.nmc.org.in
• Other recognized bodies: ICMR, ICAR, BCI, etc.
• Private institutions must have valid recognition/approval from relevant authority

QUALIFYING PROGRAMS:
✓ Bachelor's, Master's, PhD degrees at recognized universities
✓ Diploma/certificate courses (minimum 6 months duration) at recognized institutions
✓ Research fellowships, post-doctoral programs
✓ Language studies (Hindi, Sanskrit, etc.) at recognized institutes
✓ Yoga, Ayurveda, traditional medicine courses at recognized institutions

NON-QUALIFYING PROGRAMS:
✗ Short-term workshops, seminars (<6 months; may use Tourist/Business Visa)
✗ Online/distance learning programs without physical presence in India
✗ Informal/unaccredited courses at unrecognized institutions

=== DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS ===
MANDATORY DOCUMENTS:
□ Completed online application form (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in)
□ Original passport (valid 6+ months beyond intended course duration, 2 blank pages)
□ Two recent passport-sized photographs (2x2 inches, white background)
□ Admission letter from recognized Indian institution (on letterhead, signed):
  - Course name, duration, start/end dates
  - Tuition fees and payment schedule
  - Institution's recognition/accreditation details
□ Proof of financial means:
  - Bank statements (last 6 months) showing sufficient funds, OR
  - Scholarship award letter, OR
  - Sponsorship letter + sponsor's financial documents + proof of relationship
□ Educational qualifications: Attested copies of prior degrees/diplomas, transcripts
□ For research programs: Research proposal, supervisor acceptance letter, institutional approval

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (MAY BE REQUESTED):
□ No Objection Certificate (NOC) from home country government (for certain nationalities/sponsored students)
□ Medical fitness certificate (especially for long-term stays)
□ Police clearance certificate (for certain nationalities/programs)
□ For minor students (<18): Parental consent letter, guardian details in India
□ For language/yoga courses: Proof of prior study or aptitude (if required by institution)

=== APPLICATION PROCESS ===
STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Secure admission from recognized Indian institution; obtain formal admission letter
2. Complete online application at https://indianvisaonline.gov.in
3. Print, sign, and gather all required documents
4. Submit application + documents at:
   • Indian Embassy/Consulate in country of residence, OR
   • Authorized VFS Global center (appointment required in many locations)
5. Pay visa fee as directed (varies by nationality and validity)
6. Processing time: 5-15 working days (longer for certain nationalities/programs)
7. Upon approval: Collect passport with Student Visa sticker
8. Enter India; register with FRRO/FRO within 14 days if stay >180 days

PROCESSING TIMES:
• Standard cases: 5-10 working days at most Missions
• Cases requiring additional verification (certain nationalities, research programs): 10-20 working days
• Peak seasons (Jun-Aug for academic year start): Apply 3-4 weeks in advance

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE) ===
STUDENT VISA FEES (USD, VARIES BY NATIONALITY/VALIDITY):
• Up to 1 year: ~USD 80-120
• 1-3 years: ~USD 120-200
• 3-5 years: ~USD 200-300+
• Additional service fees if applying via VFS Global: ~USD 20-40

NOTE: Fees subject to change; check official portal for current rates by nationality.

=== VISA VALIDITY & EXTENSION ===
INITIAL VALIDITY:
• Typically granted for duration of course + 1 year (for post-completion departure preparation)
• Example: 2-year Master's program → Visa valid for 3 years
• Multiple entry allowed; each stay typically up to 180 days

EXTENSION IN INDIA:
• Apply via FRRO/FRO portal (https://frro.gov.in) at least 2 months before expiry
• Required documents:
  - Valid passport and current Student Visa
  - Updated admission letter/continuation certificate from institution
  - Proof of academic progress (transcripts, attendance records)
  - Proof of continued financial support
  - FRRO registration certificate (if previously registered)
• Processing: 2-4 weeks; applicant may stay in India during processing with acknowledgment receipt
• Extension granted for remaining course duration + 1 year

POST-COMPLETION STAY:
• After course completion, Student Visa remains valid for up to 1 year for:
  - Preparing for departure, OR
  - Pursuing further studies in India (with new admission and visa extension), OR
  - Seeking employment (must change to Employment Visa if job secured)

=== FRRO/FRO REGISTRATION REQUIREMENT ===
MANDATORY REGISTRATION:
• Foreigners on Student Visa staying >180 days continuously MUST register with nearest FRRO/FRO within 14 days of arrival
• Registration is also required if total stay in India exceeds 180 days in a calendar year

REGISTRATION PROCESS:
1. Visit FRRO/FRO portal: https://frro.gov.in
2. Create account and fill registration form
3. Upload required documents:
   - Passport copy (biodata page + visa page)
   - Admission letter, institution details
   - Proof of Indian address (hostel letter, rental agreement, institution letter)
   - Recent photograph
4. Pay registration fee: ~INR 100-500 (varies by duration)
5. Attend in-person appointment at FRRO/FRO office (if required)
6. Receive Registration Certificate (RC) – must carry at all times

CONSEQUENCES OF NON-REGISTRATION:
• Fine: INR 1,000-10,000 depending on delay duration
• Difficulty extending visa, obtaining NOC for travel, or accessing other immigration services
• Potential impact on future Indian visa applications

=== WORK RIGHTS FOR STUDENT VISA HOLDERS ===
PART-TIME WORK:
• Student Visa holders ARE PERMITTED to undertake part-time work/internships with prior approval:
  - Must obtain No Objection Certificate (NOC) from institution AND FRRO/FRO
  - Work must be related to field of study or for practical training
  - Maximum 20 hours/week during academic term; full-time during vacations
  - Cannot take up full-time employment without changing to Employment Visa

INTERNSHIPS/PRACTICAL TRAINING:
• Curricular internships (required by program): Generally permitted with institution approval
• Extra-curricular internships: Require FRRO/FRO approval + institution NOC
• Stipend/salary: Permitted; must comply with Indian labor laws and tax regulations

PROHIBITED ACTIVITIES:
✗ Full-time employment unrelated to studies
✗ Self-employment, freelancing for Indian clients without proper authorization
✗ Work that interferes with academic progress or attendance requirements

=== FAMILY DEPENDENTS ===
ELIGIBILITY TO SPONSOR FAMILY:
• Student Visa holders generally CANNOT sponsor family members for dependent visas
• Exception: PhD scholars/researchers with long-term funding may apply for spouse/children Entry (X) Visa on case-by-case basis

DEPENDENT VISA APPLICATION (IF ELIGIBLE):
• Apply via Indian Mission or FRRO (if already in India)
• Required documents:
  - Primary holder's Student Visa copy, passport, admission/financial proof
  - Marriage certificate (spouse) or birth certificate (children), attested
  - Proof of additional financial support for dependents
  - Passport copies and photographs of dependents

DEPENDENT RIGHTS:
• Spouse: May study or engage in unpaid activities; cannot work without separate Employment Visa
• Children: Can enroll in Indian schools; cannot work without separate authorization
• All dependents: Must register with FRRO/FRO if stay >180 days

=== SCHOLARSHIPS & FUNDING ===
GOVERNMENT OF INDIA SCHOLARSHIPS:
• ICCR (Indian Council for Cultural Relations) scholarships: https://www.iccr.gov.in
• Ministry of External Affairs scholarships for foreign students
• Benefits: Tuition waiver, monthly stipend, accommodation, medical insurance
• Application: Via Indian Mission or ICCR portal; separate from visa application

INSTITUTIONAL SCHOLARSHIPS:
• Many Indian universities offer merit-based/need-based scholarships to international students
• Check institution website for eligibility, application process, deadlines

PROOF OF FUNDING FOR VISA:
• Scholarship award letter must specify: Amount, duration, coverage (tuition/living/both)
• If partial scholarship: Must demonstrate additional funds to cover remaining expenses
• Bank statements/sponsorship letters must show consistent, accessible funds

=== MEDICAL & INSURANCE REQUIREMENTS ===
MEDICAL EXAMINATION:
• May be required for long-term Student Visa applications (>1 year)
• Tests: General physical exam, chest X-ray (TB screening), blood tests as per institution/FRRO requirements
• Conduct at recognized medical facility; report valid for 3 months

HEALTH INSURANCE:
• Strongly recommended for all international students
• Some institutions require proof of health insurance as condition of admission
• Coverage should include: Hospitalization, outpatient care, emergency evacuation, repatriation
• Options: Indian insurance providers (e.g., New India Assurance, ICICI Lombard) or international plans with India coverage

VACCINATION REQUIREMENTS:
• Yellow fever vaccination required if arriving from countries with risk of transmission
• Other vaccinations (Hepatitis, Typhoid, etc.) recommended based on region of study in India

=== COMPLIANCE & ACADEMIC PROGRESS ===
ATTENDANCE & ACADEMIC STANDARDS:
• Must maintain minimum attendance as required by institution (typically 75%+)
• Must make satisfactory academic progress per institutional standards
• Institutions report international student status to Ministry of Home Affairs periodically

CONSEQUENCES OF NON-COMPLIANCE:
• Academic probation/suspension may trigger visa review by FRRO/FRO
• Failure to maintain status may result in visa cancellation and requirement to depart India
• Institution may terminate enrollment independently of immigration status

CHANGE OF INSTITUTION/COURSE:
• If transferring to another Indian institution or changing course:
  - Obtain NOC from current institution
  - Secure admission from new institution
  - Apply for visa modification via FRRO/FRO (if already in India) or Indian Mission (if abroad)
• Cannot commence studies at new institution without proper visa authorization

=== EXIT & POST-STUDY OPTIONS ===
COMPLETION OF STUDIES:
• After course completion, Student Visa remains valid for up to 1 year for departure preparation
• Must depart India before visa expiry OR change status if pursuing further studies/employment

TRANSITION TO EMPLOYMENT VISA:
• If securing job in India after studies:
  - Obtain employment offer meeting Employment Visa criteria (salary threshold, specialized skills)
  - Apply for change of status via FRRO/FRO (if in India) or Indian Mission (if abroad)
  - Cannot commence employment until Employment Visa is granted

FURTHER STUDIES IN INDIA:
• If pursuing higher degree/research in India:
  - Secure admission to new program
  - Apply for Student Visa extension via FRRO/FRO with new admission letter
  - No need to exit India if applying before current visa expiry

=== REFUSAL & REAPPLICATION ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Admission to unrecognized/unaccredited institution
✗ Insufficient proof of financial means for course duration
✗ Incomplete documentation (missing attestation, unclear admission letter)
✗ Previous Indian visa violations or adverse immigration history
✗ Security concerns related to field of study or applicant background

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• Address specific refusal reason: Secure admission to recognized institution, enhance financial documentation, clarify study purpose
• For security-related refusals: May require clearance from Ministry of Home Affairs
• Wait at least 30 days before reapplying unless circumstances changed significantly

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR STUDENT VISA APPLICATIONS:
• Indian Embassy/Consulate: https://www.mea.gov.in/embassies.htm
• VFS Global (if applicable): https://www.vfsglobal.com/india/

FOR FRRO REGISTRATION/EXTENSIONS:
• FRRO Portal: https://frro.gov.in
• Email: frrodelhi@nic.in (Delhi FRRO; other regions have separate contacts)

FOR SCHOLARSHIPS:
• ICCR: https://www.iccr.gov.in | Email: scholarship@iccr.gov.in
• Institution-specific scholarship offices

FOR INSTITUTION RECOGNITION VERIFICATION:
• UGC: https://www.ugc.ac.in
• AICTE: https://www.aicte-india.org
• NMC: https://www.nmc.org.in
