content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: India Tourist Visa & e-Tourist Visa (e-Visa)
COUNTRY: India
SOURCE: https://indianvisaonline.gov.in | https://www.boi.gov.in | https://www.mea.gov.in
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• Tourist visa for recreation, sightseeing, casual visit to meet friends/relatives, short-duration medical treatment, casual business visit
• Two application channels: Regular Tourist Visa (via Indian Mission) OR e-Tourist Visa (online via e-Visa portal)
• e-Tourist Visa available for citizens of 166+ countries; check eligibility by nationality

=== e-TOURIST VISA (e-Visa) ELIGIBILITY ===
ELIGIBLE NATIONALITIES:
• 166+ countries including USA, UK, Canada, Australia, most EU nations, Japan, Singapore, UAE, etc.
• Full list: https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/evisa/tvoa.html

e-VISA SUB-CATEGORIES & VALIDITY:
1. e-Tourist Visa (30 days):
   • Validity: 30 days from date of arrival
   • Entries: Double entry
   • Fee: USD 25 (varies by nationality)

2. e-Tourist Visa (1 year):
   • Validity: 365 days from date of grant
   • Entries: Multiple entry
   • Max stay per visit: 90 days (180 days for USA/UK/Canada/Japan citizens)
   • Fee: USD 40 (varies by nationality)

3. e-Tourist Visa (5 years):
   • Validity: 5 years from date of grant
   • Entries: Multiple entry
   • Max stay per visit: 90 days (180 days for USA/UK/Canada/Japan citizens)
   • Fee: USD 80 (varies by nationality)

=== REGULAR TOURIST VISA (NON-e-VISA) ===
FOR NATIONALITIES NOT ELIGIBLE FOR e-VISA:
• Apply via Indian Embassy/Consulate or VFS Global center in country of residence
• Validity: 6 months to 10 years (multiple entry), at discretion of Indian Mission
• Max stay per visit: Typically 180 days (may be restricted to 90 days for some nationalities)
• Fee: Varies by nationality and validity period (USD 80-200+)

=== ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA (ALL TOURIST VISAS) ===
APPLICANT MUST:
□ Hold passport valid for at least 6 months from date of arrival in India
□ Have at least 2 blank pages in passport for stamping
□ Possess return/onward ticket and sufficient funds for stay
□ Not be of Pakistani origin or hold Pakistani passport (special rules apply)
□ Not be a diplomat/official passport holder (different visa category)
□ Not intend to engage in employment, journalism, missionary work, or long-term study

=== DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS ===

#### e-TOURIST VISA APPLICATION
MANDATORY DOCUMENTS:
□ Scanned copy of passport biodata page (PDF, max 300KB)
□ Recent passport-sized photograph (JPEG, white background, 2x2 inches, max 1MB)
□ Valid email address for e-Visa delivery
□ Credit/debit card or PayPal for fee payment

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (MAY BE REQUESTED):
□ Proof of return/onward travel (flight itinerary)
□ Proof of accommodation in India (hotel booking, invitation letter)
□ Bank statements (last 3 months) showing sufficient funds
□ For medical tourism: Letter from recognized Indian hospital

#### REGULAR TOURIST VISA APPLICATION
MANDATORY DOCUMENTS:
□ Completed online application form (https://indianvisaonline.gov.in)
□ Original passport (valid 6+ months, 2 blank pages)
□ Two recent passport-sized photographs (2x2 inches, white background)
□ Proof of residence in country of application (visa, residence permit if not citizen)
□ Proof of financial means: Bank statements (last 3-6 months)
□ Travel itinerary: Flight reservations, accommodation bookings
□ For first-time applicants: May require in-person interview at Mission/VFS

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (CASE-BY-CASE):
□ Invitation letter from host in India (if visiting friends/relatives)
□ No Objection Certificate (if applicant is minor traveling alone/with one parent)
□ Previous Indian visas (if applicable)

=== APPLICATION PROCESS ===

#### e-TOURIST VISA (ONLINE)
STEPS:
1. Visit https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/evisa/tvoa.html
2. Fill online application form with personal, passport, travel details
3. Upload passport scan and photograph per specifications
4. Pay visa fee via credit/debit card or PayPal
5. Receive Application ID via email; track status online
6. Processing time: Typically 72 hours (apply at least 4 days before travel)
7. Upon approval: Receive e-Visa via email; print copy to carry while traveling
8. At Indian airport: Present printed e-Visa + passport at dedicated e-Visa immigration counter

IMPORTANT e-VISA RULES:
• Apply minimum 4 days before travel; maximum 120 days before travel
• e-Visa is non-extendable and non-convertible to other visa types
• Entry allowed only through 28 designated airports and 5 designated seaports
• Exit can be from any authorized Immigration Check Post in India
• e-Visa holders must carry printed copy + passport with same details used in application

#### REGULAR TOURIST VISA (VIA INDIAN MISSION/VFS)
STEPS:
1. Complete online application at https://indianvisaonline.gov.in
2. Print and sign application form
3. Gather required documents (passport, photos, financial proof, itinerary)
4. Submit application + documents at:
   • Indian Embassy/Consulate in country of residence, OR
   • Authorized VFS Global center (appointment required in many locations)
5. Pay visa fee as directed (cash, card, demand draft depending on location)
6. Processing time: 3-10 working days (varies by Mission; longer during peak seasons)
7. Collect passport with visa sticker or receive via courier if service available

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE) ===

#### e-TOURIST VISA FEES (USD, VARIES BY NATIONALITY)
• 30-day e-Tourist Visa: USD 25
• 1-year e-Tourist Visa: USD 40
• 5-year e-Tourist Visa: USD 80
• Payment: Credit/debit card, PayPal (2.5% bank processing fee may apply)

#### REGULAR TOURIST VISA FEES (USD, VARIES BY NATIONALITY/VALIDITY)
• 6-month multiple entry: ~USD 80-100
• 1-year multiple entry: ~USD 100-150
• 5-year multiple entry: ~USD 150-200+
• Additional service fees if applying via VFS Global: ~USD 20-40

NOTE: Fees subject to change; check official portal for current rates by nationality.

=== PROCESSING TIMES ===
e-TOURIST VISA:
• Standard: 72 hours from application submission
• Peak seasons (Oct-Mar): May take 4-5 days
• Apply at least 4 days before intended travel; not later than 120 days before travel

REGULAR TOURIST VISA:
• Standard: 3-5 working days at most Missions
• Complex cases/additional scrutiny: 7-10 working days
• Peak seasons/high-volume Missions: May take longer; apply 2-3 weeks in advance

=== ENTRY & STAY CONDITIONS ===
MAXIMUM STAY PER VISIT:
• e-Tourist Visa (30-day): Max 30 days from date of arrival, non-extendable
• e-Tourist Visa (1-year/5-year): Max 90 days per visit (180 days for USA/UK/Canada/Japan citizens)
• Regular Tourist Visa: Typically 180 days per visit (may be restricted to 90 days for some nationalities)

TOTAL STAY LIMITS:
• e-Tourist Visa: No explicit annual limit, but consecutive stays should not appear as de facto residence
• Regular Tourist Visa: Continuous stay beyond 180 days may require registration with FRRO

EXTENSION OF STAY:
• e-Tourist Visa: NOT extendable; must exit India and apply for new visa if needed
• Regular Tourist Visa: May be extended in exceptional circumstances (medical emergency, force majeure) via FRRO/FRO office; not for tourism extension

REGISTRATION REQUIREMENT:
• Foreigners staying >180 days continuously must register with nearest FRRO/FRO within 14 days of expiry of initial 180 days
• Applies to regular tourist visa holders; e-Tourist visa holders typically cannot exceed 180 days

=== RESTRICTIONS & PROHIBITED ACTIVITIES ===
TOURIST VISA HOLDERS CANNOT:
✗ Engage in any employment, paid or unpaid
✗ Undertake journalism, film production, or missionary work
✗ Enroll in formal academic programs (requires Student Visa)
✗ Conduct business meetings beyond casual visits (requires Business Visa)
✗ Visit restricted/protected areas without special permit (e.g., certain border regions, tribal areas)

RESTRICTED AREA PERMITS:
• Certain regions require Inner Line Permit (ILP) or Protected Area Permit (PAP):
  - Northeast states (Arunachal Pradesh, Mizoram, Nagaland, etc.)
  - Andaman & Nicobar Islands (some areas)
  - Jammu & Kashmir (certain areas)
  - Sikkim (beyond Gangtok)
• Apply via Indian Mission or online portals; separate fee and processing time

=== MEDICAL & CHARACTER REQUIREMENTS ===
MEDICAL EXAMINATION:
• Not required for tourist visa applications under 180 days
• May be requested for longer stays or if applicant has declared medical conditions

YELLOW FEVER VACCINATION:
• Required if arriving from countries with risk of yellow fever transmission
• Must present International Certificate of Vaccination (ICVP) at entry

CHARACTER/SECURITY SCREENING:
• All applicants subject to security background checks
• Previous travel to certain regions, criminal history, or adverse immigration record may trigger additional scrutiny
• Full disclosure required; false statements can result in visa refusal + future ban

=== REFUSAL & REAPPLICATION ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Incomplete application or missing documents
✗ Passport validity <6 months or insufficient blank pages
✗ Insufficient proof of funds or unclear travel purpose
✗ Previous Indian visa violations (overstay, unauthorized work)
✗ Security concerns or adverse background checks
✗ Applicant of Pakistani origin (special clearance required)

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• Address specific refusal reason with stronger documentation
• Wait at least 30 days before reapplying unless circumstances changed
• For security-related refusals: May require special clearance from Ministry of Home Affairs
• Consider applying via Indian Mission directly for complex cases

=== OVERSTAY PENALTIES ===
CONSEQUENCES OF OVERSTAYING:
• Fine: USD 300+ (varies by duration and nationality)
• Possible deportation at applicant's expense
• Entry ban: 1 year to permanent, depending on overstay duration and circumstances
• Future Indian visa applications will face additional scrutiny or refusal

VOLUNTARY DEPARTURE:
• If realize overstay before detection: Report to nearest FRRO/FRO immediately
• May receive reduced penalty vs. being caught at airport exit
• Still results in immigration record affecting future applications

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR e-VISA APPLICATIONS:
• e-Visa Helpdesk: https://indianvisaonline.gov.in/evisa/helpdesk.html
• Email: indianvisaonline@gov.in
• Phone: +91-11-24300666 (during business hours)

FOR REGULAR VISA APPLICATIONS:
• Indian Embassy/Consulate in country of residence: https://www.mea.gov.in/embassies.htm
• VFS Global (if applicable): https://www.vfsglobal.com/india/

FOR EXTENSIONS/FRRO REGISTRATION:
• FRRO/FRO Portal: https://frro.gov.in
• Email: frrodelhi@nic.in (Delhi FRRO; other regions have separate contacts)

FOR RESTRICTED AREA PERMITS:
• Ministry of Home Affairs: https://www.mha.gov.in
• State tourism departments for region-specific permits

content.full_text:
=== VISA-EXEMPT COUNTRIES (SHORT-STAY) ===
NATIONALITIES THAT DO NOT REQUIRE VISA FOR TOURISM/BUSINESS (UP TO 90 DAYS):
• United States, Canada, United Kingdom, Ireland
• Most European Union countries, Switzerland, Norway, Iceland
• Australia, New Zealand, Singapore, South Korea, Brunei
• UAE, Qatar (30 days), Thailand (15 days), Indonesia (15 days), etc.
• Full list: https://www.mofa.go.jp/j_info/visit/visa/short/novisa.html

IMPORTANT CONDITIONS:
• Passport must be valid for duration of stay
• Must have return/onward ticket
• Must not engage in remunerative activities
• Immigration officer may ask for proof of funds, accommodation, itinerary

=== FINANCIAL PROOF FOR TOURIST VISA APPLICANTS ===
GUIDELINE AMOUNTS:
• ~¥10,000-15,000 per day of stay for basic tourism
• Higher amounts expected for longer stays or luxury travel
• Proof via: Bank statements (last 3 months), sponsorship letter, traveler's checks

=== INVITATION LETTER REQUIREMENTS ===
FOR BUSINESS VISAS:
• Must be on company letterhead, signed by authorized representative
• Must include: applicant name, purpose of visit, duration, itinerary, guarantee of expenses (if applicable)
• For multiple entries: Explicitly state frequency and purpose

FOR FAMILY/FRIEND VISITS:
• Letter from host in Japan with: relationship to applicant, address, phone, duration of stay
• Copy of host's residence card (if foreign resident) or family register (if Japanese national)
• Proof of host's ability to support visit (if applicable)

=== CERTIFICATE OF ELIGIBILITY (CoE) PROCESS ===
WHO APPLIES:
• Sponsor in Japan (employer, school, family member) applies to Immigration Services Agency
• NOT the visa applicant abroad

TIMELINE:
• Standard processing: 1-3 months from submission
• Complex cases: May take longer

REQUIRED DOCUMENTS (VARIES BY STATUS):
• Work visas: Employment contract, company registration, applicant's qualifications
• Student visas: School acceptance letter, financial proof, academic transcripts
• Spouse/Dependent visas: Marriage/birth certificates, sponsor's residence proof, financial stability

AFTER CoE APPROVAL:
• Original CoE sent to applicant abroad
• Applicant uses CoE + passport + photo to apply for visa at Japanese Diplomatic Mission
• Visa typically issued within 1 week if CoE is valid

=== WORK VISA CATEGORIES (COMMON) ===
ENGINEER / SPECIALIST IN HUMANITIES / INTERNATIONAL SERVICES:
• For professionals in IT, engineering, translation, marketing, etc.
• Requirements: Bachelor's degree OR 10+ years relevant experience + job offer

SKILLED LABOR:
• For chefs, artisans, athletes, etc. with specialized skills
• Requirements: Proof of skill certification + job offer

INTRA-COMPANY TRANSFEREE:
• For employees transferred from overseas branch to Japan
• Requirements: Employment history with company + job offer in Japan

HIGHLY SKILLED PROFESSIONAL (HSP):
• Points-based system for top talent (researchers, executives, engineers)
• Benefits: Faster permanent residence pathway, spouse work rights, parent accompaniment

=== STUDENT VISA REQUIREMENTS ===
ELIGIBILITY:
• Acceptance from Japanese educational institution (university, language school, vocational)
• Financial proof: ~¥1.5-2 million/year for living expenses + tuition
• Academic background consistent with intended study
• Japanese language ability (for Japanese-taught programs) or English proficiency (for English programs)

APPLICATION FLOW:
1. School applies for CoE on behalf of accepted student
2. CoE approved → sent to student abroad
3. Student applies for visa at Japanese Diplomatic Mission with CoE + passport + photo
4. Visa issued → student enters Japan
5. After arrival: Register residence at local municipal office, enroll in national health insurance

WORK RIGHTS FOR STUDENTS:
• Must apply for "Permission to Engage in Activity Other Than That Permitted" at Immigration
• If granted: Max 28 hours/week during term, full-time during scheduled breaks
• Work must not interfere with studies

=== PROCESSING TIMES & FEES ===
VISA APPLICATION PROCESSING:
• Standard: ~1 week if documents complete and no consultation with Tokyo required
• Complex cases / CoE verification: 2-4 weeks
• Peak seasons (March, September): Potential delays

VISA FEES:
• Many nationalities: Visa application fee is FREE (reciprocity agreements)
• Some nationalities: ¥3,000-6,000 depending on visa type and nationality
• Check with specific Diplomatic Mission for exact fee

=== RE-ENTRY PERMIT RULES ===
FOR FOREIGN RESIDENTS LEAVING JAPAN:
• Special Re-entry Permit: If departing with valid residence card + re-entry intent, can return within 1 year (or until residence expiry) without separate permit
• Re-entry Permit Application: If staying abroad >1 year or without residence card, must apply at Immigration before departure (fee: ¥3,500 single / ¥7,000 multiple)

=== EXTENSION / CHANGE OF STATUS OF RESIDENCE ===
APPLICATION WITHIN JAPAN:
• Submit to Immigration Services Agency office with jurisdiction over residence
• Required: Application form, passport, residence card, supporting documents for new status
• Processing: 2 weeks to 2 months depending on complexity
• During processing: Applicant may stay in Japan even if current status expires (with "Application in Progress" receipt)

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: Singapore Family Visas (Dependant's Pass, Long-Term Visit Pass, LTVP+)
COUNTRY: Singapore
SOURCE: https://www.mom.gov.sg | https://www.ica.gov.sg
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
Singapore offers several passes for family members of foreign professionals, entrepreneurs, and Singapore Citizens/Permanent Residents (SC/PR). The type of pass depends on the sponsor's status and the family relationship.

MAIN FAMILY PASS TYPES:
1. Dependant's Pass (DP): For legally married spouses and unmarried children (<21) of Employment Pass (EP), S Pass, or EntrePass holders.
2. Long-Term Visit Pass (LTVP): For common-law spouses, step-children, handicapped children (>21), parents of EP holders, or family of SC/PR.
3. LTVP+ (Enhanced): For spouses of Singapore Citizens with stable income; offers longer validity and work rights.
4. Pre-approved LTVP (for EP holders): High earners can get pre-approval for spouse/parents before they arrive.

=== 1. DEPENDANT'S PASS (DP) ===
ELIGIBILITY - SPONSOR MUST:
□ Hold a valid Employment Pass (EP), S Pass, or EntrePass.
□ Earn a fixed monthly salary of at least SGD 6,000 (higher for older applications).
□ Work for a well-established, reputable firm in Singapore.

ELIGIBLE FAMILY MEMBERS:
• Legally married spouse.
• Unmarried children under 21 years old (including legally adopted children).

KEY BENEFITS:
• Validity: Tied to sponsor's pass validity (up to 2 years initially, renewable).
• Work Rights: DP holders CAN work in Singapore WITHOUT a separate work pass, BUT must obtain a Letter of Consent (LOC) from MOM before starting employment.
• Study: Children can attend schools in Singapore.

DOCUMENTS REQUIRED:
□ Sponsor's passport and current pass details.
□ Marriage certificate (for spouse).
□ Birth certificates (for children).
□ Adoption papers (if applicable).
□ Recent passport-sized photos.
□ Educational certificates (for spouse applying for LOC).

APPLICATION PROCESS:
• Submitted online via MOM's EP Online portal by the sponsor or employer.
• Processing time: ~3 weeks (if documents complete).
• Fee: SGD 105 per pass (issuance/renewal).

=== 2. LONG-TERM VISIT PASS (LTVP) ===
ELIGIBILITY SCENARIOS:

A. FOR FAMILIES OF EP HOLDERS (Foreigners):
□ Spouse who is not eligible for DP (e.g., common-law partner).
□ Unmarried handicapped children over 21.
□ Parents of EP holder (sponsor must earn ≥ SGD 12,000/month).
□ Step-children under 21.

B. FOR FAMILIES OF SINGAPORE CITIZENS (SC) OR PERMANENT RESIDENTS (PR):
□ Foreign spouse of SC/PR.
□ Unmarried children (<21) of SC/PR.
□ Parents of SC (Long-Term Visit Pass for Parents).
□ Common-law spouses, step-children, handicapped children.

KEY BENEFITS:
• Validity: Up to 2 years initially (can be up to 3-5 years for LTVP+ or SC spouses).
• Work Rights: Generally CANNOT work unless they apply for and receive a separate work pass (Employment Pass, S Pass, Work Permit) OR a Letter of Consent (if eligible under specific schemes like LTVP+).
• No automatic work rights like DP.

DOCUMENTS REQUIRED:
□ Sponsor's NRIC (if SC/PR) or Pass details (if foreigner).
□ Marriage/Birth certificates (translated if not in English).
• Proof of relationship authenticity (photos, chat logs, joint finances) especially for common-law spouses.
□ Financial proof of sponsor (salary slips, tax notices).
□ Medical report (sometimes required for parents/older dependents).

APPLICATION PROCESS:
• SC/PR Families: Apply via ICA e-Service.
• Foreign Worker Families: Apply via MOM's EP Online portal.
• Processing time: ~3 weeks (MOM) to 6 weeks (ICA).
• Fee: SGD 30 (application) + SGD 60 (issuance) per pass.

=== 3. LTVP+ (ENHANCED LONG-TERM VISIT PASS) ===
TARGET GROUP:
• Foreign spouses of Singapore Citizens who have a stable income and intend to settle long-term in Singapore.

ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA:
□ Married to a Singapore Citizen.
□ Have at least one Singapore Citizen child together, OR been married for at least 3 years.
□ Sponsor (SC spouse) has a stable income and good employment history.

KEY BENEFITS (VS STANDARD LTVP):
• Longer Validity: Up to 3 years initially, up to 5 years on renewal.
• Work Rights: Easier pathway to work; eligible for a Letter of Consent (LOC) to work without a separate work pass (similar to DP).
• Priority consideration for permanent residence (PR) applications.

APPLICATION:
• Applied via ICA e-Service along with the standard LTVP application.
• Must explicitly indicate interest in LTVP+ and provide supporting evidence (marriage duration, children's birth certs).

=== 4. PRE-APPROVED LTVP (FOR HIGH-EARNING EP HOLDERS) ===
OVERVIEW:
• Allows top-tier EP holders to secure LTVP approval for spouse/parents BEFORE arriving in Singapore or before the family member applies.

ELIGIBILITY - SPONSOR MUST:
□ Hold an Employment Pass.
□ Earn a fixed monthly salary of at least SGD 12,000.
□ Work for a well-established firm.

BENEFITS:
• Guarantees eligibility for LTVP subject to basic health/security checks.
• Faster processing for family members when they eventually apply.
• Valid for up to 2 years.

APPLICATION:
• Employer submits request via EP Online when applying for or renewing the EP.
• No additional fee for pre-approval itself.

=== FINANCIAL REQUIREMENTS FOR SPONSORS ===
MINIMUM SALARY THRESHOLDS (GUIDELINES):
• For Dependant's Pass (Spouse/Children): Sponsor earns ≥ SGD 6,000/month.
• For LTVP (Parents of EP holder): Sponsor earns ≥ SGD 12,000/month.
• For LTVP (Parents of SC/PR): Sponsor must show ability to support parents (no fixed threshold, but stable income required).
• Additional funds may be requested for each dependent to cover living expenses.

SUPPORT UNDERTAKING:
• Sponsor must sign a Letter of Undertaking (LOU) accepting responsibility for the dependent's maintenance and conduct in Singapore.
• Sponsor must ensure dependent leaves Singapore if pass is cancelled.

=== WORK RIGHTS SUMMARY ===
| Pass Type | Can Work? | Requirement |
|-----------|-----------|-------------|
| Dependant's Pass (DP) | YES | Must obtain Letter of Consent (LOC) from MOM before starting job. |
| LTVP (Standard) | NO | Must apply for own Employment Pass, S Pass, or Work Permit. |
| LTVP+ | YES | Eligible for Letter of Consent (LOC) similar to DP. |
| Pre-approved LTVP | NO | Must apply for own work pass unless upgraded to LTVP+. |

NOTE: LOC is only granted for professional, managerial, or specialized jobs. It is not granted for semi-skilled or manual labor roles.

=== EDUCATION FOR CHILDREN ===
• DP and LTVP holders' children can enroll in Singapore schools (government, aided, or private).
• Admission is subject to vacancy and MOE guidelines.
• International students pay higher fees than citizens/PRs.
• Children do NOT need a separate Student's Pass if holding DP/LTVP.

=== VALIDITY & RENEWAL ===
VALIDITY PERIOD:
• Typically tied to the sponsor's pass validity (for foreign sponsors).
• For SC/PR sponsors: Granted up to 2 years initially, renewable up to 3-5 years (LTVP+).

RENEWAL PROCESS:
• Apply online at least 3 months before expiry.
• Required: Updated sponsor employment proof, dependent's passport validity, medical reports (if requested).
• Fee: Same as initial issuance.

CANCELLATION CONDITIONS:
• Sponsor loses their job/pass → Family passes cancelled within 7 days (grace period to leave or change status).
• Divorce/Breakup → Pass cancelled immediately upon notification.
• Breach of conditions (illegal work, overstaying).

=== APPLICATION PROCEDURES ===
FOR FAMILIES OF FOREIGN PROFESSIONALS (EP/S Pass):
1. Employer/Sponsor logs into MOM EP Online.
2. Submit application for DP/LTVP.
3. Upload documents (marriage/birth certs, passports).
4. Pay fee (SGD 105).
5. Wait for In-Principle Approval (IPA) (~3 weeks).
6. Collect pass card at MOM Services Centre.

FOR FAMILIES OF SINGAPORE CITIZENS/PRs:
1. Sponsor logs into ICA e-Service.
2. Submit application for LTVP/LTVP+.
3. Upload documents + proof of relationship/finance.
4. Pay fee (SGD 30 app + SGD 60 issuance).
5. Wait for approval (~6 weeks).
6. Collect pass card at ICA Building.

=== MEDICAL INSURANCE REQUIREMENTS ===
• Strongly recommended for all LTVP/DP holders.
• Some employers/sponsors must provide mandatory insurance (especially for LTVP+ or specific sectors).
• Coverage should include hospitalization and surgery.

=== COMMON REASONS FOR REJECTION ===
✗ Insufficient sponsor salary (below thresholds).
✗ Inability to prove genuine relationship (especially for common-law spouses).
✗ Sponsor has poor compliance record (late CPF payments, previous visa violations).
✗ Dependent has adverse immigration/medical history.
✗ Incomplete documentation (missing translations, expired passports).

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR FAMILIES OF FOREIGN PROFESSIONALS:
• Ministry of Manpower (MOM): https://www.mom.gov.sg
• MOM Contact Centre: +65 6438 5122

FOR FAMILIES OF SINGAPORE CITIZENS/PRs:
• Immigration & Checkpoints Authority (ICA): https://www.ica.gov.sg
• ICA Hotline: +65 6478 7300

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: Singapore Entry Visa (Short-Term Business/Social Visit)
COUNTRY: Singapore
SOURCE: https://www.ica.gov.sg/enter-transit-depart/entering-singapore/visa_requirements
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW & KEY DEFINITIONS ===
ENTRY VISA DEFINITION:
• Pre-entry permission to travel to and seek entry into Singapore
• Does NOT guarantee entry; final decision made by ICA officer at checkpoint
• Does NOT determine duration of stay; Visit Pass (e-Pass) issued at entry sets stay period
• SG Arrival Card is NOT a visa; separate mandatory requirement for all travelers

e-PASS DIGITAL NOTIFICATION (FROM 11 MARCH 2022):
• Visit Pass issued digitally via email (no passport endorsement)
• Email sent to address provided in SG Arrival Card submission
• e-Pass indicates: Period of stay granted, last day of stay allowed, conditions
• Must retain e-Pass email; may be requested by authorities during stay

=== NATIONALITIES REQUIRING ENTRY VISA ===
TRAVEL DOCUMENT HOLDERS REQUIRING VISA:
Afghanistan, Algeria, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, Belarus, Democratic People's Republic of Korea, Egypt, Georgia, India, Iran, Iraq, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kosovo, Kyrgyzstan, Lebanon, Libya, Mali, Moldova, Morocco, Nigeria, Pakistan, Russia, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Tajikistan, Tunisia, Turkmenistan, Ukraine, Uzbekistan, Yemen

SPECIAL TRAVEL DOCUMENTS REQUIRING VISA (REGARDLESS OF NATIONALITY):
• Refugee travel document (subject to recognition assessment)
• Alien's passport (subject to recognition assessment)
• Palestinian Authority passport
• Temporary passport issued by United Arab Emirates
• Document of Identity issued by Hong Kong SAR
• Travel Permit issued by Macao SAR
• PRC Travel Document (non-ordinary passport)

DIPLOMATIC/OFFICIAL PASSPORT EXEMPTIONS:
• Holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports from visa-required countries may be exempt
• Verify with Singapore Overseas Mission for specific nationality exemptions

=== VISA-EXEMPT NATIONALITIES (NO VISA REQUIRED) ===
NATIONALITIES THAT CAN ENTER WITHOUT VISA FOR SHORT VISITS:
• United States, Canada, United Kingdom, Ireland
• Most European Union countries, Switzerland, Norway, Iceland
• Australia, New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, Brunei
• Malaysia (unlimited stay), Thailand (30 days), Indonesia (30 days)
• UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, Oman, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia
• Full official list: https://www.ica.gov.sg/enter-transit-depart/entering-singapore/visa_requirements

DURATION OF STAY FOR VISA-EXEMPT NATIONALS:
• Most nationalities: 30 days (extendable via STVP application)
• USA, UK, EU, Australia, NZ, Japan, South Korea: Up to 90 days
• Malaysia citizens: Unlimited stay (subject to officer discretion)
• Duration granted is at discretion of ICA officer at point of entry

=== APPLICATION CHANNELS ===
ONLINE VIA e-SERVICE (REQUIRES LOCAL CONTACT):
• Local Contact must be: Singapore Citizen/PR aged 21+ with Singpass, OR Singapore company with Corppass
• Local Contact submits application on behalf of foreign applicant
• Portal: https://e-services.ica.gov.sg/visas/
• Recommended: Apply within 30 days prior to intended arrival

THROUGH AUTHORISED VISA AGENT:
• For applicants without Local Contact
• Agents appointed by Singapore Overseas Missions
• Liaise directly with Overseas Mission for agent list and procedures

AT SINGAPORE OVERSEAS MISSION:
• Apply directly at Embassy/Consulate-General in your country of residence
• Check mission website for appointment requirements, document specifics, processing times
• Recommended: Apply within 30 days prior to intended arrival

=== REQUIRED DOCUMENTS ===
MANDATORY FOR ALL APPLICATIONS:
□ Duly completed and signed Form 14A (PDF, 202KB)
□ Recent passport-sized colour photograph (taken within last 3 months, white background, 35x45mm)
□ Photocopy of passport biodata page (valid for at least 6 months from intended entry date)

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (CASE-BY-CASE):
□ Form V39A: Letter of Introduction for Visa Application (LOI) - if required
□ For Business Visits:
  - Invitation letter from Singapore company (on letterhead, signed)
  - Company registration proof (ACRA business profile)
  - Applicant's employment letter confirming business purpose
  - Conference/event registration (if applicable)
□ For Social Visits:
  - Invitation letter from Singapore resident (friend/family)
  - Host's NRIC copy (if Singapore Citizen/PR) or valid pass copy (if foreign resident)
  - Proof of relationship: photos, communication records, joint documents
  - Host's accommodation proof (if providing lodging)
□ Proof of sufficient funds: Bank statements, sponsorship letter
□ Confirmed onward/return travel tickets
□ Accommodation confirmation in Singapore

=== FEES & PAYMENT ===
VISA APPLICATION FEE:
• SGD 30 (non-refundable processing fee)
• Payable online via Visa or MasterCard credit/debit card
• Paid at time of e-Service submission or as directed by Overseas Mission

EXTENSION OF STAY (STVP) FEE:
• SGD 40 (non-refundable) if applying for Short-Term Visit Pass extension

PAYMENT METHODS:
• Credit/debit card (Visa, MasterCard) for online applications
• Cash/other methods as specified by Overseas Mission for in-person applications

=== PROCESSING TIMES ===
STANDARD PROCESSING:
• ~3 working days (excluding day of submission) for complete applications
• Some applications may take longer due to: additional verification, security checks, high volume

FACTORS AFFECTING PROCESSING:
• Completeness and consistency of application documents
• Nationality and travel history of applicant
• Purpose of visit and supporting evidence strength
• Peak travel seasons (December-January, June-August)

RECOMMENDED APPLICATION TIMING:
• Apply within 30 days prior to intended arrival in Singapore
• Allow buffer time for potential delays or requests for additional information

=== VISA-FREE TRANSIT FACILITY (VFTF) ===
ELIGIBLE NATIONALS:
• Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS) countries
• Georgia, India, Turkmenistan, Ukraine
• Selected PRC document holders: Hong Kong SAR DOI, Macau SAR Travel Permit, PRC Travel Document (non-ordinary passports)

VFTF CONDITIONS:
□ Must be in transit to/from a third country (NOT returning to country of origin)
□ Must hold valid onward ticket departing Singapore within 96 hours
□ Must meet standard entry requirements: valid passport, sufficient funds, confirmed accommodation
□ Must not have been refused entry to Singapore previously
□ Request VFTF at checkpoint upon arrival; no prior application needed

=== EXTENSION OF STAY (SHORT-TERM VISIT PASS) ===
ELIGIBILITY:
• Must apply BEFORE current Visit Pass expires
• Must have valid reason: medical treatment, family emergency, unforeseen circumstances
• Tourism extensions generally NOT granted unless exceptional circumstances

APPLICATION PROCESS:
• Apply online: https://e-services.ica.gov.sg/stvp/
• Required: Passport details, current Visit Pass details, reason for extension, supporting documents
• Processing: ~3-5 working days
• Fee: SGD 40 (non-refundable)

MAXIMUM EXTENSION:
• Typically up to 89 days total stay from original entry date
• Further extensions require strong justification and ICA approval

=== HEALTH & CHARACTER REQUIREMENTS ===
YELLOW FEVER VACCINATION:
• Required if arriving from countries with risk of yellow fever transmission
• Must present International Certificate of Vaccination (ICVP)
• List of affected countries: https://www.ica.gov.sg/health/yellow_fever

CRIMINAL RECORD CONSIDERATIONS:
• Applicants with serious criminal convictions may be refused entry/visa
• ICA may request police certificate for certain nationalities/applications
• Full disclosure required; false statements can result in entry ban

=== ENTRY PROCESS AT CHECKPOINT ===
DOCUMENTS TO PRESENT:
• Valid passport (with visa if required)
• SG Arrival Card confirmation (submitted online up to 3 days before arrival)
• e-Pass notification email (if previously visited Singapore with e-Pass system)
• Supporting documents if requested: return ticket, accommodation proof, funds evidence

POSSIBLE QUESTIONS FROM ICA OFFICER:
• Purpose of visit and intended duration
• Accommodation arrangements in Singapore
• Source of funds for the trip
• Onward travel plans
• Employment status and ties to home country

BIOMETRICS COLLECTION:
• Fingerprints and photo may be collected for certain nationalities
• Part of standard immigration processing; no additional fee

=== COMPLIANCE & PENALTIES ===
OVERSTAYING CONSEQUENCES:
• Fine: Up to SGD 10,000
• Imprisonment: Up to 6 months
• Caning: For male offenders under 50 (in severe cases)
• Future entry ban: Typically 1-5 years, depending on overstay duration
• Record in immigration system affecting future applications globally

VOLUNTARY DEPARTURE:
• If realize overstay before detection: Report to ICA immediately
• May receive reduced penalty vs. being caught at checkpoint
• Still results in immigration record and potential future restrictions

UNAUTHORIZED EMPLOYMENT:
• Engaging in work without proper work pass is illegal
• Penalties: Fine, imprisonment, deportation, entry ban
• Employers also face penalties for hiring unauthorized workers

=== REJECTION & REAPPLICATION ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Incomplete or inconsistent application information
✗ Insufficient proof of purpose/funds/accommodation
✗ Previous immigration violations (Singapore or other countries)
✗ Security concerns or adverse background checks
✗ Suspicion of intent to work illegally or overstay

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• No formal appeal process for tourist/social visit visa refusals
• May reapply with stronger documentation addressing refusal reasons
• Wait at least 1-2 months before reapplying unless circumstances changed significantly
• For business visas: Reapply with enhanced company support documentation

=== FRAUD WARNING ===
• Apply ONLY through official channels: ICA e-Service, authorised visa agents, or Singapore Overseas Missions
• ICA does NOT request fees via wire transfer, cryptocurrency, or unofficial agents
• SG Arrival Card is FREE; beware of scam sites charging fees
• Report suspicious websites/agents to ICA: https://www.ica.gov.sg/contact-us

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR VISA APPLICATION QUESTIONS:
• ICA e-Service: https://e-services.ica.gov.sg/visas/
• ICA Hotline: +65 6478 7300 (Mon-Fri 8am-5pm, Sat 8am-1pm)
• Email: ica_feedback@ica.gov.sg
• Walk-in: ICA Building, 10 Kallang Road, Singapore 208718 (appointment required)

FOR SG ARRIVAL CARD:
• Portal: https://eservices.ica.gov.sg/sgarrivalcard/
• Technical support: Available via portal help section

FOR SINGAPORE OVERSEAS MISSIONS:
• Directory: https://www.mfa.gov.sg/overseasmission
• Contact specific mission for local procedures, appointment requirements, document specifics

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: South Korea Family Visas (F-1, F-2, F-3, F-4, F-5, F-6)
COUNTRY: South Korea
SOURCE: https://www.hikorea.go.kr | https://overseas.mofa.go.kr
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
South Korea offers several family-based residence visas depending on relationship to Korean citizen or foreign resident:

• F-1: Visiting/Temporary Stay (short-term family visits)
• F-2: Long-term residence (for spouses/children of Korean citizens or permanent residents)
• F-3: Dependent Visa (for family of foreign residents on E/D visas)
• F-4: Overseas Korean Visa (for ethnic Koreans with foreign citizenship)
• F-5: Permanent Residence (after meeting long-term residence criteria)
• F-6: Marriage Immigrant Visa (for foreign spouses of Korean citizens)

This file focuses on F-3 (Dependent) and F-6 (Marriage Immigrant) as most common for family reunification.

=== F-3 DEPENDENT VISA (FOR FAMILY OF FOREIGN RESIDENTS) ===
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Primary visa holder must have valid E (work), D (student), or other long-term visa with ARC
□ Applicant must be spouse or unmarried child under 20 of primary holder
□ Primary holder must demonstrate sufficient financial means to support dependent
□ Accommodation in Korea must meet minimum standards

DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS:
□ Completed visa application form
□ Recent passport-sized photograph (3.5cm x 4.5cm, white background)
□ Original passport (valid 6+ months)
□ Photocopy of passport biodata page
□ Primary holder's ARC copy + passport copy
□ Proof of relationship:
  - Spouse: Attested marriage certificate (apostilled + Korean translation)
  - Child: Attested birth certificate showing parentage (apostilled + Korean translation)
□ Primary holder's proof of financial means: Employment contract, bank statements, tax certificate
□ Proof of accommodation in Korea: Rental agreement, dorm confirmation, or property deed

APPLICATION PROCESS:
• If applying abroad: Submit at Korean Embassy/Consulate with primary holder's documents
• If applying in Korea: Submit at local Immigration Office with primary holder present
• Processing: 5-7 business days abroad; 2-4 weeks in Korea
• Fee: ~USD 30-60 (varies by nationality)

VISA VALIDITY & RIGHTS:
• Validity: Matches primary holder's ARC validity
• Multiple-entry allowed after ARC issuance
• Work rights: F-3 holders CANNOT work without separate work permission from Immigration
• Study rights: Can enroll in Korean/international schools without additional visa

=== F-6 MARRIAGE IMMIGRANT VISA (FOR FOREIGN SPOUSES OF KOREAN CITIZENS) ===
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Legally married to Korean citizen (marriage registered in Korea)
□ Marriage must be genuine (not for immigration purposes)
□ Korean spouse must meet minimum income/housing requirements to support foreign spouse
□ Applicant must pass basic Korean language/culture interview (at Embassy or Immigration)

DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS:
□ Completed visa application form
□ Recent passport-sized photograph
□ Original passport (valid 6+ months)
□ Photocopy of passport biodata page
□ Marriage certificate (apostilled + Korean translation)
□ Korean spouse's family registry (가족관계증명서) + resident registration copy
□ Proof of Korean spouse's financial means:
  - Income certificate (소득금액증명원) showing annual income ≥ ~KRW 20-25 million
  - OR asset proof (property, savings) meeting Immigration guidelines
□ Proof of accommodation: Property deed or rental agreement in Korean spouse's name
□ Basic Korean language/culture assessment results (if required by Embassy)

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (MAY BE REQUESTED):
□ Criminal record check from applicant's home country (apostilled)
□ Medical examination report (for long-term stays)
□ Photos of wedding/couple together to prove genuine relationship
□ Communication records, joint financial documents to demonstrate ongoing relationship

APPLICATION PROCESS:
STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Register marriage in Korea (if married abroad): Submit apostilled marriage certificate to Korean Embassy for registration
2. Korean spouse prepares financial/accommodation documentation
3. Applicant completes visa application at Korean Embassy/Consulate
4. Attend interview (if required) to assess relationship genuineness and basic Korean knowledge
5. Submit application + documents
6. Pay visa fee (~USD 30-60)
7. Processing: 2-4 weeks standard; longer if additional verification required
8. Upon approval: Collect passport with F-6 Visa sticker (single-entry, 90-day validity)
9. Enter Korea; apply for ARC at Immigration Office within 90 days

OBTAINING ALIEN REGISTRATION CARD (ARC):
• Must apply at local Immigration Office within 90 days of entry
• Required documents:
  - Passport with F-6 Visa + entry stamp
  - Marriage certificate (original + copy)
  - Korean spouse's family registry + ID copy
  - Proof of address in Korea
  - Recent photo + application form + fee (~KRW 30,000)
• Processing: Typically issued same day or within 2-3 weeks
• ARC validity: Initially 1-2 years; renewable based on continued marriage/residence

VISA VALIDITY & RENEWAL:
• Initial ARC validity: Typically 1-2 years
• Renewal: Apply at Immigration Office at least 1 month before expiry
  - Required: Continued proof of marriage, Korean spouse's financial/accommodation proof
  - Processing: 2-4 weeks
• After 2 years of continuous residence on F-6: May apply for F-5 Permanent Residence

RIGHTS OF F-6 HOLDERS:
✓ Full work rights (no separate work permit needed)
✓ Access to public healthcare, education, social services
✓ Pathway to permanent residence (F-5) after 2 years, citizenship after additional period
✓ Korean language/culture integration support programs available

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE, USD/KRW) ===
F-3/F-6 VISA APPLICATION FEES (VARIES BY NATIONALITY):
• USA citizens: ~USD 50-90
• Most other nationalities: USD 30-60 for single-entry

ARC ISSUANCE FEE IN KOREA:
• Application fee: ~KRW 30,000 (~USD 25)
• Re-issuance/renewal: ~KRW 30,000

ADDITIONAL COSTS:
• Document apostille/legalization: USD 20-100 per document
• Korean translation of documents: ~KRW 30,000-100,000 per document
• Korean language/culture assessment (if required): ~KRW 50,000-100,000
• Visa application center service fee (if applicable): ~USD 20-40

NOTE: Fees subject to change; check official portals for current rates.

=== COMPLIANCE & REPORTING OBLIGATIONS ===
ADDRESS CHANGES:
• Must report change of Korean address to Immigration Office within 14 days
• Update via Hi Korea portal or in-person visit with proof of new address

MARRIAGE STATUS CHANGES:
• If marriage ends (divorce, death of Korean spouse):
  - F-6 status may be reviewed by Immigration
  - May apply for change of status (e.g., to work/student visa) if eligible
  - If no independent status secured: Must depart Korea within 30 days of status change notification

ANNUAL REPORTING (IF REQUIRED):
• Some Immigration Offices require annual check-in for F-6 holders
• Submit updated documents: Passport validity, continued marriage proof, financial support evidence

=== PATHWAY TO PERMANENT RESIDENCE (F-5) ===
ELIGIBILITY FOR F-5 (PERMANENT RESIDENCE):
□ Hold F-6 Visa for at least 2 years of continuous residence in Korea
□ Pass basic Korean language test (TOPIK Level 2 or higher) OR complete government integration program
□ Demonstrate basic knowledge of Korean culture/society
□ Meet income requirement: Annual household income ≥ ~KRW 20-25 million OR asset proof
□ No criminal record in Korea or home country

APPLICATION PROCESS:
• Apply at local Immigration Office
• Required documents:
  - Valid passport and current F-6 ARC
  - Marriage certificate + Korean spouse's family registry
  - TOPIK score certificate or integration program completion certificate
  - Income/asset proof
  - Criminal record check
  - Application form + fee (~KRW 200,000)
• Processing: 2-3 months
• If approved: Receive F-5 ARC with indefinite validity (renewable every 5 years for card re-issuance)

BENEFITS OF F-5 STATUS:
✓ No need to renew visa based on marriage/employment status
✓ Full work rights, access to all public services
✓ Pathway to citizenship after additional 3-5 years of residence

=== REFUSAL & REAPPLICATION ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Inability to prove genuine marriage/relationship (insufficient documentation, inconsistent information)
✗ Korean spouse fails to meet income/accommodation requirements
✗ Applicant has criminal record or adverse immigration history
✗ Failed Korean language/culture assessment (for F-6)
✗ Previous Korean visa violations

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• Address specific refusal reason: Strengthen relationship documentation (additional photos, communication records, joint financial documents), enhance Korean spouse's financial proof, prepare for language assessment
• For relationship verification issues: Provide notarized/certified copies of marriage certificate with official Korean translations
• Wait at least 30 days before reapplying unless circumstances changed significantly

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR F-VISA APPLICATIONS ABROAD:
• Korean Embassy/Consulate: https://overseas.mofa.go.kr
• Visa Portal: https://www.visa.go.kr

FOR ARC & STAY IN KOREA:
• Hi Korea (Immigration Portal): https://www.hikorea.go.kr
• Immigration Contact Center: 1345 (Korean/English)

FOR MARRIAGE REGISTRATION & FAMILY DOCUMENTS:
• Supreme Court Family Registry Service: https://www.familyrelation.go.kr
• Local district offices (구청/시청) for family registry documents

FOR KOREAN LANGUAGE TESTING:
• TOPIK Official Website: https://www.topik.go.kr
• National Institute for International Education (NIIED): https://www.niied.go.kr

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: South Korea Student Visa (D-2)
COUNTRY: South Korea
SOURCE: https://www.hikorea.go.kr | https://www.studyinkorea.go.kr | https://overseas.mofa.go.kr
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• D-2 Visa: For foreigners enrolled in degree programs (Bachelor's, Master's, PhD) at recognized Korean universities
• D-4 Visa: For language training, non-degree courses, exchange programs (≤2 years)
• Sponsored by Korean educational institution; requires Certificate of Admission + Visa Issuance Confirmation
• Mandatory registration with Immigration within 90 days of arrival; Alien Registration Card (ARC) required

=== ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA ===
APPLICANT MUST:
□ Hold passport valid for at least 6 months beyond intended study period
□ Have formal admission offer from recognized Korean educational institution (university, college, language school)
□ Demonstrate sufficient financial resources to cover tuition + living expenses in Korea
□ Meet academic/language requirements for intended program
□ Pass health examination at authorized facility (for long-term stays)
□ Not have adverse immigration history with Korea or other countries

RECOGNIZED INSTITUTIONS:
• Universities accredited by Ministry of Education: https://www.studyinkorea.go.kr
• Language institutes affiliated with accredited universities
• Verify institution recognition before applying; unaccredited institutions cannot issue valid admission documents

QUALIFYING PROGRAMS:
✓ Bachelor's, Master's, PhD degrees at accredited universities
✓ Korean language programs (D-4 visa) at university language centers
✓ Exchange programs, visiting scholar programs with formal institutional agreement
✓ Non-degree research programs with university sponsorship

NON-QUALIFYING PROGRAMS:
✗ Informal/unaccredited language schools or training centers
✗ Online/distance learning programs without physical presence in Korea
✗ Short-term tourism-style "study tours" without formal academic enrollment

=== DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS ===

#### D-2 VISA (DEGREE PROGRAMS)
MANDATORY DOCUMENTS:
□ Completed visa application form (https://www.visa.go.kr)
□ Recent passport-sized photograph (3.5cm x 4.5cm, white background)
□ Original passport (valid 6+ months beyond intended study)
□ Photocopy of passport biodata page
□ Certificate of Admission from Korean institution (original + copy):
  - On official letterhead, signed, with institution seal
  - Details: program name, duration, start/end dates, tuition fees
□ Visa Issuance Confirmation (original + copy):
  - Issued by institution via Hi Korea portal
  - Contains unique confirmation number, student details, program info
□ Proof of financial means:
  - Bank statements (last 6 months) showing sufficient funds (~USD 10,000-20,000 recommended for 1 year)
  - Scholarship award letter, OR
  - Sponsorship letter + sponsor's financial documents + proof of relationship
□ Highest academic degree certificate + transcript (apostilled/legalized if issued outside Korea)
□ Korean language proficiency proof (if required by program): TOPIK score, institutional test results

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (MAY BE REQUESTED):
□ Study plan/personal statement explaining academic goals
□ Letters of recommendation from previous professors/employers
□ Health Examination Certificate (for long-term stays):
  - Conducted at authorized facility; report valid for 3 months
  - Tests: Chest X-ray (TB screening), blood tests, physical exam
□ Criminal Record Check (for certain nationalities/programs)

#### D-4 VISA (LANGUAGE/NON-DEGREE PROGRAMS)
MANDATORY DOCUMENTS:
□ All standard visa documents (application form, passport, photo)
□ Certificate of Admission from language institute/university
□ Visa Issuance Confirmation (if required by institution)
□ Proof of financial means (~USD 8,000-15,000 recommended for 1 year)
□ Proof of prior education (high school diploma minimum)

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (MAY BE REQUESTED):
□ Statement of purpose for language study
□ Proof of Korean language study history (if applicable)
□ Accommodation confirmation in Korea

=== APPLICATION PROCESS ===
STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Secure admission from recognized Korean institution; receive Certificate of Admission + Visa Issuance Confirmation
2. Complete visa application at Korean Embassy/Consulate or authorized center
3. Gather all required documents (originals + photocopies)
4. Submit application + documents
5. Pay visa fee as directed (varies by nationality)
6. Processing time: 5-7 business days standard; expedited options available
7. Upon approval: Collect passport with D-2/D-4 Visa sticker (single-entry, 90-day validity from entry)
8. Enter Korea within visa validity; apply for Alien Registration Card (ARC) within 90 days of entry

OBTAINING ALIEN REGISTRATION CARD (ARC):
• Must apply at local Immigration Office within 90 days of entry
• Required documents:
  - Passport with D-Visa + entry stamp
  - Certificate of Admission + Visa Issuance Confirmation (originals)
  - Proof of address in Korea (dorm confirmation, rental agreement)
  - Recent photo (3.5cm x 4.5cm)
  - Application form + fee (~KRW 30,000)
• Processing: Typically issued same day or within 2-3 weeks
• ARC serves as primary ID in Korea; must carry at all times

PROCESSING TIMES:
• D-Visa application abroad: 5-7 business days standard; 2-3 days express
• ARC application in Korea: Same day to 2-3 weeks depending on office
• Peak seasons (Feb-Mar, Aug-Sep for academic year start): Apply 3-4 weeks in advance

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE, USD/KRW) ===
D-VISA APPLICATION FEES (VARIES BY NATIONALITY):
• USA citizens: ~USD 50-90
• Most other nationalities: USD 30-60 for single-entry

ARC ISSUANCE FEE IN KOREA:
• Application fee: ~KRW 30,000 (~USD 25)
• Re-issuance/renewal: ~KRW 30,000

ADDITIONAL COSTS:
• Document apostille/legalization: USD 20-100 per document
• Health examination (if required): KRW 50,000-150,000 (~USD 40-120)
• TOPIK test fee: ~KRW 40,000 (~USD 30)
• Visa application center service fee (if applicable): ~USD 20-40

NOTE: Fees subject to change; check official portals for current rates.

=== VISA VALIDITY & EXTENSION ===
D-4 VISA (LANGUAGE/NON-DEGREE):
• Initial ARC validity: Typically 6 months to 1 year, matching program length
• Extension: Apply at Immigration Office at least 1 month before expiry
  - Required: Updated admission letter, proof of academic progress, financial support
  - Maximum total stay on D-4: 2 years (must change to D-2 or depart after)

D-2 VISA (DEGREE PROGRAMS):
• Initial ARC validity: Typically 1-2 years, matching program stage
• Extension: Apply at Immigration Office at least 1 month before expiry
  - Required: Updated enrollment certificate, proof of academic progress (transcripts), financial support
  - Extension granted for remaining program duration

POST-COMPLETION STAY:
• After program completion, ARC remains valid until expiry date
• Graduates may apply for:
  - Job-seeking visa (D-10): Up to 2 years to seek employment in Korea
  - Change to Work Visa (E-7) if securing employment meeting criteria
  - Departure before ARC expiry if no further status change

=== WORK RIGHTS FOR STUDENT VISA HOLDERS ===
PART-TIME WORK PERMISSION:
• D-2/D-4 Visa holders ARE PERMITTED to undertake part-time work with prior approval:
  - Must obtain permission from Immigration Office + institution
  - Maximum 20 hours/week during academic term; full-time during vacations
  - Work must not interfere with academic progress
  - Cannot work in entertainment, adult industries, or unauthorized sectors

ON-CAMPUS EMPLOYMENT:
• Many universities offer on-campus jobs (library, research assistant, teaching assistant)
• Typically easier to approve; still requires Immigration + institution consent
• Stipend/salary: Permitted; must comply with Korean labor laws and tax regulations

PROHIBITED ACTIVITIES:
✗ Full-time employment unrelated to studies
✗ Self-employment, freelancing for Korean clients without proper authorization
✗ Work that interferes with academic progress or attendance requirements

=== SCHOLARSHIPS & FUNDING ===
KOREAN GOVERNMENT SCHOLARSHIPS (KGSP/GKS):
• Global Korea Scholarship (GKS): https://www.studyinkorea.go.kr
• Benefits: Tuition waiver, monthly stipend (~KRW 900,000-1,000,000), airfare, language training, medical insurance
• Application: Via Korean Embassy or university; separate from visa application
• Visa Issuance Confirmation issued upon scholarship approval

UNIVERSITY SCHOLARSHIPS:
• Many Korean universities offer merit-based/need-based scholarships to international students
• Check institution website for eligibility, application process, deadlines
• Partial/full tuition waivers common for high-achieving students

PROOF OF FUNDING FOR VISA:
• Scholarship award letter must specify: Amount, duration, coverage (tuition/living/both)
• If self-funded: Bank statements showing sufficient funds (~USD 10,000-20,000/year recommended)
• Sponsorship letters must include sponsor's financial proof + relationship documentation

=== MEDICAL & INSURANCE REQUIREMENTS ===
HEALTH EXAMINATION:
• May be required for long-term D-Visa applications (>1 year)
• Tests: Chest X-ray (TB screening), blood tests (HIV, Hepatitis), physical exam
• Conduct at authorized facility; report valid for 3 months

HEALTH INSURANCE:
• Mandatory for all international students staying >90 days
• Options:
  - National Health Insurance (NHI): ~KRW 60,000-100,000/month (income-based)
  - Private international insurance (must meet Korean minimum coverage standards)
• Coverage should include: Hospitalization, outpatient care, emergency services

VACCINATION REQUIREMENTS:
• Yellow fever vaccination required if arriving from countries with risk of transmission
• Other vaccinations (Hepatitis, Typhoid, etc.) recommended based on region of study in Korea

=== FAMILY DEPENDENTS ===
ELIGIBILITY TO SPONSOR FAMILY:
• D-2 Visa holders (degree programs) generally CANNOT sponsor family members for dependent visas
• Exception: PhD scholars/researchers with long-term funding may apply for spouse/children D-3 Visa on case-by-case basis

DEPENDENT VISA APPLICATION (IF ELIGIBLE):
• Apply via Korean Embassy/Consulate or Immigration Office
• Required documents:
  - Primary holder's Admission Certificate, Visa Confirmation, passport, ARC copy
  - Marriage certificate (spouse) or birth certificate (children), apostilled + translated
  - Proof of additional financial support for dependents
  - Passport copies and photographs of dependents

DEPENDENT RIGHTS:
• Spouse: May study or engage in unpaid activities; cannot work without separate work permission
• Children: Can enroll in international schools in Korea; cannot work without authorization
• All dependents: Must apply for ARC within 90 days of arrival if staying >90 days

=== EXIT & POST-STUDY OPTIONS ===
COMPLETION OF STUDIES:
• After program completion, ARC remains valid until expiry date
• Must depart Korea before ARC expiry OR change status if pursuing further studies/employment

TRANSITION TO WORK VISA:
• If securing job in Korea after studies:
  - Obtain employment offer meeting E-Visa criteria (degree, experience, employer registration)
  - Apply for change of status via Immigration Office
  - Cannot commence employment until E-Visa + ARC is granted

JOB-SEEKING VISA (D-10):
• Available for recent graduates from Korean universities
• Validity: Up to 2 years to seek employment
• Requirements: Degree from Korean institution, clean record, financial proof
• Apply at Immigration Office before student ARC expires

FURTHER STUDIES IN KOREA:
• If pursuing higher degree/research in Korea:
  - Secure admission to new program; receive new Admission Certificate + Visa Confirmation
  - Apply for ARC extension or new ARC via Immigration Office
  - No need to exit Korea if applying before current ARC expiry

=== REFUSAL & REAPPLICATION ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Admission to unrecognized/unaccredited institution
✗ Invalid or missing Visa Issuance Confirmation
✗ Insufficient proof of financial means for study duration
✗ Health examination showing disqualifying conditions
✗ Previous Korean visa violations or adverse immigration history

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• Address specific refusal reason: Secure admission to recognized institution, obtain valid Visa Confirmation, enhance financial documentation
• For health-related refusals: Obtain second medical opinion at authorized facility
• Wait at least 30 days before reapplying unless circumstances changed significantly

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR D-VISA APPLICATIONS ABROAD:
• Korean Embassy/Consulate: https://overseas.mofa.go.kr
• Visa Portal: https://www.visa.go.kr

FOR ARC & STAY IN KOREA:
• Hi Korea (Immigration Portal): https://www.hikorea.go.kr
• Immigration Contact Center: 1345 (Korean/English)

FOR SCHOLARSHIPS:
• National Institute for International Education (NIIED): https://www.studyinkorea.go.kr
• Institution-specific international student offices

FOR INSTITUTION RECOGNITION VERIFICATION:
• Ministry of Education: https://www.moe.go.kr
• Korea University Education Association: https://www.kcuea.or.kr

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: South Korea Tourist/General Visa (C-3-9)
COUNTRY: South Korea
SOURCE: https://www.hikorea.go.kr | https://www.visa.go.kr | https://overseas.mofa.go.kr
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• C-3-9 Visa: For tourism, visiting relatives/friends, short-term business meetings, medical treatment, participation in social/cultural events
• Single-entry, double-entry, or multiple-entry options available
• Validity: 3 months to 5 years (depending on nationality, reciprocity, and applicant profile)
• Duration of stay per entry: Typically 30-90 days (specified on visa sticker)
• Application via Korean Embassy/Consulate or authorized visa application center

=== VISA-EXEMPT NATIONALITIES ===
NATIONALITIES THAT CAN ENTER WITHOUT VISA FOR SHORT VISITS:
• USA, Canada, UK, most EU nations, Australia, New Zealand, Singapore, Japan, etc.: Up to 90 days visa-free
• Many Southeast Asian, Latin American nations: 30-90 days visa-free
• Full official list: https://www.visa.go.kr/openPage/main/MenuItem.do

K-ETA (Korea Electronic Travel Authorization):
• Visa-exempt travelers MUST obtain K-ETA before travel (valid 2 years, multiple entries)
• Apply online: https://www.k-eta.go.kr
• Fee: ~KRW 10,000 (~USD 8)
• Processing: Typically approved within 24 hours

=== ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA (FOR VISA-REQUIRED NATIONALITIES) ===
APPLICANT MUST:
□ Hold passport valid for at least 6 months beyond intended stay
□ Demonstrate genuine tourism/visit purpose with supporting documentation
□ Possess confirmed round-trip flight itinerary and accommodation bookings
□ Have sufficient financial means to cover stay in Korea (~USD 50-100/day recommended)
□ Not have adverse immigration history with Korea or other countries

=== DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS ===
MANDATORY DOCUMENTS:
□ Completed visa application form (https://www.visa.go.kr)
□ Recent passport-sized photograph (3.5cm x 4.5cm, white background, taken within 6 months)
□ Original passport (valid 6+ months, with blank visa pages)
□ Photocopy of passport biodata page
□ Proof of legal residence in country of application (if not citizen): visa, residence permit
□ Round-trip flight itinerary or confirmed booking
□ Hotel reservations or invitation letter from host in Korea:
  - If staying with host: Invitation letter + host's Korean ID/residence permit copy + proof of relationship
□ Proof of financial means: Bank statements (last 3-6 months), employment letter, sponsorship letter

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (MAY BE REQUESTED):
□ Travel itinerary detailing cities/sites to visit
□ For group tours: Confirmation letter from licensed travel agency
□ For medical tourism: Letter from Korean hospital/clinic, treatment plan, cost estimate
□ Previous Korean visas/entry stamps (if applicable)

=== APPLICATION PROCESS ===
STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Complete visa application form (online or paper, depending on Embassy requirements)
2. Gather all required documents (originals + photocopies)
3. Submit application at:
   • Korean Embassy/Consulate in country of residence, OR
   • Authorized visa application center (e.g., VFS Global in some countries)
   • Appointment required at most locations; book via Embassy website
4. Pay visa fee as directed (varies by nationality, entry type)
5. Processing time: 5-7 business days standard; expedited options available (2-3 days)
6. Collect passport with visa sticker or receive via courier if service available

PROCESSING TIMES:
• Standard: 5-7 business days from submission
• Express: 2-3 business days (additional fee)
• Peak seasons (Cherry Blossom season, summer, Chuseok): Apply 2-3 weeks in advance

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE, USD) ===
FEES VARY BY NATIONALITY (RECIPROCITY PRINCIPLE):
• USA citizens: ~USD 50-90 (multiple-entry, 5-10 year validity)
• Most other nationalities: USD 30-60 for single-entry; USD 60-90 for multiple-entry

ADDITIONAL FEES:
• Visa application center service fee: ~USD 20-40 (if applicable)
• Express processing: +USD 20-40
• Courier return service: ~USD 10-25

NOTE: Fees subject to change; check official portal for current rates by nationality.

=== VISA VALIDITY & ENTRY RULES ===
VALIDITY PERIODS:
• Single-entry: Typically 3 months from date of issue
• Double-entry: Typically 6 months from date of issue
• Multiple-entry: 1 year, 3 years, 5 years, or 10 years (based on nationality/reciprocity)

DURATION OF STAY PER ENTRY:
• Typically 30 days for most nationalities
• USA/Canada/EU citizens: Often granted 90 days per entry
• Specified on visa sticker as "Period of Stay: XX days"

ENTRY/EXIT REQUIREMENTS:
• Must enter Korea within visa validity period
• Must exit before "Period of Stay" expires
• Overstaying results in fines, detention, deportation, future entry bans

EXTENSION OF STAY IN KOREA:
• C-3 Visa generally NOT extendable within Korea except in exceptional circumstances (medical emergency, force majeure)
• Apply at local Immigration Office (Hi Korea) at least 7 days before expiry
• Approval discretionary; not for routine tourism extension

=== RESTRICTED AREAS & SPECIAL PERMITS ===
MILITARY DEMARCATION LINE (DMZ) TOURS:
• Foreign tourists can visit DMZ only through authorized tour operators
• Must present passport at checkpoint; no independent travel to restricted zones

JEJU ISLAND VISA-FREE ENTRY:
• Most nationalities can enter Jeju Island visa-free for up to 30 days (even if visa required for mainland Korea)
• Must arrive directly at Jeju International Airport; travel to mainland requires valid Korean visa

=== MEDICAL & CHARACTER REQUIREMENTS ===
MEDICAL EXAMINATION:
• Not required for tourist visa applications under 90 days
• May be requested for longer stays or if applicant has declared medical conditions

TUBERCULOSIS SCREENING:
• May be required for applicants from high-TB-burden countries staying >90 days
• Chest X-ray at authorized facility; report valid for 3 months

CHARACTER/SECURITY SCREENING:
• All applicants subject to security background checks
• Previous travel to sensitive regions, criminal history, or adverse immigration record may trigger additional scrutiny
• Full disclosure required; false statements can result in visa refusal + future ban

=== REFUSAL & REAPPLICATION ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Incomplete application or missing documents
✗ Passport validity <6 months or insufficient blank pages
✗ Insufficient proof of funds, unclear travel purpose, or implausible itinerary
✗ Previous Korean visa violations (overstay, unauthorized work, illegal entry)
✗ Security concerns or adverse background checks

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• Address specific refusal reason with stronger documentation
• Enhance itinerary, financial proof, or invitation letter as needed
• Wait at least 30 days before reapplying unless circumstances changed
• For security-related refusals: May require special clearance from Ministry of Justice

=== OVERSTAY PENALTIES ===
CONSEQUENCES OF OVERSTAYING:
• Fine: KRW 100,000-3,000,000 depending on overstay duration
• Possible detention/deportation for serious/extended overstay
• Entry ban: 1 year to permanent, depending on overstay duration and circumstances
• Future Korean visa applications will face additional scrutiny or refusal

VOLUNTARY DEPARTURE:
• If realize overstay before detection: Report to nearest Immigration Office immediately
• May receive reduced penalty vs. being caught at airport exit
• Still results in immigration record affecting future applications

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR VISA APPLICATIONS:
• Korean Embassy/Consulate: https://overseas.mofa.go.kr
• Hi Korea (Immigration Portal): https://www.hikorea.go.kr
• Visa Portal: https://www.visa.go.kr

FOR K-ETA (VISA-EXEMPT TRAVELERS):
• K-ETA Official Website: https://www.k-eta.go.kr
• Email: k-eta@korea.kr

FOR EXTENSIONS/STAY IN KOREA:
• Local Immigration Office (via Hi Korea portal)
• Immigration Contact Center: 1345 (Korean/English)

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: South Korea Work Visas (E-1 to E-7 Series)
COUNTRY: South Korea
SOURCE: https://www.hikorea.go.kr | https://www.worknet.go.kr | https://overseas.mofa.go.kr
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• E-Series Visas: For foreigners taking up employment in South Korea
• Main categories:
  - E-1: Professor
  - E-2: Foreign Language Instructor
  - E-3: Research
  - E-4: Technology Instruction
  - E-5: Professional Employment
  - E-6: Arts/Entertainment
  - E-7: Specially Designated Activity (most common for corporate professionals)
• Sponsored by Korean employer; requires Certificate of Confirmation of Visa Issuance (CCVI) from Immigration
• Mandatory registration with Immigration within 90 days of arrival; Alien Registration Card (ARC) required

=== ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA ===
APPLICANT MUST:
□ Have valid job offer/employment contract from Korean employer registered to hire foreigners
□ Possess qualifications meeting specific E-category requirements (degree, experience, certifications)
□ Receive minimum salary meeting Immigration guidelines (varies by category/industry)
□ Pass health examination at authorized facility (for certain categories/long-term stays)
□ Have no criminal record in home country or Korea
□ Not be subject to employment restrictions based on nationality/sector

COMMON E-VISA CATEGORIES:

E-2: FOREIGN LANGUAGE INSTRUCTOR
• Eligibility: Bachelor's degree + native-level proficiency in teaching language + clean criminal record
• Typical roles: English teachers (hagwons, public schools), other language instructors
• Salary guideline: ~KRW 2.3-3.0 million/month for public school positions

E-5: PROFESSIONAL EMPLOYMENT
• Eligibility: Bachelor's degree + professional license/certification + relevant experience
• Typical roles: Lawyers, accountants, architects, medical professionals, engineers
• Salary guideline: Varies by profession; must meet industry standards

E-7: SPECIALLY DESIGNATED ACTIVITY (MOST COMMON CORPORATE VISA)
• Eligibility: Bachelor's degree OR 5+ years relevant experience + job offer in approved occupation list
• Occupation list: Over 200 approved roles in IT, engineering, finance, marketing, etc. (check Hi Korea)
• Salary guideline: Must meet minimum salary threshold (typically ~KRW 2.5-3.5 million/month depending on role)
• Employer requirements: Must meet capital/turnover thresholds to sponsor E-7 visas

=== DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS ===
MANDATORY DOCUMENTS:
□ Completed visa application form (https://www.visa.go.kr)
□ Recent passport-sized photograph (3.5cm x 4.5cm, white background)
□ Original passport (valid 6+ months beyond intended stay)
□ Photocopy of passport biodata page
□ Certificate of Confirmation of Visa Issuance (CCVI) (original + copy):
  - Issued by Korean employer via Hi Korea portal
  - Contains unique visa issuance number, employer details, position, validity
□ Employment contract or official invitation letter from Korean employer:
  - On company letterhead, signed, with company seal
  - Details: position, salary, duration, responsibilities, company registration info
□ Educational/Professional Qualifications:
  - Bachelor's degree or higher (or experience for certain categories)
  - Degrees issued outside Korea must be apostilled/legalized + translated to Korean
□ Criminal Record Check:
  - Issued by home country authorities within last 6 months
  - Must be apostilled/legalized for use in Korea

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (MAY BE REQUESTED):
□ Resume/CV detailing work history and qualifications
□ Professional licenses/certifications (if required for role: teaching license, medical license, etc.)
□ Previous Korean work visas/Alien Registration Cards (if applicable)
□ For E-2 teachers: TEFL/TESOL certificate, teaching experience proof, sealed academic transcripts
□ For E-7 professionals: Proof of specialized skills, portfolio, reference letters

=== APPLICATION PROCESS ===
STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Korean employer applies for CCVI via Hi Korea portal (https://www.hikorea.go.kr)
2. Upon approval: Employer sends CCVI number/document to applicant abroad
3. Applicant completes visa application at Korean Embassy/Consulate or authorized center
4. Submit application + documents (originals + photocopies)
5. Pay visa fee as directed (varies by nationality)
6. Processing time: 5-7 business days standard; expedited options available
7. Upon approval: Collect passport with E-Visa sticker (single-entry, 90-day validity from entry)
8. Enter Korea within E-Visa validity; apply for Alien Registration Card (ARC) within 90 days of entry

OBTAINING ALIEN REGISTRATION CARD (ARC):
• Must apply at local Immigration Office within 90 days of entry
• Required documents:
  - Passport with E-Visa + entry stamp
  - CCVI (original)
  - Employment contract
  - Proof of address in Korea (rental agreement, hostel confirmation)
  - Recent photo (3.5cm x 4.5cm)
  - Application form + fee (~KRW 30,000)
• Processing: Typically issued same day or within 2-3 weeks
• ARC serves as primary ID in Korea; must carry at all times

PROCESSING TIMES:
• CCVI application by employer: 2-4 weeks (varies by category/Immigration office workload)
• E-Visa application abroad: 5-7 business days standard; 2-3 days express
• ARC application in Korea: Same day to 2-3 weeks depending on office

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE, USD/KRW) ===
E-VISA APPLICATION FEES (VARIES BY NATIONALITY):
• USA citizens: ~USD 50-90
• Most other nationalities: USD 30-60 for single-entry

ARC ISSUANCE FEE IN KOREA:
• Application fee: ~KRW 30,000 (~USD 25)
• Re-issuance/renewal: ~KRW 30,000

ADDITIONAL COSTS:
• CCVI application (employer-paid): Typically no fee
• Document apostille/legalization: USD 20-100 per document
• Health examination (if required): KRW 50,000-150,000 (~USD 40-120)
• Visa application center service fee (if applicable): ~USD 20-40

NOTE: Fees subject to change; check official portals for current rates.

=== VISA VALIDITY & RENEWAL ===
INITIAL VALIDITY:
• E-Visa sticker: Single-entry, valid for 90 days from date of issue (must enter Korea within this period)
• ARC validity: Typically 1 year initially, matching employment contract duration
• Multiple-entry allowed after ARC issuance; holder can travel in/out of Korea freely during validity

RENEWAL PROCESS:
• Apply at local Immigration Office at least 1 month before ARC expiry
• Required documents:
  - Valid passport and current ARC
  - Renewed employment contract or extension letter from employer
  - Tax payment certificate (proof of income tax compliance)
  - Proof of address in Korea
  - Recent photo + application form + fee
• Processing: Typically 2-4 weeks; applicant may stay in Korea during processing with acknowledgment receipt
• Renewal granted in 1-year increments; long-term employees may receive 2-3 year renewals

=== FAMILY DEPENDENTS ===
ELIGIBILITY TO SPONSOR FAMILY:
• E-Visa/ARC holders CAN sponsor spouse and unmarried children under 20 for F-3 Dependent Visa
• Dependents receive visa validity matching primary holder's ARC

DEPENDENT VISA APPLICATION:
• Apply via Korean Embassy/Consulate (if abroad) or Immigration Office (if in Korea)
• Required documents:
  - Primary holder's ARC copy, passport, employment proof
  - Marriage certificate (spouse) or birth certificate (children), apostilled + translated
  - Proof of financial support from primary holder
  - Passport copies and photographs of dependents

DEPENDENT RIGHTS:
• Spouse: May study or engage in unpaid activities; can apply for part-time work permission (up to 20 hrs/week) with Immigration approval
• Children: Can enroll in Korean/international schools; cannot work without authorization
• All dependents: Must apply for ARC within 90 days of arrival if staying >90 days

=== TAX & COMPLIANCE OBLIGATIONS ===
INCOME TAX:
• Foreign employees are tax residents if stay in Korea exceeds 183 days in a calendar year
• Must obtain Taxpayer Identification Number from National Tax Service
• Employer typically withholds income tax; file annual tax return (May each year)

SOCIAL INSURANCE:
• Foreign employees generally required to contribute to National Pension, Health Insurance, Employment Insurance, Industrial Accident Compensation
• Exemptions possible under bilateral social security agreements (e.g., with USA, Canada, Australia, UK, Germany)
• Contribution rates: ~9% of salary shared between employer/employee

LABOR LAW COMPLIANCE:
• Employment must comply with Korean Labor Standards Act: minimum wage, working hours (max 52 hrs/week), leave entitlements
• Foreign employees have same rights as Korean employees under most labor statutes
• Disputes resolved via local labor committees or courts

=== VISA/ARC CANCELLATION & EXIT PROCEDURES ===
EMPLOYMENT TERMINATION:
• If employment ends before ARC expiry: Employer must report to Immigration within 15 days
• ARC holder has 30-day grace period to:
  - Secure new employment and transfer visa sponsorship, OR
  - Change visa status (e.g., to tourist visa), OR
  - Depart Korea

EXIT CLEARANCE:
• No formal exit visa required for ARC holders
• Ensure tax clearance and pension refund application (if eligible) before departure
• Carry passport with ARC when exiting; may be asked for proof of employment/tax compliance

PENSION REFUND:
• Foreign employees who leave Korea permanently may apply for National Pension refund
• Apply at National Pension Service within 5 years of departure
• Refund amount: Contributions made by employee (employer portion not refundable)

=== REFUSAL & REAPPLICATION ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ CCVI invalid, expired, or from unregistered employer
✗ Qualifications not meeting E-category requirements
✗ Criminal record or adverse immigration history
✗ Incomplete documentation or inconsistent information
✗ Employer fails to meet capital/turnover thresholds for sponsorship

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• Address specific refusal reason: Secure valid CCVI, enhance qualification documentation, clarify employer credentials
• For criminal record issues: Provide rehabilitation evidence, character references
• Wait at least 30 days before reapplying unless circumstances changed significantly

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR E-VISA APPLICATIONS ABROAD:
• Korean Embassy/Consulate: https://overseas.mofa.go.kr
• Visa Portal: https://www.visa.go.kr

FOR CCVI & STAY IN KOREA:
• Hi Korea (Immigration Portal): https://www.hikorea.go.kr
• Immigration Contact Center: 1345 (Korean/English)
• Local Immigration Offices: Listed on Hi Korea portal

FOR TAX & SOCIAL INSURANCE:
• National Tax Service: https://www.nts.go.kr
• National Pension Service: https://www.nps.or.kr
• Health Insurance Corporation: https://www.nhis.or.kr

FOR LABOR DISPUTES:
• Ministry of Employment and Labor: https://www.moel.go.kr
• Labor rights hotline: 1350
