content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: Thailand Family/Retirement Visa (Non-Immigrant O Visa)
COUNTRY: Thailand
SOURCE: https://www.immigration.go.th | https://www.thaiembassy.org
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• Non-Immigrant O Visa: For family reunion, retirement, or other non-business purposes
• Two main subcategories:
  - O-A (Long Stay): For retirement (age 50+); apply at Thai Embassy abroad; 1-year validity
  - O (Family/Other): For spouses/children of Thai citizens or foreign residents; apply at Embassy or in Thailand
• Mandatory registration with Immigration; 90-day reporting required for long-term stays

=== O-A RETIREMENT VISA (FOR APPLICANTS AGE 50+) ===
ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA:
□ Applicant must be aged 50 years or older at time of application
□ Hold passport valid for at least 18 months from date of application
□ Meet ONE of the following financial requirements:
  - Bank deposit in Thailand: THB 800,000 maintained for at least 2 months before application
  - Monthly income/pension: At least THB 65,000 per month (verified by Embassy/consulate)
  - Combination: Bank deposit + annual income totaling at least THB 800,000 per year
□ Have health insurance meeting Thai requirements (or purchase after arrival)
□ Provide criminal record check from home country (authenticated)
□ Pass medical examination at authorized facility (in home country or Thailand)

DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS:
□ Completed visa application form (Embassy-specific)
□ Recent passport-sized photographs (4x6 cm, white background)
□ Original passport (valid 18+ months)
□ Photocopy of passport biodata page
□ Proof of age: Birth certificate or passport copy showing DOB
□ Financial proof (ONE of the following):
  - Thai bank statement showing THB 800,000 deposit (account in applicant's name, held 2+ months)
  - Pension/income verification letter from government/employer (translated to English/Thai)
  - Combination: Bank statement + income letter totaling THB 800,000/year
□ Health insurance policy meeting Thai requirements:
  - Outpatient coverage: At least THB 40,000
  - Inpatient coverage: At least THB 400,000
  - Valid for duration of visa application
□ Criminal record check from home country (issued within 3 months, authenticated)
□ Medical certificate (issued within 3 months):
  - Confirming no prohibited diseases (Leprosy, Tuberculosis, Drug addiction, Elephantiasis, Syphilis 3rd stage)
  - Format: Embassy-specific or Thai Immigration form

APPLICATION PROCESS (ABROAD):
1. Gather all required documents (originals + photocopies)
2. Submit application at Royal Thai Embassy/Consulate in country of residence
3. Pay visa fee: ~USD 80-200 (varies by nationality/Embassy)
4. Processing time: 3-5 business days standard; expedited options available
5. Upon approval: Collect passport with O-A Visa sticker (1-year validity, multiple-entry)
6. Enter Thailand within visa validity; report address to Immigration within 24 hours of arrival
7. Within 90 days of entry: Apply for 1-year extension of stay at Immigration Office (if not already granted 1-year visa)

EXTENSION OF STAY IN THAILAND:
• O-A Visa typically grants 1-year permission to stay upon entry; no extension needed initially
• For annual renewal: Apply at Immigration Office at least 30 days before current permission expires
• Required documents:
  - Passport with O-A Visa + entry stamp
  - Updated financial proof (bank statement showing THB 800,000 maintained, or income verification)
  - Updated health insurance policy
  - TM.7 extension form + fee (~THB 1,900)
  - Recent photo (4x6 cm)
• Processing: Typically 7-15 business days; applicant may stay in Thailand during processing

=== O VISA FOR FAMILY REUNION (SPOUSE/CHILDREN OF THAI CITIZENS) ===
ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA:
□ Applicant is legally married to Thai citizen (marriage registered in Thailand) OR is child under 20 of Thai citizen
□ Thai spouse/parent must meet minimum income/financial requirements to support applicant
□ Marriage must be genuine (not for immigration purposes)
□ Applicant must pass basic Thai language/culture interview (at Embassy or Immigration, if required)

DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS:
□ Completed visa application form
□ Recent passport-sized photograph (4x6 cm, white background)
□ Original passport (valid 6+ months)
□ Photocopy of passport biodata page
□ Marriage certificate (for spouse applicants):
  - Registered in Thailand (Kor Ror 2/Kor Ror 3) OR apostilled + Thai translation if married abroad
□ Thai spouse's documents:
  - Thai ID card copy, household registration (Tabien Baan) copy
  - Financial proof: Income certificate (showing annual income ≥ ~THB 40,000) OR bank statement (THB 400,000)
□ For child applicants: Birth certificate showing Thai parentage (apostilled + Thai translation)
□ Proof of accommodation in Thailand: Property deed or rental agreement in Thai spouse's name
□ Criminal record check from applicant's home country (apostilled, if required by Embassy)

APPLICATION PROCESS:
STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Register marriage in Thailand (if married abroad): Submit apostilled marriage certificate to Thai Embassy for registration
2. Thai spouse prepares financial/accommodation documentation
3. Applicant completes visa application at Thai Embassy/Consulate
4. Attend interview (if required) to assess relationship genuineness and basic Thai knowledge
5. Submit application + documents
6. Pay visa fee: ~USD 80 (single-entry) / ~USD 200 (multiple-entry)
7. Processing: 3-5 business days standard; longer if additional verification required
8. Upon approval: Collect passport with O Visa sticker (single-entry, 90-day validity from entry)
9. Enter Thailand; apply for 1-year extension at Immigration Office within 90 days

OBTAINING 1-YEAR EXTENSION (IN THAILAND):
• Apply at local Immigration Office within 90 days of entry
• Required documents:
  - Passport with O Visa + entry stamp
  - Marriage certificate (original + copy) + Thai spouse's ID/household registration
  - Financial proof (Thai spouse's income certificate or bank statement)
  - Proof of address in Thailand (rental agreement, property deed)
  - Recent photo (4x6 cm)
  - TM.7 extension form + fee (~THB 1,900)
• Processing: Typically 7-15 business days; applicant may stay in Thailand during processing
• Extension granted: 1 year, renewable annually based on continued marriage/financial support

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE, USD/THB) ===
O-A RETIREMENT VISA FEES (VARIES BY NATIONALITY/EMBASSY):
• Application fee: ~USD 80-200 (~THB 2,800-7,000)

O FAMILY VISA FEES:
• Single-Entry: ~USD 80 (~THB 2,800)
• Multiple-Entry (1-year): ~USD 200 (~THB 7,000)

EXTENSION FEES IN THAILAND:
• Extension fee: ~THB 1,900 per year
• Multiple re-entry permit (optional): ~THB 1,000 (single) / ~THB 3,800 (multiple)

ADDITIONAL COSTS:
• Document apostille/legalization: USD 20-100 per document
• Thai translation of documents: ~THB 300-1,000 per page
• Health insurance for O-A: ~THB 15,000-50,000/year depending on age/coverage
• Medical examination: ~THB 1,000-3,000
• Criminal record check authentication: USD 20-50

NOTE: Fees subject to change; check official portals for current rates.

=== RIGHTS & RESTRICTIONS ===
O-A RETIREMENT VISA HOLDERS:
✓ Can stay in Thailand for 1 year (renewable annually)
✓ Multiple-entry allowed; can travel in/out of Thailand freely
✓ CANNOT work in Thailand without separate Work Permit
✓ Can open Thai bank account, obtain driver's license, access healthcare
✓ Must maintain financial/insurance requirements for annual renewal

O FAMILY VISA HOLDERS:
✓ Can stay in Thailand for 1 year (renewable annually based on continued relationship)
✓ Multiple-entry allowed after extension
✓ CANNOT work in Thailand without separate Work Permit
✓ Can study in Thailand without additional student visa
✓ Must maintain relationship/financial support for annual renewal

PROHIBITED ACTIVITIES FOR BOTH:
✗ Engaging in employment or business activities without proper Work Permit
✗ Overstaying visa/extension validity
✗ Providing false information in application/renewal

=== 90-DAY REPORTING REQUIREMENT ===
MANDATORY FOR ALL LONG-TERM STAYS (>90 DAYS):
• Report current address to Immigration every 90 days
• Methods:
  - In-person at Immigration Office
  - By mail (with return envelope)
  - Online via Immigration website (https://www.immigration.go.th) – when available
• Fee: Free if done on time; THB 1,000 fine if late
• First report: Within 90 days of entry or extension approval
• Subsequent reports: Every 90 days from last report date

CONSEQUENCES OF NON-COMPLIANCE:
• Fine: THB 1,000 for late reporting
• Difficulty renewing visa/extension
• Potential impact on future Thai visa applications

=== PATHWAY TO PERMANENT RESIDENCE ===
ELIGIBILITY FOR THAI PERMANENT RESIDENCE:
□ Hold Non-Immigrant Visa (O-A or O) for at least 3 consecutive years
□ Meet financial requirements: Annual income ≥ THB 80,000 OR bank deposit ≥ THB 3 million
□ Pass Thai language test (basic speaking/reading) OR complete government integration program
□ No criminal record in Thailand or home country
□ Demonstrate good character/contribution to Thai society

APPLICATION PROCESS:
• Apply at Immigration Bureau in Bangkok (only office processing PR applications)
• Required documents: Extensive documentation including financial proof, language test results, police clearance, references
• Processing: 6-12 months or longer
• If approved: Receive Permanent Residence Certificate; no need for annual visa extensions

BENEFITS OF PERMANENT RESIDENCE:
✓ No need to renew visa annually
✓ Full work rights (no separate Work Permit needed)
✓ Pathway to Thai citizenship after additional 5 years of residence
✓ Access to all public services, property ownership rights (with restrictions)

=== REFUSAL & REAPPLICATION ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Financial requirements not met (insufficient bank balance, unverified income)
✗ Health insurance not meeting Thai requirements (for O-A)
✗ Inability to prove genuine marriage/relationship (for O Family)
✗ Criminal record or adverse immigration history
✗ Failed Thai language/culture assessment (for O Family, if required)

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• Address specific refusal reason: Enhance financial documentation, obtain compliant health insurance, strengthen relationship proof
• For relationship verification issues: Provide additional evidence (photos, communication records, joint financial documents)
• Wait at least 30 days before reapplying unless circumstances changed significantly

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR O-A/O VISA APPLICATIONS ABROAD:
• Royal Thai Embassy/Consulate: https://www.thaiembassy.org
• Thai e-Visa Portal: https://www.thaievisa.go.th

FOR EXTENSIONS & STAY IN THAILAND:
• Immigration Bureau: https://www.immigration.go.th
• Local Immigration Offices: Listed on Immigration Bureau website
• Immigration Hotline: 1178 (Thai/English)

FOR HEALTH INSURANCE REQUIREMENTS:
• Office of Insurance Commission: https://www.oic.or.th
• Approved insurers list: Available via Immigration Bureau website

FOR PERMANENT RESIDENCE APPLICATIONS:
• Immigration Bureau, Permanent Residence Division: https://www.immigration.go.th
• Application only accepted at Immigration Bureau Headquarters, Bangkok

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: Thailand Student Visa (Non-Immigrant ED Visa)
COUNTRY: Thailand
SOURCE: https://www.immigration.go.th | https://www.mhesi.go.th | https://www.thaiembassy.org
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• Non-Immigrant ED Visa: For foreigners enrolled in recognized educational programs in Thailand
• Covers: University degrees, language courses, vocational training, Muay Thai schools, Buddhist studies, etc.
• Single-entry (90-day stay) or Multiple-entry (1-year validity) options
• Sponsored by Thai educational institution; requires acceptance letter + institution documentation
• Mandatory registration with Immigration; 90-day reporting required for long-term stays

=== ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA ===
APPLICANT MUST:
□ Hold passport valid for at least 6 months beyond intended study period
□ Have formal admission offer from recognized Thai educational institution
□ Demonstrate sufficient financial resources to cover tuition + living expenses in Thailand
□ Meet academic/language requirements for intended program
□ Pass health examination at authorized facility (for long-term stays)
□ Not have adverse immigration history with Thailand or other countries

RECOGNIZED INSTITUTIONS:
• Universities accredited by Ministry of Higher Education, Science, Research and Innovation (MHESI): https://www.mhesi.go.th
• Language schools licensed by Ministry of Education
• Vocational training centers approved by relevant authorities
• Muay Thai schools, Buddhist temples with educational programs (must be licensed)
• Verify institution recognition before applying; unlicensed institutions cannot issue valid ED visa support

QUALIFYING PROGRAMS:
✓ Bachelor's, Master's, PhD degrees at accredited universities
✓ Thai language programs (semester/year-long) at university language centers or licensed schools
✓ Vocational training programs (cooking, massage, hospitality, etc.) at licensed institutions
✓ Muay Thai training programs at licensed camps/schools
✓ Buddhist studies/meditation programs at licensed temples
✓ Exchange programs, visiting scholar programs with formal institutional agreement

NON-QUALIFYING PROGRAMS:
✗ Informal/unlicensed language schools or training centers
✗ Online/distance learning programs without physical presence in Thailand
✗ Short-term tourism-style "study tours" without formal academic enrollment

=== DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS ===

#### NON-IMMIGRANT ED VISA APPLICATION (ABROAD)
MANDATORY DOCUMENTS:
□ Completed visa application form (https://www.thaievisa.go.th or Embassy form)
□ Recent passport-sized photograph (4x6 cm, white background)
□ Original passport (valid 6+ months beyond intended study)
□ Photocopy of passport biodata page
□ Acceptance Letter from Thai institution (original + copy):
  - On official letterhead, signed, with institution seal
  - Details: program name, duration, start/end dates, tuition fees
□ Institution Documentation:
  - Copy of institution's license/registration from Ministry of Education/MHESI
  - Letter confirming institution's authorization to enroll foreign students
□ Proof of financial means:
  - Bank statements (last 3-6 months) showing sufficient funds (~THB 20,000-50,000 recommended for 1 year)
  - Scholarship award letter, OR
  - Sponsorship letter + sponsor's financial documents + proof of relationship
□ Educational qualifications: Highest degree certificate + transcript (authenticated if issued outside Thailand)
□ For language programs: Proof of prior language study or placement test results (if required)

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (MAY BE REQUESTED):
□ Study plan/personal statement explaining educational goals
□ Letters of recommendation from previous professors/employers
□ Health Examination Certificate (for long-term stays):
  - Conducted at authorized facility; report valid for 3 months
  - Tests: Chest X-ray (TB screening), blood tests, physical exam
□ Criminal Record Check (for certain nationalities/programs)
□ For minors (<18): Parental consent letter, guardian details in Thailand

=== APPLICATION PROCESS ===
STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Secure admission from recognized Thai institution; receive Acceptance Letter + institution documentation
2. Complete visa application at Thai Embassy/Consulate or authorized center
3. Gather all required documents (originals + photocopies)
4. Submit application + documents
5. Pay visa fee as directed:
   - Single-Entry ED Visa: ~USD 80 (~THB 2,800)
   - Multiple-Entry ED Visa (1-year): ~USD 200 (~THB 7,000)
6. Processing time: 3-5 business days standard; expedited options available
7. Upon approval: Collect passport with ED Visa sticker (single-entry, 90-day validity from entry)
8. Enter Thailand within ED Visa validity; report address to Immigration within 24 hours of arrival
9. For programs >90 days: Apply for visa extension at Immigration Office before initial 90 days expire

VISA EXTENSION PROCESS (IN THAILAND):
• Apply at local Immigration Office at least 30 days before initial 90-day stay expires
• Required documents:
  - Passport with ED Visa + entry stamp
  - Acceptance Letter + institution documentation (originals + copies)
  - Proof of tuition payment for extension period
  - Proof of address in Thailand (rental agreement, dorm confirmation)
  - Recent photo (4x6 cm)
  - TM.7 extension form + fee (~THB 1,900 per 90-day extension)
• Processing: Typically 7-15 business days; applicant may stay in Thailand during processing with acknowledgment receipt
• Extensions granted in 90-day increments up to program duration

PROCESSING TIMES:
• ED Visa application abroad: 3-5 business days standard; 1-2 days express
• Visa extension in Thailand: 7-15 business days
• Peak seasons (May-Jun, Nov-Dec for academic terms): Apply 2-3 weeks in advance

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE, USD/THB) ===
ED VISA APPLICATION FEES (VARIES BY NATIONALITY/EMBASSY):
• Single-Entry: ~USD 80 (~THB 2,800)
• Multiple-Entry (1-year): ~USD 200 (~THB 7,000)

VISA EXTENSION FEES IN THAILAND:
• Extension fee: ~THB 1,900 per 90-day extension
• Multiple re-entry permit (optional): ~THB 1,000 (single) / ~THB 3,800 (multiple)

ADDITIONAL COSTS:
• Document authentication/legalization: USD 20-100 per document
• Health examination (if required): ~THB 1,000-3,000
• Tuition fees: Varies widely by institution/program (THB 20,000-200,000+/year)
• Visa application center service fee (if applicable): ~USD 20-40

NOTE: Fees subject to change; check official portals for current rates.

=== VISA VALIDITY & EXTENSION ===
INITIAL VALIDITY:
• ED Visa sticker: Single-entry, valid for 90 days from date of issue (must enter Thailand within this period)
• Initial stay: 90 days from entry date
• Extensions: Granted in 90-day increments up to program duration

EXTENSION PROCESS:
• Apply at local Immigration Office at least 30 days before current permission to stay expires
• Required documents (as above) + proof of academic progress (attendance records, test results)
• Institution must provide updated acceptance letter for each extension period
• Processing: 7-15 business days; applicant may stay in Thailand during processing

MAXIMUM STAY:
• Language programs: Typically up to 1 year total (multiple 90-day extensions)
• Degree programs: Up to program duration (e.g., 4 years for Bachelor's)
• Must maintain full-time enrollment and satisfactory academic progress

=== WORK RIGHTS FOR ED VISA HOLDERS ===
PART-TIME WORK PERMISSION:
• ED Visa holders ARE PERMITTED to undertake part-time work with prior approval:
  - Must obtain permission from Immigration Office + educational institution
  - Maximum 20 hours/week during academic term; full-time during vacations
  - Work must not interfere with academic progress
  - Cannot work in prohibited occupations (see Work Visa file)

ON-CAMPUS EMPLOYMENT:
• Many universities offer on-campus jobs (library, research assistant, teaching assistant)
• Typically easier to approve; still requires Immigration + institution consent
• Stipend/salary: Permitted; must comply with Thai labor laws and tax regulations

PROHIBITED ACTIVITIES:
✗ Full-time employment unrelated to studies
✗ Self-employment, freelancing for Thai clients without proper authorization
✗ Work that interferes with academic progress or attendance requirements

=== SCHOLARSHIPS & FUNDING ===
THAI GOVERNMENT SCHOLARSHIPS:
• Royal Thai Government Scholarship: https://www.mhesi.go.th
• Benefits: Tuition waiver, monthly stipend, accommodation, medical insurance, airfare
• Application: Via Thai Embassy or university; separate from visa application
• Acceptance Letter issued upon scholarship approval

UNIVERSITY SCHOLARSHIPS:
• Many Thai universities offer merit-based/need-based scholarships to international students
• Check institution website for eligibility, application process, deadlines
• Partial/full tuition waivers common for high-achieving students

PROOF OF FUNDING FOR VISA:
• Scholarship award letter must specify: Amount, duration, coverage (tuition/living/both)
• If self-funded: Bank statements showing sufficient funds (~THB 20,000-50,000/year recommended)
• Sponsorship letters must include sponsor's financial proof + relationship documentation

=== MEDICAL & INSURANCE REQUIREMENTS ===
HEALTH EXAMINATION:
• May be required for long-term ED Visa applications (>1 year)
• Tests: Chest X-ray (TB screening), blood tests (HIV, Hepatitis), physical exam
• Conduct at authorized facility; report valid for 3 months

HEALTH INSURANCE:
• Strongly recommended for all international students staying >90 days
• Some institutions require proof of health insurance as condition of enrollment
• Coverage should include: Hospitalization, outpatient care, emergency evacuation, repatriation
• Options: Thai insurance providers (Bangkok Hospital, Bumrungrad) or international plans with Thailand coverage

VACCINATION REQUIREMENTS:
• Yellow fever vaccination required if arriving from countries with risk of transmission
• Other vaccinations (Hepatitis, Typhoid, etc.) recommended based on region of study in Thailand

=== FAMILY DEPENDENTS ===
ELIGIBILITY TO SPONSOR FAMILY:
• ED Visa holders generally CANNOT sponsor family members for dependent visas
• Exception: University students in degree programs may apply for spouse/children Non-Immigrant O Visa on case-by-case basis

DEPENDENT VISA APPLICATION (IF ELIGIBLE):
• Apply via Thai Embassy/Consulate or Immigration Office
• Required documents:
  - Primary holder's Acceptance Letter, passport, visa/extension proof
  - Marriage certificate (spouse) or birth certificate (children), authenticated + translated
  - Proof of additional financial support for dependents
  - Passport copies and photographs of dependents

DEPENDENT RIGHTS:
• Spouse: May study or engage in unpaid activities; cannot work without separate Work Permit
• Children: Can enroll in international schools in Thailand; cannot work without authorization
• All dependents: Must report address to Immigration within 24 hours of arrival; 90-day reporting if staying >90 days

=== EXIT & POST-STUDY OPTIONS ===
COMPLETION OF STUDIES:
• After program completion, ED Visa/extensions remain valid until expiry date
• Must depart Thailand before visa expiry OR change status if pursuing further studies/employment

TRANSITION TO WORK VISA:
• If securing job in Thailand after studies:
  - Obtain employment offer meeting Non-Immigrant B Visa criteria (degree, experience, employer registration)
  - Apply for change of status via Immigration Office + Department of Employment
  - Cannot commence employment until B Visa + Work Permit is granted

FURTHER STUDIES IN THAILAND:
• If pursuing higher degree/research in Thailand:
  - Secure admission to new program; receive new Acceptance Letter + institution documentation
  - Apply for ED Visa extension or new visa via Immigration Office
  - No need to exit Thailand if applying before current visa expiry

=== REFUSAL & REAPPLICATION ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Admission to unrecognized/unlicensed institution
✗ Invalid or missing Acceptance Letter/institution documentation
✗ Insufficient proof of financial means for study duration
✗ Health examination showing disqualifying conditions
✗ Previous Thai visa violations or adverse immigration history

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• Address specific refusal reason: Secure admission to recognized institution, obtain valid Acceptance Letter, enhance financial documentation
• For health-related refusals: Obtain second medical opinion at authorized facility
• Wait at least 30 days before reapplying unless circumstances changed significantly

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR ED VISA APPLICATIONS ABROAD:
• Royal Thai Embassy/Consulate: https://www.thaiembassy.org
• Thai e-Visa Portal: https://www.thaievisa.go.th

FOR VISA EXTENSIONS & STAY IN THAILAND:
• Immigration Bureau: https://www.immigration.go.th
• Local Immigration Offices: Listed on Immigration Bureau website
• Immigration Hotline: 1178 (Thai/English)

FOR SCHOLARSHIPS:
• Ministry of Higher Education, Science, Research and Innovation (MHESI): https://www.mhesi.go.th
• Institution-specific international student offices

FOR INSTITUTION RECOGNITION VERIFICATION:
• Ministry of Education: https://www.moe.go.th
• Office of the Higher Education Commission: https://www.mhesi.go.th

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: Thailand Tourist Entry (Visa Exemption, Visa on Arrival, Tourist Visa TR)
COUNTRY: Thailand
SOURCE: https://www.immigration.go.th | https://www.thaiembassy.org | https://www.consular.go.th
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
Thailand offers three main entry options for tourists:

1. VISA EXEMPTION: For nationals of 64+ countries; enter without visa for 30 days (air) or 15 days (land)
2. VISA ON ARRIVAL (VOA): For nationals of 19 countries; obtain 15-day visa at Thai airport/land border
3. TOURIST VISA (TR): Single-entry (60 days) or Multiple-entry (6 months validity); apply at Thai Embassy/Consulate

=== VISA EXEMPTION (MOST COMMON FOR TOURISTS) ===
ELIGIBLE NATIONALITIES (64+ COUNTRIES):
• USA, Canada, UK, most EU nations, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, etc.
• Full official list: https://www.consular.go.th/main/th/services/104849-Visa-Exemption.html

ENTRY CONDITIONS:
□ Passport valid for at least 6 months from date of entry
□ Confirmed onward/return ticket within 30 days (air) or 15 days (land)
□ Proof of accommodation in Thailand (hotel booking, host letter)
□ Proof of sufficient funds: THB 10,000 per person or THB 20,000 per family (may be requested)
□ No adverse immigration history with Thailand

DURATION OF STAY:
• Air entry: 30 days (non-extendable for most nationalities; some can extend once for 30 days)
• Land entry: 15 days (non-extendable)
• Extension possible at Immigration Office: +30 days for ~THB 1,900 (subject to approval)

=== VISA ON ARRIVAL (VOA) ===
ELIGIBLE NATIONALITIES (19 COUNTRIES):
• China, India, Saudi Arabia, Ukraine, Bulgaria, Taiwan, etc.
• Full list: https://www.immigration.go.th/en/page/visa-on-arrival

REQUIREMENTS:
□ Passport valid for at least 30 days from date of entry
□ Confirmed onward/return ticket within 15 days
□ Proof of accommodation in Thailand
□ Proof of sufficient funds: THB 10,000 per person or THB 20,000 per family
□ Completed VOA application form + recent photo (4x6 cm)
□ VOA fee: THB 2,000 (cash only, Thai Baht)

PROCESS AT AIRPORT:
• Proceed to "Visa on Arrival" counter before immigration
• Submit documents + fee; receive VOA sticker in passport
• Proceed to immigration for entry stamp (15-day stay)

=== TOURIST VISA (TR) - SINGLE OR MULTIPLE ENTRY ===
FOR NATIONALITIES NOT ELIGIBLE FOR VISA EXEMPTION/VOA OR NEEDING LONGER STAY:
• Single-Entry Tourist Visa (SETV): Valid 3 months from issue; 60-day stay per entry; extendable +30 days
• Multiple-Entry Tourist Visa (METV): Valid 6 months from issue; 60-day stay per entry; extendable +30 days per entry

ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA:
□ Hold passport valid for at least 6 months beyond intended stay
□ Demonstrate genuine tourism purpose with supporting documentation
□ Possess confirmed round-trip flight itinerary and accommodation bookings
□ Have sufficient financial means (~THB 20,000 per person or THB 40,000 per family recommended)

DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS:
□ Completed visa application form (https://www.thaievisa.go.th or Embassy form)
□ Recent passport-sized photograph (4x6 cm, white background, taken within 6 months)
□ Original passport (valid 6+ months, with blank visa pages)
□ Photocopy of passport biodata page
□ Proof of legal residence in country of application (if not citizen)
□ Round-trip flight itinerary or confirmed booking
□ Hotel reservations or invitation letter from host in Thailand
□ Bank statements (last 3-6 months) showing sufficient funds
□ For METV: Additional proof of travel history, multiple destination bookings

APPLICATION PROCESS:
STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Complete visa application form (online via https://www.thaievisa.go.th or paper at Embassy)
2. Gather all required documents (originals + photocopies)
3. Submit application at:
   • Royal Thai Embassy/Consulate in country of residence, OR
   • Authorized visa application center (e.g., VFS Global in some countries)
   • Appointment required at most locations; book via Embassy website
4. Pay visa fee as directed:
   - SETV: ~USD 30-40
   - METV: ~USD 150-200
5. Processing time: 3-5 business days standard; expedited options available (1-2 days)
6. Collect passport with visa sticker or receive via courier if service available

PROCESSING TIMES:
• Standard: 3-5 business days from submission
• Express: 1-2 business days (additional fee)
• Peak seasons (Nov-Feb, Apr): Apply 1-2 weeks in advance

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE, USD/THB) ===
TOURIST VISA FEES (VARIES BY NATIONALITY/EMBASSY):
• Single-Entry (SETV): ~USD 30-40 (~THB 1,000-1,400)
• Multiple-Entry (METV): ~USD 150-200 (~THB 5,000-7,000)

ADDITIONAL FEES:
• Visa application center service fee: ~USD 20-40 (if applicable)
• Express processing: +USD 20-40
• Courier return service: ~USD 10-25
• Visa on Arrival fee: THB 2,000 (cash only)

NOTE: Fees subject to change; check official portal for current rates by nationality.

=== EXTENSION OF STAY ===
VISA EXEMPTION/VOA EXTENSION:
• Apply at local Immigration Office at least 7 days before expiry
• Fee: THB 1,900
• Extension granted: +30 days for visa exemption holders (subject to approval); VOA generally not extendable
• Required: Passport, TM.7 extension form, photo, proof of onward travel, accommodation proof

TOURIST VISA EXTENSION:
• Apply at local Immigration Office at least 7 days before 60-day stay expires
• Fee: THB 1,900
• Extension granted: +30 days (subject to approval)
• Required: Passport with TR visa, TM.7 form, photo, proof of funds, accommodation proof

=== RESTRICTED AREAS & SPECIAL PERMITS ===
SOUTHERN BORDER PROVINCES:
• Certain areas in Yala, Pattani, Narathiwat, Songkhla may have travel restrictions
• Check with local authorities or Embassy before travel

NATIONAL PARKS/PROTECTED AREAS:
• Some parks require additional permits/fees for foreign visitors
• Check with Department of National Parks: https://www.dnp.go.th

=== MEDICAL & CHARACTER REQUIREMENTS ===
MEDICAL EXAMINATION:
• Not required for tourist entries under 90 days
• May be requested for longer stays or if applicant has declared medical conditions

YELLOW FEVER VACCINATION:
• Required if arriving from countries with risk of yellow fever transmission
• Must present International Certificate of Vaccination (ICVP) at entry

CHARACTER/SECURITY SCREENING:
• All entrants subject to immigration screening
• Previous overstays, deportations, or criminal history may trigger additional scrutiny
• Full disclosure required; false statements can result in entry denial + future ban

=== OVERSTAY PENALTIES ===
CONSEQUENCES OF OVERSTAYING:
• Fine: THB 500 per day of overstay (max THB 20,000)
• Detention/deportation for overstays >90 days
• Entry ban: 1 year (overstay <90 days), 3 years (90 days-1 year), 5 years (1-3 years), 10 years (3+ years), permanent (5+ years)
• Future Thai visa applications will face additional scrutiny or refusal

VOLUNTARY DEPARTURE:
• If realize overstay before detection: Report to nearest Immigration Office immediately
• Pay overstay fine at airport immigration when departing
• Still results in immigration record affecting future applications

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR VISA APPLICATIONS:
• Royal Thai Embassy/Consulate: https://www.thaiembassy.org
• Thai e-Visa Portal: https://www.thaievisa.go.th
• Ministry of Foreign Affairs: https://www.mfa.go.th

FOR EXTENSIONS/STAY IN THAILAND:
• Immigration Bureau: https://www.immigration.go.th
• Local Immigration Offices: Listed on Immigration Bureau website
• Immigration Hotline: 1178 (Thai/English)

FOR VISA ON ARRIVAL:
• Suvarnabhumi Airport Immigration: +66 2 134 0490
• Don Mueang Airport Immigration: +66 2 535 1181

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: Thailand Work Visa (Non-Immigrant B Visa + Work Permit)
COUNTRY: Thailand
SOURCE: https://www.immigration.go.th | https://www.doe.go.th | https://www.thaiembassy.org
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• Non-Immigrant B Visa: For foreigners taking up employment or business activities in Thailand
• Single-entry (90-day stay) or Multiple-entry (1-year validity) options
• MUST obtain Work Permit from Department of Employment (DOE) within 90 days of entry
• Sponsored by Thai employer; employer must meet capital/foreign employee ratio requirements
• Mandatory registration with Immigration; 90-day reporting required for long-term stays

=== ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA ===
APPLICANT MUST:
□ Have valid job offer/employment contract from Thai company registered to hire foreigners
□ Possess qualifications meeting job requirements (degree, experience, certifications)
□ Receive minimum salary meeting Immigration/DOE guidelines (varies by nationality/industry)
□ Pass health examination at authorized facility (for Work Permit application)
□ Have no criminal record in home country or Thailand
□ Not be on prohibited occupation list for foreigners (see below)

PROHIBITED OCCUPATIONS FOR FOREIGNERS:
✗ Manual labor, agriculture, animal husbandry, forestry, fishery
✗ Wood carving, driving vehicles/machinery (except international pilots)
✗ Auctioneering, hairdressing, weaving, handicrafts
✗ Tourist guiding, street vending, certain retail roles
• Full list: Ministerial Regulation on Prohibited Occupations for Aliens B.E. 2522

QUALIFYING EMPLOYMENT CATEGORIES:
✓ Executives, managers, technical experts assigned to Thai entity
✓ Teachers/faculty at recognized Thai educational institutions
✓ Skilled professionals in IT, engineering, finance, healthcare, education
✓ Specialists with expertise not readily available in Thai labor market
✓ Employees of BOI-promoted companies (Board of Investment incentives)

=== DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS ===

#### NON-IMMIGRANT B VISA APPLICATION (ABROAD)
MANDATORY DOCUMENTS:
□ Completed visa application form (https://www.thaievisa.go.th or Embassy form)
□ Recent passport-sized photograph (4x6 cm, white background)
□ Original passport (valid 6+ months beyond intended stay)
□ Photocopy of passport biodata page
□ Letter of approval from Thai Ministry of Labour OR Work Permit application support letter from employer:
  - On company letterhead, signed, with company seal
  - Details: position, salary, duration, responsibilities, company registration info
□ Employment contract or official invitation letter from Thai employer
□ Educational/Professional Qualifications:
  - Bachelor's degree or higher (or relevant experience for certain roles)
  - Degrees issued outside Thailand must be authenticated/legalized + translated to Thai
□ Company documents from Thai employer:
  - Company affidavit, business registration, VAT registration, shareholder list
  - Financial statements, tax payment receipts, employee list showing foreign/Thai ratio

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (MAY BE REQUESTED):
□ Resume/CV detailing work history and qualifications
□ Professional licenses/certifications (if required for role: teaching license, medical license, etc.)
□ Previous Thai work visas/Work Permits (if applicable)
□ For teachers: TEFL/TESOL certificate, teaching experience proof, criminal record check

#### WORK PERMIT APPLICATION (IN THAILAND)
MANDATORY DOCUMENTS (SUBMITTED BY EMPLOYER TO DOE):
□ Non-Immigrant B Visa + entry stamp in passport
□ Employment contract signed by employer and employee
□ Company documents (as above) + proof of capital registration
□ Employee qualifications (degree, transcripts, certifications) authenticated + translated
□ Medical certificate from Thai hospital (within 30 days of application)
□ Criminal record check from home country (authenticated)
□ Recent photos of employee (4x6 cm)
□ Work Permit application forms (WP.1, WP.2, etc.)

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS (MAY BE REQUESTED):
□ Proof of office location (lease, photos)
□ Organizational chart showing employee's role
□ Justification letter explaining why foreign expertise is needed

=== APPLICATION PROCESS ===
STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Thai employer prepares Work Permit support documents + applies for Ministry of Labour approval (if required)
2. Applicant applies for Non-Immigrant B Visa at Thai Embassy/Consulate abroad
3. Submit visa application + documents; pay fee (~USD 80-200 depending on entry type)
4. Processing: 3-5 business days standard; expedited options available
5. Upon approval: Collect passport with B Visa sticker (single-entry, 90-day validity from entry)
6. Enter Thailand within B Visa validity
7. Employer submits Work Permit application to Department of Employment within 90 days of entry
8. Upon Work Permit approval: Employee receives Work Permit book/card
9. Employee must report address to Immigration within 24 hours of arrival; 90-day reporting thereafter

PROCESSING TIMES:
• B Visa application abroad: 3-5 business days standard; 1-2 days express
• Work Permit application in Thailand: 7-15 business days standard; expedited options available
• Ministry of Labour approval (if required): 2-4 weeks

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE, USD/THB) ===
NON-IMMIGRANT B VISA FEES (VARIES BY NATIONALITY/EMBASSY):
• Single-Entry: ~USD 80 (~THB 2,800)
• Multiple-Entry (1-year): ~USD 200 (~THB 7,000)

WORK PERMIT FEES IN THAILAND:
• Application fee: ~THB 3,000-5,000 depending on duration (3 months to 1 year)
• Re-issuance/renewal: Similar fees

ADDITIONAL COSTS:
• Document authentication/legalization: USD 20-100 per document
• Medical examination in Thailand: ~THB 1,000-3,000
• Criminal record check authentication: USD 20-50
• Visa application center service fee (if applicable): ~USD 20-40

NOTE: Fees subject to change; check official portals for current rates.

=== WORK PERMIT VALIDITY & RENEWAL ===
INITIAL VALIDITY:
• Typically 3 months to 1 year, matching employment contract duration
• Multiple-entry allowed after Work Permit issuance; holder can travel in/out of Thailand freely

RENEWAL PROCESS:
• Apply at Department of Employment at least 30 days before expiry
• Required documents:
  - Valid passport and current Work Permit
  - Renewed employment contract or extension letter from employer
  - Updated company documents (financial statements, tax receipts)
  - Medical certificate (if required; typically annually)
  - Application forms + fee
• Processing: 7-15 business days; applicant may stay in Thailand during processing with acknowledgment receipt
• Renewal granted in 1-year increments for continued employment

=== FAMILY DEPENDENTS ===
ELIGIBILITY TO SPONSOR FAMILY:
• Non-Immigrant B Visa + Work Permit holders CAN sponsor spouse and children for Non-Immigrant O Visa (Dependent)
• Dependents receive visa validity matching primary holder's Work Permit

DEPENDENT VISA APPLICATION:
• Apply via Thai Embassy/Consulate (if abroad) or Immigration Office (if in Thailand)
• Required documents:
  - Primary holder's Work Permit copy, passport, employment proof
  - Marriage certificate (spouse) or birth certificate (children), authenticated + translated
  - Proof of financial support from primary holder
  - Passport copies and photographs of dependents

DEPENDENT RIGHTS:
• Spouse: May study or engage in unpaid activities; CANNOT work without separate Work Permit
• Children: Can enroll in Thai/international schools; cannot work without authorization
• All dependents: Must report address to Immigration within 24 hours of arrival; 90-day reporting if staying >90 days

=== TAX & COMPLIANCE OBLIGATIONS ===
INCOME TAX:
• Foreign employees are tax residents if stay in Thailand exceeds 180 days in a calendar year
• Must obtain Tax ID from Revenue Department
• Employer typically withholds Personal Income Tax (PIT); file annual tax return (March each year)
• Tax rates: Progressive 5-35% based on income

SOCIAL SECURITY:
• Foreign employees generally required to contribute to Social Security Fund (if employer has 1+ Thai employees)
• Contribution: 5% of salary (capped at THB 750/month) shared between employer/employee
• Benefits: Healthcare, maternity, disability, death, pension

LABOR LAW COMPLIANCE:
• Employment must comply with Thai Labor Protection Act: minimum wage, working hours (max 48 hrs/week), leave entitlements
• Foreign employees have same rights as Thai employees under most labor statutes
• Disputes resolved via local labor courts or mediation

=== VISA/WORK PERMIT CANCELLATION & EXIT PROCEDURES ===
EMPLOYMENT TERMINATION:
• If employment ends before Work Permit expiry: Employer must cancel Work Permit within 7 days
• Visa holder has 24-hour grace period to depart Thailand OR change visa status
• Failure to depart/change status may result in overstay penalties

EXIT CLEARANCE:
• No formal exit visa required for Work Permit holders
• Ensure Work Permit cancellation and tax clearance before departure
• Carry passport with visa/Work Permit when exiting; may be asked for proof of employment/tax compliance

=== REFUSAL & REAPPLICATION ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Employer fails to meet capital/foreign employee ratio requirements
✗ Qualifications not meeting job requirements or prohibited occupation
✗ Criminal record or adverse immigration history
✗ Incomplete documentation or inconsistent information
✗ Salary below minimum guidelines for nationality/industry

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• Address specific refusal reason: Secure employer compliance, enhance qualification documentation, clarify job role
• For criminal record issues: Provide rehabilitation evidence, character references
• Wait at least 30 days before reapplying unless circumstances changed significantly

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR NON-IMMIGRANT B VISA APPLICATIONS ABROAD:
• Royal Thai Embassy/Consulate: https://www.thaiembassy.org
• Thai e-Visa Portal: https://www.thaievisa.go.th

FOR WORK PERMIT & STAY IN THAILAND:
• Department of Employment (DOE): https://www.doe.go.th
• Immigration Bureau: https://www.immigration.go.th
• DOE Hotline: 1506 (Thai)
• Immigration Hotline: 1178 (Thai/English)

FOR TAX & SOCIAL SECURITY:
• Revenue Department: https://www.rd.go.th
• Social Security Office: https://www.sso.go.th

FOR LABOR DISPUTES:
• Ministry of Labour: https://www.mol.go.th
• Labor protection hotline: 1546

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: UAE Family Sponsorship Residence Visa
COUNTRY: United Arab Emirates
SOURCE: https://www.icp.gov.ae | https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae | https://www.mohap.gov.ae
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• Expatriate residents with valid UAE residence permit can sponsor family members for residence visas
• Sponsor must meet minimum salary requirement: AED 4,000/month OR AED 3,000/month + valid accommodation
• Sponsored family members receive residence visas linked to sponsor's visa validity (except parents: yearly visas)
• Medical fitness test mandatory for sponsored members aged 18+ at approved UAE health centers
• Profession type no longer a barrier to family sponsorship eligibility

=== SPONSOR ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA ===
SPONSOR MUST:
□ Hold valid UAE residence permit/visa (not under cancellation process)
□ Earn minimum monthly salary: AED 4,000 gross OR AED 3,000 gross + valid accommodation proof
□ Provide valid accommodation meeting emirate occupancy standards
□ Not have pending legal/immigration violations
□ Maintain medical insurance for sponsored parents (if applicable)

SALARY PROOF ACCEPTABLE DOCUMENTS:
• Employment contract signed by employer and employee, stating gross monthly salary
• Salary certificate on company letterhead, signed/stamped
• Bank statements (last 3-6 months) showing consistent salary deposits
• Wage Protection System (WPS) report from MOHRE

ACCOMMODATION PROOF (FOR AED 3,000 + HOUSING ROUTE):
• Registered tenancy contract (Ejari in Dubai, Tawtheeq in Abu Dhabi, equivalent in other emirates)
• Property ownership deed (if sponsor owns residence)
• Company-provided accommodation letter (for employer-sponsored housing)
• Must meet occupancy standards: typically 1 bedroom per couple, additional space for children

=== MEDICAL FITNESS REQUIREMENTS ===
MANDATORY FOR SPONSORED MEMBERS AGED 18+:
• Medical examination at MOHAP-approved health center in UAE
• Tests: Chest X-ray (TB screening), blood tests (HIV, Hepatitis B/C, Syphilis), general physical exam
• Results typically available within 24-48 hours
• Medical fitness certificate valid for 3 months for visa issuance

MEDICAL FITNESS EXCEPTIONS:
• Dormant/inactive pulmonary tuberculosis: Granted one-year "Health Fitness Certificate for Residence" subject to treatment and follow-up by respective health authority
• Medically unfit applicants (active communicable diseases): Will not be granted residence visa
• Applicants under 18: May have reduced testing requirements per emirate health authority

APPROVED HEALTH CENTERS:
• Abu Dhabi: Disease Prevention & Screening Center, SEHA facilities
• Dubai: Dubai Health Authority (DHA) approved centers
• Other Emirates: Emirates Health Services facilities
• Full list: https://www.mohap.gov.ae

=== SPONSORING SPOUSE ===
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Legally married to sponsor (marriage recognized under UAE law)
□ Marriage certificate attested and translated to Arabic if not in Arabic/English
□ Sponsor meets salary/accommodation requirements

DOCUMENTS REQUIRED:
• Application form (online via ICP/GDRFA or through registered typing office)
• Passport copy of spouse (valid 6+ months)
• Recent passport-sized photograph of spouse (white background, 4.3x5.5cm)
• Medical clearance certificate (if spouse is 18+)
• Copy of sponsor's employment contract or company contract
• Salary certificate from employer stating sponsor's monthly salary
• Attested marriage certificate (Arabic or certified Arabic translation)
• Registered tenancy contract or property deed

SPECIAL CASE: SPONSORING TWO WIVES (MUSLIM RESIDENTS):
• Permitted under UAE personal status law for Muslim men
• Requirements:
  - Legal marriage certificates for both wives (attested)
  - Proof of financial capacity to support both households (salary ≥ AED 8,000 or AED 6,000 + accommodation)
  - Written consent from first wife (recommended)
  - Separate accommodation arrangements for each family unit
• Each wife sponsored independently; children sponsored under respective mother

=== SPONSORING CHILDREN ===

#### DAUGHTERS
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Unmarried daughters of any age
□ No upper age limit for sponsorship

DOCUMENTS:
• Birth certificate attested and translated to Arabic if needed
• Passport copy (valid 6+ months)
• Recent photograph
• Medical clearance (if 18+)
• Sponsor's salary/accommodation proof

#### SONS
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Sons until age 25
□ Sons of determination (special needs): No age limit

ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS FOR SONS OF DETERMINATION:
• Medical report confirming disability from MOHAP-approved center
• Proof of ongoing care needs
• Special education/therapy enrollment documentation (if applicable)

#### NEWBORNS BORN IN UAE
CRITICAL TIMELINE:
• Residence visa application must be submitted within 120 days of birth
• Late application fines:
  - Days 121-180: AED 100 per day
  - Days 181+: AED 200 per day + potential travel ban until fines paid

DOCUMENTS:
• UAE birth certificate (issued by health authority)
• Parents' passport copies and residence visas
• Parents' attested marriage certificate
• Passport copy of newborn
• Photograph of newborn

#### STEPCHILDREN
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Subject to GDRFA/ICP approval
□ Requires deposit per child (typically AED 3,000)
□ Written No-Objection Certificate (NOC) from biological parent

VISA DETAILS:
• Validity: 1 year, renewable annually
• Renewal requires updated NOC and deposit maintenance

DOCUMENTS:
• Birth certificate showing biological parentage
• Marriage certificate of sponsor to child's parent
• NOC from biological parent (attested)
• Passport copy and photograph of stepchild
• Medical clearance (if 18+)
• Sponsor's salary/accommodation proof

=== SPONSORING PARENTS ===
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Sponsor must earn minimum salary (AED 4,000 or AED 3,000 + accommodation)
□ Must sponsor BOTH parents together (cannot sponsor one parent alone)
□ Exception: If one parent deceased or parents divorced, provide official documentation
□ Must prove sponsor is parents' sole financial support with no one to care for them in home country
□ Must obtain medical insurance policy for each parent with minimum coverage (recommended AED 150,000+)

VISA DETAILS:
• Validity: 1 year, renewable annually regardless of sponsor's visa duration
• Renewal requires: Updated medical insurance, proof of continued financial support, medical fitness certificate

DEPOSIT REQUIREMENT:
• Typically AED 3,000-5,000 per parent (varies by emirate)
• Refundable upon visa cancellation or parent's departure from UAE
• Paid via authorized typing center, Amer center, or ICP/GDRFA portal

DOCUMENTS:
• Application form
• Passports of parents (valid 6+ months)
• Photographs of parents
• Medical clearance certificates for parents
• Sponsor's employment contract and salary certificate
• Attested birth certificate proving relationship
• Proof of parents' dependency: Bank transfer records, affidavit of support, home country documentation
• Medical insurance policy documents for each parent
• Proof of accommodation suitable for parents

=== UAE CITIZEN SPONSORSHIP (5-YEAR VISA) ===
ELIGIBLE BENEFICIARIES:
• Foreign spouse of UAE citizen
• Foreign parent of UAE citizen
• Foreign child of UAE citizen (any age)

KEY BENEFITS:
• 5-year renewable residence visa
• No salary requirement for UAE citizen sponsor
• Full work rights for sponsored spouse (no separate work permit needed)
• Access to UAE public services (healthcare, education subsidies)

APPLICATION PROCESS:
• UAE citizen sponsor applies via ICP portal or Amer center
• Required: UAE citizen's passport/Emirates ID, beneficiary's passport, relationship proof (attested marriage/birth certificate)
• Processing: 2-3 weeks standard
• Fees: ~AED 1,000-2,000 total (lower than expatriate sponsorship)

FOREIGN WIDOW/DIVORCED MOTHER OF UAE CITIZEN:
• Eligible for residence visa sponsored by oldest UAE citizen child
• Requirements: Death certificate/divorce decree, child's UAE citizenship proof, proof of dependency
• Visa validity: Matches child's sponsorship capacity (typically 1-5 years renewable)

=== APPLICATION PROCESS ===
CHANNELS:

#### DIGITAL (ICP SMART SERVICES / GDRFA DUBAI)
STEPS:
1. Login to https://smartservices.icp.gov.ae or https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae
2. Select "Sponsor Family" service
3. Fill application form with sponsor and dependent details
4. Upload required documents (passport copies, photos, marriage/birth certificates, salary proof, accommodation proof)
5. Pay fees online via credit/debit card
6. Receive application reference number for tracking
7. Await approval notification (2-4 weeks standard)
8. Dependent enters UAE on entry permit; sponsor has 60 days to complete residence visa issuance
9. Complete medical exam (if dependent 18+), biometrics, Emirates ID registration
10. Collect residence visa + Emirates ID

#### IN-PERSON (TYPING CENTERS / AMER CENTERS)
STEPS:
1. Visit authorized typing center or Amer Center (Dubai)
2. Submit completed application form + all required documents
3. Staff enters application into system; verifies documents on-spot
4. Pay fees via cash, card, or local payment methods
5. Receive receipt with reference number
6. Track application status online using reference number
7. Upon approval: Dependent enters UAE; complete medical exam, biometrics, ID registration within 60 days

PROCESSING TIMES:
• Standard: 2-4 weeks from complete application submission
• Express (if available): 24-72 hours (additional fee may apply)
• Factors affecting timing: Document completeness, medical exam scheduling, security checks, peak seasons

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE) ===
PER DEPENDENT VISA (SPOUSE/CHILD):
• Application/Processing Fee: AED 100-300
• Visa Issuance Fee: AED 100-300
• Medical Examination (if 18+): AED 350-500
• Emirates ID Registration: AED 100-370 (depending on validity)
• Typing/Service Center Fee: AED 100-200 (if using third-party)
• **Estimated Total per Spouse/Child: ~AED 1,500-2,500**

FOR PARENT SPONSORSHIP (PER PARENT):
• All above fees PLUS:
• Deposit: AED 3,000-5,000 (refundable)
• Annual medical insurance: ~AED 1,000-3,000 (depending on coverage)
• **Estimated Total per Parent (first year): ~AED 2,000-3,500**

NOTE: Fees vary by emirate, service channel, and applicant age. Check official portals for current rates.

=== CANCELLATION & GRACE PERIOD ===
WHEN SPONSOR'S VISA IS CANCELLED:
• Dependents' visas automatically cancelled simultaneously
• 6-month grace period from cancellation date for dependents to:
  - Obtain new sponsorship (new employer, family member, or investor visa)
  - Change status to tourist visa and depart UAE
  - Apply for Golden Visa or other long-term residence if eligible

CONSEQUENCES OF EXPIRING GRACE PERIOD:
• Overstay fines accrue: AED 125 first day, AED 25 each subsequent day
• Potential entry ban (6 months to permanent) for excessive overstay
• Future UAE visa applications face additional scrutiny

VOLUNTARY CANCELLATION OF DEPENDENT VISA:
• Sponsor can cancel dependent visa anytime via ICP/GDRFA portal
• Dependent must depart UAE within 30 days of cancellation or change status
• Deposit refund processed after dependent departure confirmation

SPONSOR RESPONSIBILITY:
• If sponsor fails to renew or cancel dependent visa upon expiry, sponsor may be liable for fines
• Fines for late renewal: Similar to overstay fines (AED 100-200/day after grace period)

=== DOCUMENT ATTESTATION PROCESS ===
FOREIGN MARRIAGE/BIRTH CERTIFICATES MUST BE:
1. Attested by issuing country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs
2. Legalized by UAE Embassy/Consulate in that country
3. Further attested by MOFAIC (Ministry of Foreign Affairs & International Cooperation) in UAE upon arrival

TRANSLATION REQUIREMENTS:
• Documents not in Arabic or English must be translated by certified translator
• Translation must be attested alongside original document
• Arabic translation preferred for faster processing

MOFAIC ATTESTATION IN UAE:
• Can be completed online: https://www.mofaic.gov.ae
• Or at MOFAIC customer happiness centers
• Fee: ~AED 150-300 per document depending on type

=== FINE AMOUNTS FOR LATE APPLICATIONS ===
NEWBORN VISA DEADLINE (120 DAYS FROM BIRTH):
• Days 121-180: AED 100 per day fine
• Days 181+: AED 200 per day fine + potential travel ban until fines paid

DEPENDENT ENTRY PERMIT DEADLINE (60 DAYS FROM ENTRY):
• Days 61-90: AED 100 per day fine
• Days 91+: AED 200 per day fine + potential visa cancellation

PAYMENT & WAIVERS:
• Fines must be paid before visa issuance or departure
• Compassionate waivers possible in exceptional circumstances (medical emergency, force majeure)
• Apply for waiver via ICP/GDRFA portal with supporting evidence (medical reports, police reports, etc.)

=== APPEAL PROCESS FOR REFUSALS ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Insufficient salary proof or inconsistent bank statements
✗ Invalid/unattested marriage or birth certificates
✗ Medical unfitness of sponsored member (active communicable disease)
✗ Sponsor's visa nearing expiry or under cancellation process
✗ Incomplete application or missing documents
✗ Accommodation does not meet occupancy standards

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• Address specific refusal reason with stronger documentation
• Wait at least 30 days before reapplying unless circumstances changed significantly
• For medical refusals: Obtain second opinion from MOHAP-approved center
• For salary disputes: Provide additional proof (WPS report, tax documents, employer letter)

FORMAL APPEAL:
• Submit written appeal to ICP/GDRFA within 30 days of refusal notice
• Include: Refusal reference number, corrected documents, explanation letter
• Processing: 2-4 weeks for appeal decision
• No fee for initial appeal; legal representation optional
• Decision communicated via email/SMS; if approved, application resumes from point of refusal

=== COMPLIANCE & RESPONSIBILITIES ===
SPONSOR MUST:
• Maintain minimum salary throughout sponsorship period
• Keep accommodation valid and meeting standards
• Renew medical insurance for sponsored parents annually
• Notify authorities of address changes, passport renewals, family status changes
• Ensure sponsored dependents comply with UAE laws and visa conditions

DEPENDENT RESPONSIBILITIES:
• Carry Emirates ID at all times while in UAE
• Depart UAE or change status if sponsor's visa is cancelled
• Renew visa before expiry to avoid overstay fines
• Comply with medical treatment requirements if granted conditional fitness certificate

PENALTIES FOR NON-COMPLIANCE:
• Visa cancellation and deportation for serious violations
• Fines for overstaying or working without proper authorization (dependents need separate work permit)
• Future entry bans for fraudulent applications or criminal activity
• Sponsor may face fines, visa cancellation, or employment consequences for dependent violations

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR FAMILY SPONSORSHIP APPLICATIONS:
• ICP Federal: https://www.icp.gov.ae | Call: 600 522222
• GDRFA Dubai: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae | Call: 800 5111
• TAMM Abu Dhabi: https://www.tamm.abudhabi | Call: 800 555
• Email: Use contact forms on respective official websites

FOR MEDICAL FITNESS EXAMINATIONS:
• MOHAP Approved Centers: https://www.mohap.gov.ae
• SEHA (Abu Dhabi): https://www.seha.ae
• DHA (Dubai): https://www.dha.gov.ae

FOR DOCUMENT ATTESTATION:
• MOFAIC Attestation Services: https://www.mofaic.gov.ae
• Authorized typing centers across UAE emirates (verify authorization via official portals)

FOR SALARY/WPS VERIFICATION:
• Ministry of Human Resources & Emiratisation (MOHRE): https://www.mohre.gov.ae | Call: 600 665 555

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: UAE Golden Visa (Long-Term Residence Visa - 5 or 10 Years)
COUNTRY: United Arab Emirates
SOURCE: https://www.icp.gov.ae | https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae | https://www.tamm.abudhabi
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW & KEY BENEFITS ===
• Long-term renewable residence visa: 5 years (real estate investors, entrepreneurs, students) or 10 years (public investors, outstanding talents, humanitarian pioneers)
• No sponsor required within UAE
• Multiple-entry entry visa (6 months validity) to complete residence issuance
• Privilege to stay outside UAE for more than 6 months without visa cancellation
• Sponsor family members: spouses, children (any age), parents (under conditions)
• Sponsor unlimited domestic helpers
• Family members retain residence validity if primary holder passes away
• Pathway to long-term stability in UAE for eligible foreign talents

=== ELIGIBILITY CATEGORIES & REQUIREMENTS ===

#### 1. INVESTORS IN PUBLIC INVESTMENTS (10-YEAR VISA)
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Invest at least AED 2 million in an accredited UAE investment fund
  • Submit letter from fund confirming deposit amount
□ OR Hold valid commercial/industrial license with MoA showing capital ≥ AED 2 million
□ OR Pay annual government taxes ≥ AED 250,000 (verified by Federal Tax Authority letter)
□ Own invested capital completely (not borrowed/loaned)
□ Provide proof of medical insurance for self and family (if sponsoring)

#### 2. REAL ESTATE INVESTORS (5-YEAR VISA, RENEWABLE)
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Own one or more properties valued at ≥ AED 2 million (verified by emirate Land Department letter)
□ Property purchase can be financed via loan from specific local banks approved by competent local entity
□ Property must be completed (off-plan properties may have additional conditions)
□ Maintain ownership throughout visa validity for renewal eligibility

#### 3. ENTREPRENEURS (5-YEAR VISA)
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Own economic project of technical/future nature based on risk & innovation
□ Project value ≥ AED 500,000 (verified by UAE auditor letter)
□ Approval from emirate authorities confirming project is technical/future-oriented
□ Approval from accredited UAE business incubator for proposed activity
□ Business must be registered and operational in UAE

#### 4. OUTSTANDING SPECIALISED TALENTS (10-YEAR VISA)
ELIGIBLE PROFESSIONS:
• Doctors, Scientists, Creative people (culture/art), Inventors, Executive Directors
• Specialists in: epidemiology, AI, big data, computer/electronic/software/electrical engineering, genetics, biotechnology
• Athletes, PhD holders, Engineering/Science specialists

CATEGORY-SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS:

DOCTORS:
□ Approval Letter from UAE Ministry of Health and Prevention to practice medicine

SCIENTISTS:
□ Letter of recommendation from Emirates Council of Scientists, OR
□ Letter confirming award of Mohammed bin Rashid Medal for Scientific Excellence

INVENTORS:
□ Recommendation letter from Ministry of Economy stating patent adds value to UAE economy

CREATIVE PEOPLE (CULTURE/ART):
□ Approval letter from emirate's department of culture and arts

EXECUTIVE DIRECTORS:
□ Accredited university degree (Bachelor's minimum) attested by Ministry of Education
□ Experience letter: ≥5 years in same executive position
□ Salary certificate: ≥AED 50,000 monthly
□ Valid UAE work contract

ATHLETES:
□ Recommendation letter from General Sports Authority or emirate sports council

SPECIALISTS IN ENGINEERING/SCIENCE:
□ Accredited Bachelor's or Master's degree (Ministry of Education attestation)
□ Valid UAE work contract in relevant field

#### 5. OUTSTANDING STUDENTS

HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS (5-YEAR VISA):
□ National-level topper with minimum 95% grade in public/private secondary school
□ Recommendation letter from Ministry of Education (Emirates Schools Establishment)
□ Visa duration: 5 years, extendable if enrolled in UAE program requiring >5 years study

UNIVERSITY STUDENTS - UAE INSTITUTIONS (10-YEAR VISA):
□ University rated Class A or B by UAE Ministry of Education
□ Cumulative GPA: ≥3.5 for Class A universities, ≥3.8 for Class B universities
□ Graduated within last 2 years at time of application
□ Recommendation letter from university OR accredited graduation certificate/academic record

UNIVERSITY STUDENTS - FOREIGN INSTITUTIONS (10-YEAR VISA):
□ University ranked among global top 100 per Ministry of Education-recognized rating system
□ Cumulative GPA: ≥3.5
□ Graduated within last 2 years at time of application
□ Graduation certificate accredited by UAE Ministry of Education

#### 6. PIONEERS OF HUMANITARIAN WORK (10-YEAR VISA)
ELIGIBILITY (MUST MEET ONE):
□ Member/outstanding employee of international/regional humanitarian organization (≥5 years service)
□ Member/outstanding employee of civil association/public interest institution (≥5 years service)
□ Recipient of appreciation award from local/regional/international humanitarian organization
□ Funder of humanitarian work with support value ≥ AED 2 million (or equivalent)

#### 7. FRONTLINE HEROES (10-YEAR VISA)
ELIGIBILITY:
□ Displayed extraordinary effort during crises (e.g., COVID-19 pandemic)
□ Categories: nurses, medical assistants, lab technicians, pharmacologists, other approved cadres
□ Recommendation letter from competent authority (e.g., Frontline Heroes Office, health authority)

=== APPLICATION PROCESS ===
STEP-BY-STEP:
1. Determine eligibility category and gather required documents
2. Submit pre-approval application via:
   • ICP Smart Services: https://smartservices.icp.gov.ae
   • GDRFA Dubai: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae
   • TAMM Abu Dhabi: https://www.tamm.abudhabi
   • Authorized typing centers or Amer centers
3. Receive initial approval notification (if eligible)
4. Complete medical examination at MOHAP-approved health center
5. Submit biometrics (fingerprints, photo) at authorized ICP center
6. Pay visa issuance fees and Emirates ID fees
7. Receive Golden Visa residence permit + Emirates ID card

PROCESSING TIMES:
• Investors/Entrepreneurs: 2-4 weeks (after document verification)
• Talents/Professionals: 3-6 weeks (requires ministry endorsements)
• Students: 2-3 weeks (after university/ministry verification)
• Humanitarian/Frontline: 2-4 weeks (after authority recommendation)
• Peak seasons may cause delays; apply well in advance

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE) ===
COMMON FEES ACROSS CATEGORIES:
• Application/Processing Fee: AED 100-500 (varies by emirate/channel)
• Visa Issuance Fee: AED 100-300
• Medical Examination: AED 350-500 (at approved health centers)
• Emirates ID Registration: AED 100-370 (depending on validity period)
• Typing/Service Center Fee: AED 100-200 (if using third-party service)

ESTIMATED TOTAL COSTS:
• 5-Year Golden Visa: ~AED 2,500-4,000 total
• 10-Year Golden Visa: ~AED 3,500-6,000 total
• Note: Fees vary by emirate, service channel, and applicant category
• Payment methods: Credit/debit card, cash at authorized centers

=== DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS (GENERAL) ===
MANDATORY FOR ALL APPLICANTS:
□ Valid passport (6+ months validity from intended entry)
□ Recent passport-sized photograph (white background, 4.3x5.5cm)
□ Completed application form via smart services portal
□ Category-specific supporting documents (as listed above)

DOCUMENT AUTHENTICATION:
• Foreign documents must be:
  - Attested by issuing country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs
  - Legalized by UAE Embassy/Consulate in that country
  - Further attested by MOFAIC in UAE upon arrival
• Academic degrees, marriage/birth certificates: Must be attested + translated to Arabic if not in English/Arabic
• Financial documents: Officially stamped by issuing bank/institution

=== MEDICAL EXAMINATION ===
REQUIREMENTS:
• Mandatory for all residence visa applicants at MOHAP-approved health centers
• Tests: Chest X-ray (TB screening), blood tests (HIV, Hepatitis B/C, Syphilis), general physical exam
• Results typically available within 24-48 hours
• Medical fitness certificate valid for 3 months for visa issuance

EXEMPTIONS:
• Applicants under 18 may have reduced testing requirements
• Some categories may have expedited medical processing

=== EMIRATES ID & BIOMETRICS ===
POST-APPROVAL REQUIREMENTS:
• Must register for Emirates ID at Federal Authority for Identity and Citizenship (ICP)
• Biometrics collection: Fingerprints, iris scan, digital photo at authorized center
• Emirates ID card serves as official residence proof + identity document
• Must carry Emirates ID at all times while in UAE

VALIDITY:
• Emirates ID validity matches Golden Visa duration (5 or 10 years)
• Renewal process begins 30 days before expiry via ICP smart services

=== FAMILY SPONSORSHIP RIGHTS ===
ELIGIBLE DEPENDENTS:
• Spouse (legally married)
• Children (any age, including adult children)
• Parents (under specific conditions: financial dependency proof, accommodation)
• Unlimited domestic helpers (separate application process)

DEPENDENT VISA DETAILS:
• Validity matches primary Golden Visa holder's duration
• Dependents receive residence permits enabling study, healthcare access
• Dependents can travel in/out of UAE with primary holder or independently

WORK RIGHTS FOR DEPENDENTS:
• Spouses: Can work in UAE but require separate work permit or Letter of Consent (if eligible under MOM rules)
• Adult children: Must obtain own work visa/permit for employment
• Domestic helpers: Work rights limited to household duties for sponsoring family

=== RENEWAL PROCESS ===
ELIGIBILITY FOR RENEWAL:
• Must continue to meet original category requirements:
  - Investors: Maintain investment threshold
  - Entrepreneurs: Business remains operational and innovative
  - Talents: Continue practicing in approved field with endorsements
  - Students: Maintain academic standing if still studying
• No criminal record or immigration violations during visa validity
• Valid passport (6+ months remaining at renewal application)

RENEWAL STEPS:
1. Submit renewal application via ICP/GDRFA portal 30-90 days before expiry
2. Provide updated supporting documents (e.g., updated bank statements, employment letter, business license)
3. Complete medical examination if required (typically every 5 years)
4. Pay renewal fees (similar structure to initial issuance)
5. Receive renewed visa sticker + updated Emirates ID

=== VISA CANCELLATION CONDITIONS ===
GOLDEN VISA MAY BE CANCELLED IF:
✗ Holder obtains residence in another country and abandons UAE residency
✗ Holder is convicted of serious crime or poses security threat to UAE
✗ Fraudulent documents or misrepresentation discovered during validity period
✗ Investor category: Investment withdrawn or falls below AED 2 million threshold
✗ Talent category: No longer practicing in approved field or losing ministry endorsements
✗ Student category: Discontinued studies or failed to maintain required academic standards
✗ Violation of UAE laws or immigration regulations

CONSEQUENCES OF CANCELLATION:
• 30-day grace period to either rectify status or depart UAE
• Sponsored dependents' visas cancelled simultaneously
• Future UAE visa applications may face additional scrutiny or refusal
• Potential fines or penalties depending on violation nature

=== TRANSITION TO CITIZENSHIP ===
CURRENT POLICY:
• Golden Visa is a long-term residence permit, NOT a direct pathway to UAE citizenship
• UAE citizenship is granted through separate, highly selective process (Royal Decree)
• Long-term residency via Golden Visa may strengthen future citizenship consideration but is not guaranteed

ALTERNATIVE PATHWAYS TO CITIZENSHIP:
• Naturalization: Typically requires 30 years continuous residence (reduced for certain categories like scientists, artists)
• Marriage to UAE national: Possible pathway with specific conditions and approval
• Exceptional contribution: Scientists, artists, investors may be considered for citizenship via special Royal Decree
• Note: Citizenship decisions are at discretion of UAE leadership; no automatic entitlement

=== COMPLIANCE & RESPONSIBILITIES ===
GOLDEN VISA HOLDER MUST:
• Maintain eligibility criteria throughout visa validity
• Renew visa before expiry to maintain legal status
• Notify authorities of address changes, passport renewals, family status changes
• Comply with all UAE laws and regulations
• Ensure sponsored dependents also comply with visa conditions

PENALTIES FOR NON-COMPLIANCE:
• Visa cancellation and deportation for serious violations
• Fines for overstaying or working without proper authorization
• Future entry bans for fraudulent applications or criminal activity

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR GOLDEN VISA APPLICATIONS:
• ICP Federal: https://www.icp.gov.ae | Call: 600 522222
• GDRFA Dubai: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae | Call: 800 5111
• TAMM Abu Dhabi: https://www.tamm.abudhabi | Call: 800 555
• Email: Use contact forms on respective official websites

FOR DOCUMENT ATTESTATION:
• MOFAIC Attestation Services: https://www.mofaic.gov.ae
• Authorized typing centers across UAE emirates (verify authorization via official portals)

FOR MEDICAL EXAMINATION:
• MOHAP Approved Health Centers: https://www.mohap.gov.ae
• SEHA (Abu Dhabi): https://www.seha.ae
• DHA (Dubai): https://www.dha.gov.ae

FOR EMIRATES ID SERVICES:
• ICP Smart Services: https://smartservices.icp.gov.ae
• ICP Customer Happiness Centers: Locations listed on official website
