content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: UAE 5-Year Multi-Entry Tourist Visa
COUNTRY: United Arab Emirates
SOURCE: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae | https://www.icp.gov.ae
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• Multi-entry tourist visa valid for 5 years from date of issue
• No guarantor or host required within UAE
• For travelers not eligible for visa-on-arrival or visa-free entry
• Purpose: Tourism, visiting family/friends, short business visits (NO employment)

=== ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA ===
APPLICANT MUST:
□ Hold passport or travel document valid for at least 6 months from intended entry date
□ Passport must allow entry into UAE and return to country of residence/issuance
□ Provide proof of bank balance: USD 4,000 (or equivalent) maintained over 6 months prior to application
□ Hold valid health insurance covering UAE stay
□ Possess round-trip ticket or onward travel ticket
□ Not be subject to UAE entry ban or immigration violation
□ Intend stay of max 90 days per entry (extendable once), max 180 days total per calendar year

=== DOCUMENT REQUIREMENTS ===
MANDATORY DOCUMENTS:
□ Personal photograph:
  • Recent color photo (within 6 months)
  • Size: 4.3 x 5.5 cm (passport-sized)
  • White/light blue background, neutral expression, face fully visible
  • Digital format: JPG/PNG, 300 DPI minimum for online applications

□ Passport copy:
  • Clear copy of biodata page
  • Valid for at least 6 months from intended entry date
  • Must allow return to country of residence/issuance

□ Bank statement:
  • Official statement from recognized bank covering last 6 months
  • Must show consistent balance of at least USD 4,000 (or equivalent)
  • Stamped/signed by bank; in English or Arabic (or certified translation)

□ Health insurance:
  • Policy document showing applicant name, coverage amount, validity dates
  • Minimum recommended coverage: ~AED 150,000 (~USD 40,000)
  • Must cover emergency medical treatment, hospitalization, repatriation
  • Valid for entire duration of intended UAE stay

□ Round-trip ticket:
  • Confirmed flight reservation showing entry and exit from UAE
  • Can be provisional booking; final ticket not required at application stage

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE) ===
APPLICATION FEE BREAKDOWN:
• Application Fee: AED 100
• Issuance Fee: AED 500
• Guarantee Amount: AED 3,000 (refundable upon compliance)
• Warranty Service Fee: AED 21
• Guarantee Collection/Return Fee: AED 20
• Knowledge Dirham: AED 10
• Innovation Dirham: AED 10
• Service Fee: AED 52.50
• **Estimated Total: AED 3,713.50** (~USD 1,010)

IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Total amount may vary based on applicant circumstances, emirate, or service channel
• Guarantee amount (AED 3,000) is refundable if visa conditions are met
• Payment methods: Credit/debit card (online), cash/card (in-person centers)
• Fees are non-refundable if application is rejected

=== APPLICATION CHANNELS ===

#### DIGITAL CHANNELS (UAE PASS / ICP SMART SERVICES)
STEPS:
1. Log in to smart services system via UAE Pass or username/password
2. Search for "5-Year Multi-Entry Tourist Visa" service
3. Fill in application form with personal, passport, and travel details
4. Upload required documents (photo, passport copy, bank statement, insurance, ticket)
5. Pay service fees online via credit/debit card
6. Receive application reference number for tracking
7. Await approval notification via email/SMS (2-5 working days standard)
8. Download e-visa upon approval; print or save digitally for travel

ADVANTAGES:
• 24/7 access from anywhere
• Track application status online
• Receive e-visa directly via email
• No physical document submission required

#### CUSTOMER HAPPINESS CENTERS (GDRFA)
STEPS:
1. Visit nearest Customer Happiness Center (Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Sharjah, etc.)
2. Take automated turn ticket and wait for your number
3. Submit completed application form + all required documents to service employee
4. Documents verified on-spot; any issues addressed immediately
5. Pay fees via cash, card, or local payment methods
6. Receive application receipt with reference number
7. Await approval notification (2-5 working days)
8. Collect visa sticker or receive e-visa notification

ADVANTAGES:
• In-person assistance with document verification
• Immediate help for complex cases or questions
• Multiple locations across UAE emirates

#### AMER SERVICE CENTERS
STEPS:
1. Visit nearest Amer Center (authorized GDRFA service provider)
2. Take automated turn ticket and wait
3. Submit application + documents to customer service employee
4. Pay fees as directed
5. Receive receipt and tracking information
6. Await approval; receive visa via email or collection

ADVANTAGES:
• Extended operating hours (some centers open evenings/weekends)
• Additional services: visa extensions, status changes, fine payments
• Staff trained on GDRFA procedures

=== STAY CONDITIONS & LIMITS ===
PER-ENTRY STAY:
• Maximum 90 consecutive days per entry into UAE
• Clock resets upon exit and re-entry

EXTENSION OPTION:
• Can apply to extend 90-day stay once for additional 90 days
• Must apply BEFORE current stay period expires
• Extension fee: ~AED 600-800 (varies by emirate)
• Processing: ~2-5 working days
• Total stay cannot exceed 180 days within any calendar year

ANNUAL LIMIT:
• Maximum 180 days total stay in UAE per calendar year across all entries
• Days counted from entry stamp to exit stamp (include partial days)
• Exceeding limit may result in visa cancellation and future application issues

=== HEALTH INSURANCE DETAILS ===
MINIMUM COVERAGE REQUIREMENTS:
• Emergency medical treatment and hospitalization
• Repatriation of remains in case of death
• Ambulance services and emergency evacuation
• Recommended minimum: AED 150,000 (~USD 40,000) coverage

ACCEPTABLE INSURANCE PROVIDERS:
• UAE-based: Daman, AXA Gulf, Oman Insurance, NextCare, MetLife, etc.
• International: Must have UAE network coverage and direct billing capability
• Travel insurance: Must explicitly cover UAE and meet minimum coverage amounts

PROOF OF INSURANCE:
• Policy document or certificate showing:
  - Applicant name matching passport
  - Coverage amount and scope
  - Validity dates covering intended UAE stay
  - Insurer contact information and policy number

=== PROCESSING TIMES ===
STANDARD PROCESSING:
• Digital applications: 2-5 working days
• In-person applications: 2-5 working days (submission day not counted)
• Express processing (if available): 24-48 hours (additional fee may apply)

FACTORS AFFECTING PROCESSING:
• Application completeness and document quality
• Nationality and security check requirements
• Peak travel seasons: December-January, March-April, summer holidays
• UAE public holidays (processing paused)

TRACKING APPLICATION:
• Digital: Log in to smart services portal with reference number
• In-person: Use receipt reference number on GDRFA/ICP website
• Notifications: Email/SMS updates at key stages (received, under review, approved/rejected)

=== ENTRY TO UAE ===
AT PORT OF ENTRY:
• Present: Passport with visa (printed or digital), return/onward ticket, health insurance proof if requested
• Immigration officer may ask: Purpose of visit, accommodation details, funds proof, travel itinerary
• Biometrics: Fingerprints and photo may be collected (standard procedure)

e-VISA USAGE:
• Print e-visa or save digitally on phone/tablet
• Present alongside passport at immigration counter
• Keep copy accessible throughout stay for hotel check-in, etc.

=== COMPLIANCE & PENALTIES ===
OVERSTAY FINES:
• First day of overstay: AED 125
• Each subsequent day: AED 25
• Additional service fees may apply for fine payment

CONSEQUENCES OF NON-COMPLIANCE:
• Must pay all fines before departure or future visa applications
• Excessive overstay may result in:
  - Entry ban (6 months to permanent)
  - Deportation at applicant's expense
  - Future visa applications refused
  - Difficulty obtaining visas for other countries

GRACE PERIOD:
• Typically 10 days after visa expiry to leave UAE or apply for extension
• NOT guaranteed; always confirm current policy with GDRFA/ICP
• Overstay fines may still accrue during grace period in some cases

UNAUTHORIZED EMPLOYMENT:
• Tourist visa DOES NOT permit any form of employment or business activity
• Violation may result in: visa cancellation, deportation, fines, entry ban
• For work: Must obtain proper work visa/residence permit through employer

=== REJECTION & REAPPLICATION ===
COMMON REFUSAL GROUNDS:
✗ Insufficient or unclear bank balance proof
✗ Passport validity <6 months or damaged passport
✗ Previous UAE immigration violations (overstay, deportation, ban)
✗ Security concerns or adverse background checks
✗ Incomplete/unclear application information or document quality issues
✗ Health insurance not meeting requirements

REAPPLICATION GUIDELINES:
• Address specific refusal reason with stronger, clearer documentation
• Wait at least 30 days before reapplying unless circumstances changed significantly
• For security-related refusals: May require special clearance from UAE authorities
• Consider applying through authorized travel agent for guidance on complex cases
• Ensure all documents are translated to English/Arabic by certified translator if needed

=== FAMILY APPLICATIONS ===
SEPARATE APPLICATIONS REQUIRED:
• Each family member (including children) must submit individual application
• Applications can be linked/submitted together via same account for convenience
• Same document requirements apply to each applicant

CHILDREN'S ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:
• Birth certificate (translated to English/Arabic if not in these languages)
• Passport copy (valid 6+ months)
• Photo meeting specifications
• If traveling with one parent: Consent letter from other parent may be requested (notarized recommended)

DEPENDENT COVERAGE:
• Health insurance must cover each family member individually
• Bank statement can be from primary applicant if demonstrating family funds
• Round-trip tickets required for each traveler

=== VISA-EXEMPT & VISA-ON-ARRIVAL CONTEXT ===
WHO DOES NOT NEED THIS VISA:
• GCC citizens (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia): No visa required with national ID
• ~50 nationalities eligible for 30-day visa-on-arrival (extendable once): USA, UK, Canada, Australia, EU, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, etc.
• Select nationalities eligible for 90-day visa-on-arrival (non-extendable): Mexico, Seychelles, etc.

WHY CHOOSE 5-YEAR MULTI-ENTRY VISA:
• Frequent travelers to UAE wanting convenience of pre-approved visa
• Nationalities not eligible for visa-on-arrival
• Travelers wanting longer validity than standard 30/90-day tourist visas
• Business visitors making multiple short trips (without employment)

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR GENERAL VISA QUESTIONS:
• GDRFA Dubai: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae | Call: 800 5111
• ICP Federal: https://www.icp.gov.ae | Call: 600 522222
• Amer Service: https://www.amer.ae | Call: 800 5111 (Dubai)

FOR APPLICATION SUPPORT:
• Smart Services Portal Help: Available via chat/email on official websites
• Customer Happiness Centers: Visit in-person for complex cases
• Authorized Travel Agents: Verify authorization via GDRFA/ICP website

FOR EMERGENCIES IN UAE:
• Police: 999
• Ambulance: 998
• Tourist Police (Dubai): 901

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: UAE Green Visa for Work (5-Year Self-Sponsored Residence Visa)
COUNTRY: United Arab Emirates
SOURCE: https://www.icp.gov.ae | https://www.mohre.gov.ae | https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
• 5-year renewable residence visa that allows holder to sponsor themselves
• No requirement for UAE national or employer sponsorship
• Eligible categories: Freelancers/self-employed individuals, skilled employees
• Provides greater flexibility and stability compared to standard employer-sponsored visas
• Holders can sponsor family members (subject to standard family sponsorship requirements)

=== ELIGIBILITY CATEGORIES & REQUIREMENTS ===

#### 1. FREELANCERS AND SELF-EMPLOYED INDIVIDUALS
ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA:
□ Hold valid freelance/self-employment permit from Ministry of Human Resources and Emiratisation (MOHRE)
□ Hold bachelor's degree OR specialized diploma relevant to freelance activity
□ Provide ONE of the following financial proofs:
   • Evidence of annual income from self-employment ≥ AED 360,000 for each of previous two years, OR
   • Proof of financial solvency throughout intended stay in UAE

FREELANCE PERMIT REQUIREMENTS:
• Apply via MOHRE Freelance Portal: https://www.mohre.gov.ae/freelance
• Eligible activities: Media, marketing, consulting, education, technology, design, arts, etc.
• Permit validity: 1-3 years (renewable)
• Fee: ~AED 7,500-15,000 depending on activity and emirate

FINANCIAL PROOF OPTIONS:
INCOME-BASED (AED 360,000/YEAR):
• Bank statements showing consistent freelance income deposits
• Client contracts/invoices demonstrating project revenue
• Tax returns from UAE or home country (converted to AED)
• Average calculation: AED 30,000/month over 24 months

SOLVENCY-BASED (ALTERNATIVE TO INCOME THRESHOLD):
• Bank statements (last 12 months) showing balance ≥ AED 300,000
• Investment portfolio statements valued ≥ AED 500,000
• Business ownership documents + audited financial statements showing profitability
• Letter from recognized financial institution confirming assets/liquidity

DOCUMENTS REQUIRED:
• MOHRE freelance/self-employment permit (valid)
• Attested bachelor's degree or specialized diploma (Arabic/English or certified translation)
• Financial proof documents (as above)
• Passport copy (valid 6+ months)
• Recent passport-sized photograph
• Completed application form via ICP/GDRFA portal

#### 2. SKILLED EMPLOYEES
ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA:
□ Hold valid employment contract with UAE-based employer
□ Be classified in first, second, or third occupational level per MOHRE classification
□ Hold minimum bachelor's degree or equivalent (attested)
□ Receive monthly salary of not less than AED 15,000 (gross, fixed components only)

OCCUPATIONAL LEVEL DEFINITIONS (MOHRE CLASSIFICATION):
FIRST LEVEL (Senior Management/Specialized Professionals):
• Roles: CEOs, Directors, Senior Managers, Consultants, Doctors, Engineers, Lawyers, University Professors
• Requirements: Bachelor's degree + 5+ years experience OR Master's/PhD

SECOND LEVEL (Mid-Level Professionals/Technicians):
• Roles: Department Managers, Team Leads, Senior Specialists, IT Professionals, Accountants, Nurses
• Requirements: Bachelor's degree + 2+ years experience OR Diploma + 5+ years experience

THIRD LEVEL (Skilled Technicians/Supervisors):
• Roles: Supervisors, Senior Technicians, Skilled Tradespeople, Chefs, Senior Administrative Staff
• Requirements: Diploma/Certificate + 3+ years experience OR proven specialized skills

SALARY VERIFICATION RULES:
• AED 15,000 minimum refers to GROSS monthly salary (basic + fixed allowances)
• INCLUDED: Housing allowance (fixed), transportation allowance (fixed), communication allowance (fixed)
• EXCLUDED: Bonuses, commissions, overtime pay, variable incentives
• Verified via: Employment contract, Wage Protection System (WPS) records, bank statements

WPS (WAGE PROTECTION SYSTEM) REQUIREMENT:
• Employer must register employee in MOHRE WPS
• Salary deposits must be consistent and match contract amount
• ICP/GDRFA cross-references WPS data during visa application
• Access WPS verification: https://www.mohre.gov.ae

DOCUMENTS REQUIRED:
• Valid employment contract signed by employer and employee
• MOHRE occupational classification confirmation (via employer or WPS)
• Attested bachelor's degree or equivalent (Arabic/English or certified translation)
• Salary proof: Employment contract, WPS report, bank statements (last 3-6 months)
• Passport copy (valid 6+ months)
• Recent passport-sized photograph
• Completed application form via ICP/GDRFA portal

=== APPLICATION PROCESS ===
STEP-BY-STEP GUIDE:
1. Determine eligibility category and gather required documents
2. Submit application via:
   • ICP Smart Services: https://smartservices.icp.gov.ae
   • GDRFA Dubai: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae
   • Authorized typing centers or Amer centers
3. Pay application fees online or at service center
4. Complete medical examination at MOHAP-approved health center
5. Submit biometrics (fingerprints, photo) at ICP center
6. Receive initial approval notification
7. Pay issuance fees and collect Green Visa residence permit + Emirates ID

PROCESSING TIMES:
• Standard processing: 2-4 weeks from complete application submission
• Express processing (if available): 24-72 hours (additional fee may apply)
• Factors affecting timing: Document completeness, occupational verification, medical exam scheduling, peak seasons

=== FEES (APPROXIMATE) ===
GREEN VISA APPLICATION FEES:
• Application/Processing Fee: AED 300-500
• Visa Issuance Fee: AED 300-500
• Medical Examination: AED 350-500 (at approved health centers)
• Emirates ID Registration: AED 100-370 (5-year validity)
• Typing/Service Center Fee: AED 100-200 (if using third-party)
• **Estimated Total: ~AED 1,500-2,500**

ADDITIONAL COSTS FOR FREELANCERS:
• MOHRE Freelance Permit: AED 7,500-15,000 (depending on activity/emirate, valid 1-3 years)
• Permit renewal: ~50-70% of initial fee

NOTE: Fees vary by emirate and service channel. Check official portals for current rates.

=== MEDICAL EXAMINATION REQUIREMENTS ===
MANDATORY FOR ALL GREEN VISA APPLICANTS:
• Conducted at MOHAP-approved health centers in UAE
• Tests: Chest X-ray (TB screening), blood tests (HIV, Hepatitis B/C, Syphilis), general physical exam
• Results typically available within 24-48 hours
• Medical fitness certificate valid for 3 months for visa issuance

EXEMPTIONS:
• Applicants under 18 may have reduced testing requirements
• Dormant/inactive pulmonary tuberculosis: May receive one-year conditional certificate subject to treatment

APPROVED HEALTH CENTERS:
• Abu Dhabi: Disease Prevention & Screening Center, SEHA facilities
• Dubai: Dubai Health Authority (DHA) approved centers
• Other Emirates: Emirates Health Services facilities
• Full list: https://www.mohap.gov.ae

=== FAMILY SPONSORSHIP RIGHTS ===
ELIGIBILITY TO SPONSOR FAMILY:
• Green Visa holders CAN sponsor family members (spouse, children, parents)
• Standard family sponsorship requirements apply:
  - Minimum salary: AED 4,000/month OR AED 3,000 + valid accommodation
  - Valid accommodation meeting emirate occupancy standards
  - Medical insurance for sponsored parents (if applicable)

ADVANTAGES OVER STANDARD VISA:
• Green Visa validity (5 years) provides longer stability for sponsored dependents
• No employer dependency: Family visas not affected if Green Visa holder changes jobs or becomes unemployed (as long as Green Visa remains valid)

APPLICATION PROCESS FOR DEPENDENTS:
• Submit separate family sponsorship application via ICP/GDRFA portal
• Required: Green Visa copy, salary proof, accommodation proof, relationship documents (attested marriage/birth certificates)
• Processing: 2-4 weeks standard
• Fees: ~AED 1,500-2,500 per dependent (spouse/child); ~AED 2,000-3,500 per parent (includes deposit + insurance)

=== RENEWAL PROCESS (EVERY 5 YEARS) ===
ELIGIBILITY FOR RENEWAL:
• Freelancers: Must maintain valid MOHRE freelance permit AND continue to meet income/solvency requirements
• Skilled employees: Must maintain qualifying employment OR secure new qualifying job within 6 months of previous employment ending
• No criminal record or immigration violations during visa validity
• Valid passport (6+ months remaining at renewal application)

RENEWAL STEPS:
1. Submit renewal application via ICP/GDRFA portal 30-90 days before expiry
2. Provide updated supporting documents:
   • Freelancers: Renewed freelance permit, updated income/financial proof
   • Skilled employees: New/continued employment contract, updated salary proof, MOHRE occupational confirmation
3. Complete medical examination if required (typically every 5 years)
4. Pay renewal fees (similar structure to initial issuance)
5. Receive renewed Green Visa + updated Emirates ID

=== VISA CANCELLATION CONDITIONS ===
GREEN VISA MAY BE CANCELLED IF:
✗ Freelancer: Loses MOHRE freelance permit OR income falls below threshold for 2 consecutive years
✗ Skilled employee: Employment contract terminated AND no new qualifying job secured within 6-month grace period
✗ Holder convicted of serious crime or poses security threat to UAE
✗ Fraudulent documents or misrepresentation discovered during validity period
✗ Holder abandons UAE residency (stays outside UAE >6 months continuously without valid reason)
✗ Violation of UAE laws or immigration regulations

GRACE PERIOD FOR JOB LOSS (SKILLED EMPLOYEES ONLY):
• If employment ends: 6-month grace period to secure new qualifying job
• During grace period: Green Visa remains valid; holder can stay in UAE, sponsor family, seek new employment
• If no new job secured within 6 months: Visa may be cancelled; dependent visas affected simultaneously

CONSEQUENCES OF CANCELLATION:
• 30-day grace period to either rectify status or depart UAE
• Sponsored dependents' visas cancelled simultaneously
• Future UAE visa applications may face additional scrutiny or refusal
• Potential fines or penalties depending on violation nature

=== TRANSITION FROM STANDARD WORK VISA ===
ELIGIBILITY TO SWITCH:
• Holders of standard employer-sponsored work visa CAN apply for Green Visa if they meet eligibility criteria
• No requirement to leave UAE; application can be submitted from within country

PROCESS:
1. Ensure eligibility: Freelancers obtain MOHRE permit; skilled employees verify occupational level/salary
2. Submit Green Visa application via ICP/GDRFA portal while holding standard visa
3. If approved: Standard visa cancelled; Green Visa issued (no gap in legal status)
4. Notify current employer of status change (if remaining with same employer)

ADVANTAGES OF SWITCHING:
• Independence from employer sponsorship
• Longer validity (5 years vs. 2 years standard)
• Enhanced family sponsorship stability
• Greater flexibility to change jobs, become self-employed, or start business

=== DOCUMENT ATTESTATION REQUIREMENTS ===
FOREIGN DEGREES/DIPLOMAS MUST BE:
1. Attested by issuing country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs
2. Legalized by UAE Embassy/Consulate in that country
3. Further attested by MOFAIC (Ministry of Foreign Affairs & International Cooperation) in UAE upon arrival

TRANSLATION REQUIREMENTS:
• Documents not in Arabic or English must be translated by certified translator
• Translation must be attested alongside original document
• Arabic translation preferred for faster processing

MOFAIC ATTESTATION IN UAE:
• Can be completed online: https://www.mofaic.gov.ae
• Or at MOFAIC customer happiness centers
• Fee: ~AED 150-300 per document depending on type

=== COMPLIANCE & RESPONSIBILITIES ===
GREEN VISA HOLDER MUST:
• Maintain eligibility criteria throughout visa validity (freelance permit, income, employment, salary)
• Renew visa before expiry to maintain legal status
• Notify authorities of address changes, passport renewals, family status changes
• Comply with all UAE laws and regulations
• Ensure sponsored dependents also comply with visa conditions

PENALTIES FOR NON-COMPLIANCE:
• Visa cancellation and deportation for serious violations
• Fines for overstaying or working without proper authorization
• Future entry bans for fraudulent applications or criminal activity
• Sponsor may face consequences for dependent violations

=== STANDARD WORK VISA COMPARISON ===
KEY DIFFERENCES:

| Feature | Green Visa | Standard Work Visa |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|
| Validity | 5 years (renewable) | Typically 2 years (renewable) |
| Sponsor | Self-sponsored | Employer-sponsored |
| Job Change Flexibility | High (no sponsor dependency) | Low (requires new employer sponsorship) |
| Family Sponsorship | Yes (subject to salary/accommodation) | Yes (subject to salary/accommodation) |
| Eligibility | Freelancers, self-employed, skilled employees (specific criteria) | Any employee with valid job offer |
| Application | Individual applies directly | Employer applies on behalf of employee |
| Fee Responsibility | Applicant pays | Typically employer pays |

=== INQUIRY CONTACTS ===
FOR GREEN VISA APPLICATIONS:
• ICP Federal: https://www.icp.gov.ae | Call: 600 522222
• GDRFA Dubai: https://www.gdrfad.gov.ae | Call: 800 5111
• MOHRE (for freelance permits/occupational classification): https://www.mohre.gov.ae | Call: 600 665 555

FOR MEDICAL EXAMINATION:
• MOHAP Approved Centers: https://www.mohap.gov.ae
• SEHA (Abu Dhabi): https://www.seha.ae
• DHA (Dubai): https://www.dha.gov.ae

FOR DOCUMENT ATTESTATION:
• MOFAIC Attestation Services: https://www.mofaic.gov.ae
• Authorized typing centers across UAE emirates (verify authorization via official portals)

FOR WPS/SALARY VERIFICATION:
• Ministry of Human Resources & Emiratisation (MOHRE): https://www.mohre.gov.ae | Call: 600 665 555

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: Family Visa (Partner/Spouse Route)
COUNTRY: United Kingdom
SOURCE: https://www.gov.uk/uk-family-visa
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== OVERVIEW ===
You need a family visa to live with a family member in the UK for more than 6 months.
Eligible relationships: spouse, partner, fiancé(e), child, parent, relative providing long-term care.

=== ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA - PARTNER ROUTE ===
APPLICANT AND PARTNER MUST:
• Both be 18 or over
• Intend to live together permanently in the UK after application
• Partner must be: British/Irish citizen OR settled in UK (ILR, settled status) OR pre-settled status (EU/EEA/Swiss, arrived before 1 Jan 2021) OR have Turkish Businessperson/Worker visa OR refugee/humanitarian protection OR stateless person leave

RELATIONSHIP PROOF (One of the following):
• Marriage or civil partnership recognized in UK
• Living together in relationship for at least 2 years at time of application
• Fiancé(e)/proposed civil partner planning to marry/enter civil partnership in UK within 6 months of arrival
• In relationship 2+ years but cannot live together due to work/study/cultural reasons (provide evidence of ongoing commitment)

=== FINANCIAL REQUIREMENTS ===
MINIMUM INCOME THRESHOLD:
• Standard: £29,000 combined annual income (applicant + partner)
• Transitional protection (applied as partner before 11 April 2024): £18,600 combined income
• Additional for children: £3,800 for first child, £2,400 for each additional child (capped at £29,000 total)

EXEMPTIONS FROM MINIMUM INCOME:
• Partner receives qualifying disability/carer benefits: Disability Living Allowance, PIP, Attendance Allowance, Carer's Allowance, Armed Forces Independence Payment, etc.
• Human rights claim: Child in UK who is British/Irish or lived 7+ years in UK, and unreasonable for child to leave
• Human rights breach: Refusal would breach applicant's human rights

PROOF OF INCOME:
• Employment: Payslips (6 months), employer letter, bank statements
• Self-employment: Tax returns, accounts, dividend vouchers
• Savings: £16,000+ held for 6 months (can supplement income shortfall)
• Pension, benefits, or other specified sources

=== ENGLISH LANGUAGE REQUIREMENT ===
MUST PROVE ENGLISH AT CEFR LEVEL A1 (SPEAKING & LISTENING) VIA:
• Approved SELT test (IELTS Life Skills A1, Trinity GESE Grade 2, etc.)
• Academic qualification taught in English recognized as equivalent to UK degree
• National of majority English-speaking country (USA, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, etc.)

=== DOCUMENTS REQUIRED ===
• Valid passport or travel document
• Proof of relationship: marriage certificate, cohabitation evidence (joint bills, tenancy, bank statements), photos, communication records
• Proof of partner's status: British passport, ILR biometric card, settled status share code
• Financial evidence: payslips, bank statements, employer letter, tax documents
• English language proof: SELT certificate, degree certificate, passport from English-speaking country
• TB test certificate (if applying from listed country)
• Translation of any non-English documents by certified translator

=== FEES & COSTS ===
APPLICATION FEES:
• Outside UK: £1,938 (joining partner/parent/child)
• Inside UK: £1,321 (joining partner/parent/child)
• Dependant added to application: Same as above per person
• Adult dependent relative (care): £3,413 (outside UK) / £1,321 (inside UK) OR £424/£1,321 if sponsor has refugee/humanitarian protection

IMMIGRATION HEALTH SURCHARGE (IHS):
• Adults (18+): £2,587.50 (2.5 years), £3,105 (2.75 years), £5,175 (5 years)
• Children (<18): £1,940 (2.5 years), £2,328 (2.75 years), £3,880 (5 years)

PRIORITY SERVICES (UK applications only):
• Super Priority: +£1,000 for next-day decision (not available for adult dependent relative route)

=== PROCESSING TIMES ===
• Outside UK: Usually 12 weeks
• Inside UK (meeting all requirements): Usually 8 weeks
• Inside UK (not meeting financial/English requirements): ~12 months

=== DURATION & EXTENSION ===
• Initial grant: 2 years 9 months (partner route) or 6 months (fiancé(e) route)
• Extension: Apply before current visa expires; grants additional 2 years 6 months
• Can extend multiple times

=== SETTLEMENT (INDEFINITE LEAVE TO REMAIN) ===
• Earliest eligibility: After 5 years continuous residence in UK on family visa as partner
• Time spent as fiancé(e) does NOT count toward 5 years
• Time spent on other visas does NOT count
• Must still meet financial and English requirements at settlement stage (unless exempt)

=== SWITCHING RULES ===
CAN SWITCH TO FAMILY VISA FROM INSIDE UK IF:
• Currently on: Most work visas, student visas, graduate visa, certain humanitarian protections
• Applying to stay with same eligible partner/family member

CANNOT SWITCH FROM INSIDE UK IF:
• Currently on: Standard Visitor visa, visa for 6 months or less
• Family member is in UK temporarily on work/student visa (apply as dependant instead)

=== EXCLUSION CRITERIA (WHEN YOU CANNOT APPLY) ===
✗ Family member has only temporary work/student visa (apply as dependant instead)
✗ Applicant has visitor visa or permission for ≤6 months (usually must leave UK to apply)
✗ Relationship is not genuine/subsisting or entered for immigration purposes
✗ General grounds for refusal apply (criminal history, deception, unpaid NHS debts, etc.)

=== SPECIAL CIRCUMSTANCES ===
DOMESTIC ABUSE OR PARTNER DEATH:
• May apply to settle in UK if permission was as partner and:
  - Applicant was victim of domestic abuse, OR
  - Partner died during relationship
• Must provide evidence (police reports, court orders, death certificate, support letters)

PRIVATE LIFE ROUTE (ALTERNATIVE PATH):
• Under 18 + lived in UK continuously 7+ years + unreasonable to leave
• Age 18-24 + lived in UK > half your life
• 18+ + <20 years in UK + very significant problems returning to home country
• 20+ years continuous residence in UK
• Grants 2.5 years initially; settlement after 5 or 10 years depending on category

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: Skilled Worker Visa (Work)
COUNTRY: United Kingdom
SOURCE: https://www.gov.uk/skilled-worker-visa/eligibility
LAST UPDATED: 2024

=== ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA ===
Your job:
• Work for a UK employer that's been approved by the Home Office
• Have a 'certificate of sponsorship' (CoS) from your employer with information about the role you've been offered in the UK
• Do a job that's on the list of eligible occupations
• Be paid a minimum salary - how much depends on the type of work you do and the date you got your CoS
• Must have a confirmed job offer before you apply for your visa

Knowledge of English:
• Must be able to speak, read, write and understand English
• Usually need to prove knowledge of English when you apply

=== POINTS-BASED SYSTEM (Must score 70 points total) ===
• 50 points: Mandatory criteria (job offer from approved sponsor, job at appropriate skill level, English at B1)
• 20 points: Salary criteria (meet general threshold OR going rate for occupation OR new entrant rate)

=== SALARY THRESHOLDS (2024) ===
• General threshold: £38,700 per year OR the "going rate" for your occupation code (whichever is higher)
• Health/education workers: £30,960 per year OR going rate
• New entrants (under 26, recent graduate, switching from Student visa): 70% of going rate OR £30,960 (whichever is higher)

=== ENGLISH LANGUAGE PROOF OPTIONS ===
• Pass an approved SELT test at CEFR level B1 (reading, writing, speaking, listening)
• Have an academic qualification taught in English equivalent to UK bachelor's/master's/PhD
• Be a national of a majority English-speaking country (USA, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, etc.)

=== MAINTENANCE FUNDS ===
• Must have £1,270 in bank account for 28 consecutive days before applying
• OR sponsor certifies maintenance on CoS (exempts this requirement)

=== DOCUMENTS REQUIRED ===
• Certificate of sponsorship reference number (from employer)
• Proof of English knowledge
• Valid passport or identity document
• Job title and annual salary
• Job's occupation code
• Employer name and sponsor licence number (on CoS)
• TB test certificate (if from listed country)
• Criminal record certificate (for education/healthcare roles)

=== FEES & PROCESSING ===
• Application fee: £719–£1,420 (depends on duration and shortage occupation)
• Immigration Health Surcharge: £1,035 per year
• Standard processing: 3 weeks (outside UK), 8 weeks (inside UK)

=== SWITCHING RULES ===
• Can apply from inside UK if on: Student visa, Graduate visa, certain work visas
• Must apply from outside UK if on: Visitor visa, short-term study visa

content.full_text:
## United Kingdom - Student Visa
### Source: United_Kingdom_Student.txt

REQUIREMENTS:
- Unconditional offer from licensed student sponsor
- Prove English language proficiency
- Show sufficient funds for course fees and living costs
- Be from country requiring visa

REQUIRED DOCUMENTS:
- Valid passport
- Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies (CAS)
- Proof of financial support (£1,334/month for London)
- English language test results
- TB test certificate (if applicable)

PROCESSING TIME: 3-8 weeks
FEES: £490

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: Family-Based Immigration (USA)
CATEGORIES: Immediate Relatives + Family Preference

=== IMMEDIATE RELATIVES (No Annual Cap) ===
ELIGIBLE RELATIONSHIPS:
- Spouse of a U.S. citizen
- Unmarried child under 21 of a U.S. citizen
- Parent of a U.S. citizen (if citizen is 21 or older)
- Orphan adopted abroad or to be adopted in the U.S. by a U.S. citizen

ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA - PETITIONER (U.S. CITIZEN SPONSOR):
- Must be a U.S. citizen (not just a permanent resident)
- Must be at least 18 years old
- Must have a domicile (residence) in the United States or intend to re-establish one
- Must demonstrate ability to financially support the beneficiary (Form I-864, Affidavit of Support)
- Must provide proof of relationship (marriage certificate, birth certificate, adoption decree)

ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA - BENEFICIARY (APPLICANT):
- Must prove bona fide relationship (not entered for immigration purposes only)
- Must be admissible to the United States (no criminal inadmissibility, health grounds, etc.)
- Must undergo medical examination by authorized physician
- Must attend visa interview at U.S. Embassy/Consulate (if applying from abroad)

=== FAMILY PREFERENCE CATEGORIES (Subject to Annual Caps) ===
F1: Unmarried sons and daughters (21+) of U.S. citizens
F2A: Spouses and unmarried children (under 21) of Lawful Permanent Residents
F2B: Unmarried sons and daughters (21+) of Lawful Permanent Residents
F3: Married sons and daughters of U.S. citizens
F4: Brothers and sisters of U.S. citizens (if citizen is 21+)

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS FOR ALL FAMILY VISAS:
- Petitioner must file Form I-130, Petition for Alien Relative
- Beneficiary must wait for priority date to become current (for preference categories)
- Must not have violated prior U.S. immigration laws (unlawful presence, fraud, etc.)
- May require waiver of inadmissibility if grounds exist (Form I-601)

DOCUMENTS TYPICALLY REQUIRED:
- Proof of petitioner's U.S. citizenship (passport, birth certificate, naturalization certificate)
- Proof of qualifying relationship (marriage certificate, birth certificate, photos, correspondence)
- Affidavit of Support (Form I-864) + financial evidence (tax returns, employment letter, bank statements)
- Beneficiary's passport, birth certificate, police certificates, medical exam results
- DS-260 immigrant visa application confirmation

content.full_text:
VISA TYPE: H-1B Specialty Occupation (Work Visa)
COUNTRY: USA

ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA - EMPLOYER (PETITIONER):
- Must offer a job that qualifies as a "specialty occupation" (requires theoretical/practical application of highly specialized knowledge).
- Must demonstrate a valid employer-employee relationship with the beneficiary.
- Must file a Labor Condition Application (LCA) with the Department of Labor and certify compliance with wage requirements.
- Must agree to pay the higher of the actual wage or the prevailing wage for the occupation in the area of employment.
- Must pay required filing fees (including ACWIA fee, Fraud Prevention fee if applicable).

ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA - APPLICANT (BENEFICIARY):
- Must possess at least a bachelor's degree or its equivalent in a field directly related to the specialty occupation.
- If degree is from outside the US, it must be evaluated as equivalent to a US degree.
- If lacking a degree, must demonstrate equivalent expertise through progressive work experience (typically 3 years of experience = 1 year of college).
- Must be coming to the US to work in the specialty occupation for which they are qualified.
- Must maintain valid immigration status if already in the US.

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:
- Position must require a degree as a minimum entry requirement (not preferred, but required).
- The nature of the duties must be so specialized and complex that the knowledge required to perform them is usually associated with a bachelor's degree or higher.
- Most H-1B petitions are subject to an annual cap (65,000 regular + 20,000 for US advanced degree holders), unless exempt (e.g., higher education, nonprofit research).

DOCUMENTS TYPICALLY REQUIRED:
- Copy of beneficiary's degree(s) and transcripts
- Credential evaluation for foreign degrees
- Detailed job description showing specialty occupation requirements
- Approved Labor Condition Application (LCA)
- Employer support letter explaining the role and qualifications
- Proof of employer's ability to pay the offered wage
